PLANT OPERATING MANUAL
Plant No. 140,170 Plant Name: POWER PLANT-2
Chapter No: 1 Rev. No. 2 Rev. Date : 10-07-2021
ADMINISTRATIVE REQUIREMENTS
Visakh Refinery

breadcrumb: Actual Photos of various components of manway cover of Steam Drum of Old Co Boiler > OCOB March-24 SD Photos > SECTION - A
text: SECTION - A

breadcrumb: Actual Photos of various components of manway cover of Steam Drum of Old Co Boiler > OCOB March-24 SD Photos > SECTION - A
text: PREFACE The principal objective of the operating manual is to describe relevant operating procedures, instructions and process safety information in an orderly manner for use by operating personnel for safe and efficient operation of the plant. These operating procedures and instructions are up-to-date reflecting changes in plant hardware and operating practices carried out from time to time. Power Plant-2 (PP-2) unit consists FCCU-2 CO Boiler, FCCU-1 CO Boiler, WHRS (Waste Heat Recovery Steam Generator. VREP-I Pressure Reducing and De-superheating (PRDS), VREP-II PRDS, VREP-I Bearing cooling water (BCW) System and VREP-II BCW System. FCCU-2 CO Boiler, VREP-I PRDS and VREP-I BCW system were commissioned in the year 1985 under the Visakh Refinery Expansion Project-I (VREP-I) and FCCU-I CO Boiler and VREP-II PRDS and VREP-II BCW System were commissioned in the year 1999 under Visakh Refinery Expansion Project-II (VREP-II). WHRS commissioned in year 2018. The present edition of the operating manual of Power Plant-2 is the revised version of earlier operating manual prepared on 03-11-2015. The operating procedures are prepared / updated basis the inputs available in the process Safety Information, Plant Standing Instructions (PSI) issued and the past operating experiences. Primary purpose of this revised operating manual is to consolidate, improve and upgrade all the operating procedures fulfilling the requirements of Process Safety Management (PSM) standard. HPCL Visakh Refinery adopted OSHA (Occupational Safety & Health Administration) PSM Standard 29-CFR-1910.119 (PSM for highly hazardous chemicals) in 2013 and released the “PSM Procedure

breadcrumb: Actual Photos of various components of manway cover of Steam Drum of Old Co Boiler > OCOB March-24 SD Photos > SECTION - A
text: and upgrade all the operating procedures fulfilling the requirements of Process Safety Management (PSM) standard. HPCL Visakh Refinery adopted OSHA (Occupational Safety & Health Administration) PSM Standard 29-CFR-1910.119 (PSM for highly hazardous chemicals) in 2013 and released the “PSM Procedure for preparation of Operating Procedures” (PSM-PR- 4) in the refinery. The present version of the Power Plant-2 Operating Manual is prepared as per PSM-PR-4 and PSM audit observations in order to make the Operating Manual OSHA PSM Standard compliant. Operating procedures & conditions given in this manual are indicative and general guide for routine start-up and operation of the unit. Vendor’s manual can be referred for detailed operating instructions and precautions. Efforts have been made to include the relevant information in a concise, step-by-step, easy-to-read format so that they are within the comprehension of the readers. The users of this manual are encouraged to read the operating manual and provide suggestions for further improvements of the manual.

INSTRUCTIONS
The Operating Procedures and Practices w.r.to unit start up, normal operation, non-routine
operations, normal shutdown and emergency shutdown are periodically reviewed for
improvements based on experiences, new requirements and better understanding of the process.
Accordingly, operating manuals are validated every year and revised once in 5 years.
The yearly validation and revision details of the manual are as below:
 The present edition of unit operating manual is reviewed and validated for the current
operation of the plant as indicated in the Table-1 below.
 A record of Plant Standing Instructions (PSI) issued in the intervening period is listed in

breadcrumb: Actual Photos of various components of manway cover of Steam Drum of Old Co Boiler > ANNEXURE s > RECORDS OF REVISION, VALIDATION AND PLANT STANDING > Table-2.
text:
Table-2.
 The PSIs issued since last revision till the date of next revision (after 5 years) of the
operating manual is reviewed for its applicability in the current operation during yearly
validation and the same is indicated during validation. During revision of the manual after
5 years, all the applicable PSIs are properly integrated in the revised manual.


The administrative sections (Sections A to F of Chapter-1 of the Operating Manual ) are
approved by Division Head- Operations.

The original operating manual in a box file with tab is maintained by respective Division
Head - Operations.
 Hard Bound copies with stamping & numbering, “Controlled Copy No. …. ” are made
and issued as below for usage. Controlled Copy means that Div. Head will monitor it
for its status, requirement of revision, and accessibility.
Sl. Identification No. Issued to Issued on (date)
Original Kept with Div. Head - Operations
(Utilities, CPP)
1 Controlled Copy No. 1 Plant Field Room
2 Controlled Copy No. 2 Plant DCS Panel
3 Controlled Copy No. 3 Section Head – Operations
(Utilities)
4 Controlled Copy No. 4 Office of HOD - Operations
 Uncontrolled Controlled hard copies with stamping, “ Reference copy ” are made and
issued as below:
a) Plant Personnel: Individual copies or by issuing on returnable basis from a set
of Manuals (like Library) maintained by Section Head.
b) Office of RSM / YSF
c) Disaster Control Room (formerly “Central Control Centre”)
d) Refinery Engineering Documentation
e) Technical Department (PAD / PS&E)
 In case of any doubt regarding the latest revision, the Original Copy is the reference
document for confirmation.

After the revised version is made and new “Controlled Copies” and “Reference Copies”
are issued, the obsolete copies are destroyed.

PROCEDURE FOR REVISION OF THE OPERATING MANUAL
1. This Operating Manual is revised for the following :
 Change in Operating practice in any part of the Plant
 Implementation of changes in Hardware and/or software systems of the Plant which
have impact on operating procedure.
 Change in Chemicals.
 Changes in Safety systems.

breadcrumb: Actual Photos of various components of manway cover of Steam Drum of Old Co Boiler > ANNEXURE s > SECTION - E PROCEDURE FOR REVISION OF THE OPERATING MANUAL
text:
2. The revision of the Operating Manual is done in two stages :
 Managing changes in the Operating Manual within 5 year cycle by validating the
manual annually.
 Updating (revising) the manual after 5 years of earlier revision.

breadcrumb: Actual Photos of various components of manway cover of Steam Drum of Old Co Boiler > ANNEXURE s > SECTION - E PROCEDURE FOR REVISION OF THE OPERATING MANUAL
text:
3. Changes in any operating procedure are issued as “Plant Standing Instructions,” which
is maintained in a separate file by respective Division Head - Operations. Copy of the
valid Standing Instructions is made available in plant field room also for reference. A
record of applicable Plant Standing Instructions is maintained in Section-C of Chapter

PLANT OPERATING MANUAL
Plant No. 140,170 Plant Name: POWER PLANT-2
Chapter No: 2 Rev. No. 2 Rev. Date : 10-07-2021
INTRODUCTION
Visakh Refinery

breadcrumb: Actual Photos of various components of manway cover of Steam Drum of Old Co Boiler > 1 in the Operating Manual controlled copies. > CHAPTER - 2
text: CHAPTER - 2

breadcrumb: Actual Photos of various components of manway cover of Steam Drum of Old Co Boiler > 1 in the Operating Manual controlled copies. > CHAPTER - 2
text: INTRODUCTION The steam is required in the refinery for the following Purposes: i. For Heating purpose ii. For Driving Turbines which drives Pumps & Blowers iii. For atomising fuel oil in FO Burner iv. For producing vacuum in the column. v. For cleaning purpose. VHP, HP and MP steams are required for driving turbines. MP/LP steam is required for heating and steaming. MP steam is used for atomisation and used as motive fluid for vacuum ejectors. Bearing Cooling water is required for cooling the bearings of pumps and turbines in units. The Power Plant-2 mainly consists of the following Equipment i. Boilers (FCCU-2 CO, WHRS & FCCU-1 CO Boilers). ii. VREP-I & II Pressure Reducing and De-super heating systems (PRDS). iii. VREP-I & II Bearing Cooling Water (BCW) Systems. FCCU-2 CO Boiler and FCCU-1 CO Boilers generates VHP Steam. FCCU-2 CO Boiler was designed, supplied, erected by Heat Research Corporation Ltd, USA. FCCU-1 CO Boiler was designed, supplied and erected by (Bharat Heavy Plates and Vessels Limited (BHPV) (Presently BHEL), Visakhapatnam. WHRS (Waste Heat Recovery Steam Generator) is provided between FCCU-II CO Boiler and FGD-II to enable recovery of heat from hot flue gases at FCCU-II CO Boiler outlet to FGD-II. It generates LP Steam. Hot flue gases from FCCU-II routed to FCCU-II CO Boiler through orifice chamber for steam generation and the hot flue gases, downstream of the Boiler at 320 deg C, routed to FGD-II for SPM and

breadcrumb: Actual Photos of various components of manway cover of Steam Drum of Old Co Boiler > 1 in the Operating Manual controlled copies. > CHAPTER - 2
text: FGD-II. It generates LP Steam. Hot flue gases from FCCU-II routed to FCCU-II CO Boiler through orifice chamber for steam generation and the hot flue gases, downstream of the Boiler at 320 deg C, routed to FGD-II for SPM and SOx removal. Hence, it was proposed to provide WHRS to generate steam by recovering heat from flue gases. In this process, flue gas temperature at the inlet of FGD-II will reduce from 320 deg C to 200 deg C. VREP-I&VREP-II PRDS Coverts VHP steam into MP Steam and MP steam into LP steam as per process demand. VREP-I & II PRDS Systems designed by EIL and erected by HPCL Projects Dept. VREP-I BCW system supplies BCW to all VREP-I units and VREP-II BCW supplies BCW to VREP-II units. VREP-I &II BCW Systems Designed by EIL and erected by HPCL Projects Dept. text: 3.0 Process Design Basis:

breadcrumb: Actual Photos of various components of manway cover of Steam Drum of Old Co Boiler > 3.0 Process Design Basis:
text:
FCCU-2 CO Boiler is a VHP steam Boiler designed for VREP-I steam requirement.
It is designed to burn the FCCU-2 CO gas also. It is a 60 T/hr, 35.5 kg/cm2 and 370
deg C. it is commissioned in 1984.
After the revamp of FCCU-2 unit in 2010, the lean CO gas fuel from FCCU-2 unit
has reduced the CO Boiler steam generating capacity from 60 t/hr to 40 T/hr along
with supplementary fuel gas firing.
FCCU-1 CO Boiler is a VHP steam Boiler designed for VREP-2 steam requirement.
It is designed to burn the FCCU-1 CO gas also. It is a 40 T/hr, 35.5 kg/cm2 and 370
deg C. it is commissioned in 1999.
WHRS Steam drum outlet pressure maintained at 12.5 Kg/cm2 (g) and Steam drum
outlet steam temperature: 185 Deg. C and LP steam generating capacity id 6.1 TPH
VREP-I & II PRDS Systems reduces pressure from 35.5 to 10 kg/cm2 and 10 to
3.5kg/cm2 and de-superheat the steam by injection of BFW Water at respective
pressures.
VREP-I BCW Systems Circulating Capacity is 250 m3/hr at 7.5 kg/cm2, It is
Induced draft cross flow type, operating Inlet temperature is 44 deg C and Outlet
Temperature is 34 deg C.
VREP-II BCW Systems Circulating Capacity is 400 m3/hr at 7.5 kg/cm2,, It is
Induced draft cross flow type, operating Inlet temperature is 44 deg C and Outlet
Temperature is 33 deg C.

breadcrumb: Actual Photos of various components of manway cover of Steam Drum of Old Co Boiler > 5 TDS <800 > 5.1 Process Principle of FCCU-II & FCCU-I CO Boilers:
text:
FCCU-II & FCCU-I CO Boilers are dual fired (Oil & Gas) Boilers. The unique feature
of CO Boilers is that CO Gas Coming from FCCU Process will be routed stack through
CO Boilers where Heat in CO gas utilized for generating steam. The saturation
temperature table is given below for having the idea of at what temperature dry &
saturated will be formed in Boiler at particular pressure.
5.2 Process Principle of WHRS

text: FLUE GAS TO FGD

breadcrumb: Actual Photos of various components of manway cover of Steam Drum of Old Co Boiler > 5 TDS <800 > 5.1 Process Principle of FCCU-II & FCCU-I CO Boilers:
text:
Hot flue gases from FCCU-II routed to OCO boiler through orifice chamber for steam
generation and the hot flue gases, downstream of the boiler at 320 deg C, are routed to
FGD-II for SPM and SOx removal.
It was observed that 6.0 TPH of LP steam can be generated by recovering the heat from
hot flue gases. Hence, it was proposed to provide Waste Heat Recovery System to
generate steam by recovering heat from flue gases. In this process, flue gas temperature
at the inlet of FGD-II will reduce from 320 deg C to 200 deg C.

is added to steam and rejected from steam, the enthalpy in steam remains constant. But

temperature at that pressure, to reduce the temp of steam after pressure reduction water
is injected to LP steam end through de-super heater.

text:
The principle of operation of BCW system is transfer of heat from hot media (Hot BCW
return) to cold media (Air sucked from bottom). BCW System Supplies chemical treated
water to the units for cooling of pumps bearings, after passing through Bearings BCW
water gets heated and return to BCW system. Hot return water is cooled in tower by
using induced draft fans located on top of cooling tower. Cooled BCW again supplied
to Units.

text: 6.1. Brief Process Description of Boilers: FCCU-2 CO Boiler is a gas and oil fired boiler erected in the year 1985 in VREP-1 project. Initially it is designed for 60 t/hr. But after the revamp of FCCU-2 unit in 2010 the lean CO gas fuel from FCCU-2 unit has reduced the CO Boiler steam generating capacity from 60 t/hr to 40 T/hr along with supplementary fuel gas firing. As it is VHP steam generation the pressure and temperature of steam are 36 kg/cm 2 and 360 Deg C. Fuel gas is the auxiliary fuel fire provided in this boiler. FCCU-1 CO Boiler is a CO gas fired boiler erected in the year 1999 in VREP-2 project. It is designed for 40 T/hr, 36 kg/cm2 and 360 Deg C. Fuel oil/ Fuel gas are the auxiliary fuels fired in this boiler. 6.2. Brief Process Description of WHRS: WHRS is designed for dry run conditions. There is no bypass facility for the WHRS. FCCU-II CO Boiler flue gas outlet routed to WHRS instead of FGD-II. WHRS flue gas outlet routed to FGD-II for treatment. Sulphur dew point was calculated as 175 deg C for the design composition of the flue gas. Therefore, in order to avoid acid dew point corrosion, the evaporator coil operating temperature has to be at least 10 deg C above Sulphur dew point. Accordingly, it was envisaged that the boiler is to be operated at 10.5 kg/cm2g and

breadcrumb: Actual Photos of various components of manway cover of Steam Drum of Old Co Boiler > 5 TDS <800 > PROCESS DESCRIPTION AND CONFIGURATION
text: in order to avoid acid dew point corrosion, the evaporator coil operating temperature has to be at least 10 deg C above Sulphur dew point. Accordingly, it was envisaged that the boiler is to be operated at 10.5 kg/cm2g and saturation temperature of 185 deg C. The same is to be let down to 3.0/3.5 kg/cm2g using a Pressure Control Valve.LP steam generated from the system is routed to LP steam header at the FCCU-II CO Boiler FD fan turbine discharge. VHP BFW for the new system is taken from OCO boiler VHP BFW provision at the upstream of BFW control valve. All other utilities requirement for the new system are taken from FCCU-II battery limit. 6.3. Brief Process Description of PRDS: There are Two Types of PRDS in PP-2 they are 1) VREP-I PRDS 2) VREP-II PRDS. VREP-I PRDS (2Nos) Reduces VHP Steam (35.5 Kg/cm2) to MP Steam (10.5 kg/cm2) and VLP Steam (3.5 kg/cm2) VREP-II PRDS (2Nos) Reduces VHP Steam (35.5Kg/cm2) to MP Steam (10.5Kg/cm2) and MP Steam (10.5Kg/cm2) to LP Steam (3.5Kg/cm2). 6.4. Brief Process Description of BCW System: This BCW system consists of two unit’s viz., BCW VREP-1 and BCW VREP-2. These units supply Bearing Cooling Water at desired pressure and temperature to VREP-1 and VREP-2 units. BCW VREP-1 system which is commissioned in the year 1985 supplies BCW to all VREP-1 units and some off-site location. BCW VREP-2 which is commissioned in the year 1999

breadcrumb: Actual Photos of various components of manway cover of Steam Drum of Old Co Boiler > 5 TDS <800 > PROCESS DESCRIPTION AND CONFIGURATION
text: Water at desired pressure and temperature to VREP-1 and VREP-2 units. BCW VREP-1 system which is commissioned in the year 1985 supplies BCW to all VREP-1 units and some off-site location. BCW VREP-2 which is commissioned in the year 1999 supplies BCW to VREP-2 units, Sea water Cooling tower pumps and few off site pumps. This cooling water is used for bearing housing cooling, pump gland cooling, turbine oil coolers and air compressor coolers etc., where salt water cannot be used. The cooling water supply at desired pressure, temperature and quality is very much required for efficient heat exchange. Failure of which cause damage to bearings and other rotating parts, tripping of unit and sometimes cease of shaft. VREP-1 BCW system: This system consists of 1) Cooling water tower with two cells 2) Sump 3) Recirculation pumps 4) Chemical dosing facility 5) Supply and Return header. 6) Make up water facility

breadcrumb: Actual Photos of various components of manway cover of Steam Drum of Old Co Boiler > 5 HVGO PUMPS > FCCU-2 CO BOILER CONTROL LOOPS
text: The CO Boiler is designed for combusting the FCCU - II regenerator off-gas containing lean concentrations of carbon monoxide in the presence of auxiliary fuel oil and/or fuel gas. The heat from the combustion is used to heat feed water and generate superheated steam. The CO bearing flue gas is burned in the presence of auxiliary fuel to provide a maximum continuous rated production of 40 t/hr of steam (after FCCU-II revamp) at a gauge pressure of 38 kg/cm2 and a temperature of 370C. The boiler consists of two major components. The first is the hot refractory line combustor, in which complete incineration of carbon monoxide takes place, supported by firing of auxiliary chamber at several points in the arch above the supplementary burners. The second component is the horizontal tube convection section in which heat recovery takes place. The hot refractory line combustor is designed for temperatures in the range of 1000 0 C or higher, to ensure complete conversion of the carbon monoxide in the Regenerator off gas and to maintain stable ignition during the transient effects accompanying load changes or varying rates of coke burn. The firebox size ensures sufficient combustion residence time; so that the entire CO is completely converted to CO2 before any significant temperature reduction is permitted to take place as a result of heat transfer to cold absorbing surfaces. In effect, the ideal adiabatic conversion is approached. The steam generation section is based

breadcrumb: Actual Photos of various components of manway cover of Steam Drum of Old Co Boiler > 5 HVGO PUMPS > FCCU-2 CO BOILER CONTROL LOOPS
text: CO is completely converted to CO2 before any significant temperature reduction is permitted to take place as a result of heat transfer to cold absorbing surfaces. In effect, the ideal adiabatic conversion is approached. The steam generation section is based on a natural circulation system utilizing a conservative circulation ratio which provides inlet velocities to the steam generation coil in excess of 2 meters per second. Tabulators are provided in bottom two rows of the lower steam generator coil. These swirlers increase the turbulence and mixing action of the water steam mixture thereby improving the fluid flow and heat transfer characteristics of the tubes exposed to the highest flue gas temperature in addition to the inlet manifolds are sized and individual risers are provided to assure uniform flow to each pass. The tube coils are designed using 114 mm OD tubes with 5 mm wall thickness which is well in

breadcrumb: Actual Photos of various components of manway cover of Steam Drum of Old Co Boiler > 5 HVGO PUMPS > FCCU-2 CO BOILER CONTROL LOOPS
text: Power Plant-2 Operating Manual Chapter No:6 Rev. No.2 Page 33 of 251

breadcrumb: Actual Photos of various components of manway cover of Steam Drum of Old Co Boiler > 5 HVGO PUMPS > FCCU-2 CO BOILER CONTROL LOOPS
text:
excess of actual code requirements, representing a substantial safety factor. The use of
this tube size and thickness presents three advantages. First, large tubes minimize any
problems resulting from high concentration of solids in the boiler water. Second, extra
wall thickness gives added protection should any problem of external erosion develop.
Third possibility of tube vibration is eliminated, which is sometimes a problem with tubes
of smaller diameter. Any tube in the entire boiler system can be removed and replaced
individually without the need for removing or even forcing aside any other tube. Header
boxes are provided at both ends of the convection section.
The unit is designed for high flue gas velocities which help maintain “clean” convection
surface. These velocities maintain a check on solids build up on heat transfer surface but
without creating problems of tube erosion. Three levels of two retractable soot blowers
complete with a scavenging and sealing air system are provided.
The side wall and floors of the combustor are of double wall construction with a forced
flow of combustion air. This design eliminates combustor heat losses. Combustion air is
provided by the turbine and motor driven forced draft fan. Each fan is provided with an
inlet air silencer and connecting duct with isolation dampers.

breadcrumb: Actual Photos of various components of manway cover of Steam Drum of Old Co Boiler > 5 HVGO PUMPS > FCCU-2 CO BOILER CONTROL LOOPS > 6.4.2 FCCU-I CO Boiler:
text:
The CO-furnace is a cylindrical vessel of 4m ID (Net) x 9.5m length (TL-TL) attached
horizontally to the CO-Boiler, such that the combustion products directly enter the boiler.
The furnace is internally lined with two-layers of refractories viz. Rescocast AA 22S
925 mm THK), with SS 310 Hexmesh and insulating castable type-V of EIL spec. 6-79-

breadcrumb: Actual Photos of various components of manway cover of Steam Drum of Old Co Boiler > 5 HVGO PUMPS > FCCU-2 CO BOILER CONTROL LOOPS > 6.4.2 FCCU-I CO Boiler:
text: Power Plant-2 Operating Manual Chapter No:6 Rev. No.2 Page 34 of 251

breadcrumb: Actual Photos of various components of manway cover of Steam Drum of Old Co Boiler > 5 HVGO PUMPS > FCCU-2 CO BOILER CONTROL LOOPS > 6.4.2 FCCU-I CO Boiler:
text: 08 temperature. Outer surface of furnace shell is provided with no insulation but with protective hood on its top. The protective hood is provided with chimneys (with cover) to maintain proper air circulation and also it acts as a rain hood. The furnace is equipped with three numbers of CO-burners and one number of support burner. Co-burners are provided on the circular shell of the CO-furnace, firing tangentially to make a whirlpool, thereby increasing the CO-gas. Support burner is also suitable for firing of LSHS & fuel oil and provided with a pilot burner firing fuel gas continuously at all cases of operation of the boiler. The furnace supports are heated by saturated water from steam drum to minimize thermal stresses in the system. The maximum temperature of the furnace is maintained around 1000 0 C, by controlling the support burner firing duty and combustion air flow to CO-furnace. The CO-Boiler is a vertical, water tube boiler with membrane wall type construction. It has a rectangular cross section and operates on natural circulation principle. The membrane wall evaporative shell houses the superheater and evaporator (Fahnen) tube bundles. Economizer tubes are provided above the Fahnen tubes in metallic shell casing. Flue gas from the boiler at 2450C (max) is further cooled down to 1800` 1850C in a cast iron gilled ring economizer, located downstream of the CO-Boiler. Flue gasses from the gilled ring economizer exit to atmosphere at 60m elevation through

breadcrumb: Actual Photos of various components of manway cover of Steam Drum of Old Co Boiler > 5 HVGO PUMPS > FCCU-2 CO BOILER CONTROL LOOPS > 6.4.2 FCCU-I CO Boiler:
text: gas from the boiler at 2450C (max) is further cooled down to 1800` 1850C in a cast iron gilled ring economizer, located downstream of the CO-Boiler. Flue gasses from the gilled ring economizer exit to atmosphere at 60m elevation through the grade mounted stack. The boiler stack is of metal plate (CS) construction and internally lined with 19mm of low cement castable (50% Al2, O3 min, 0.8% Fe2, O3 max, 2% CaO max) on hot face with SS304H hexmesh followed by 65 mm thick insulating castable (type-II of EIL spec. 6-79-08). No external insulation is provided on stack. However, stack metallic shell is provided with 3 coats of heat resisting chimney black paint internally. The Boiler membrane walls are provided with 105 mm thick mineral wool insulation with Alcladding (for protection against weather) externally to minimize the heat loss to atmosphere. The economizer section is provided with 155 mm thick mineral wool insulation externally and the downstream flue gas ducting up to the exit of gilled ring economizer is provided with 105 mm thick mineral wool insulation externally. Flue gas ducting d/s of Gilled ring economizer up to the stack is to be applied with 40 mm thick mineral wool insulation for personnel protection, complete flue gas ducting from CO- Boiler to stack to be applied with 19 mm thick Rescocast AA 22 (s) lining internally with SS304H Hexmesh to prevent erosion. The boiler is provided with a combustion chamber

breadcrumb: Actual Photos of various components of manway cover of Steam Drum of Old Co Boiler > 5 HVGO PUMPS > FCCU-2 CO BOILER CONTROL LOOPS > 6.4.2 FCCU-I CO Boiler:
text: insulation for personnel protection, complete flue gas ducting from CO- Boiler to stack to be applied with 19 mm thick Rescocast AA 22 (s) lining internally with SS304H Hexmesh to prevent erosion. The boiler is provided with a combustion chamber for auxiliary fuel firing, which is made by water-wall tubes. All the water walls of auxiliary combustion chamber are provided with separate Risers and Distributors for water circulation and all the water circuit operate on natural circulation principle. The bottom wall of auxiliary firing chamber is lined with a layer of refractory bricks (type-1 of EIL specn. 6-79-03). Three number of top down shot fired, low Nox, combination fuel fired burners of total capacity of 31.5 x 100 kcal/hr to fire fuel gas/LSHS/Fuel oil are installed on top wall of the auxiliary firing combustion chamber. Each auxiliary burner is provided with a fuel gas fired pilot burner for lighting the main burners. The combustion products enter the radiation chamber of boiler at bottom. The super heater tube bundles are located just on top of the radiation chamber and followed by FAHNEN I and then by FAHNEN II (1st and 2nd evaporative tube bundles) respectively. FAHNEN I consists of 16 numbers of tube rows i.e. 4 numbers of tubes branching out from each water wall tube of wall No.3 and again joining back to the respective mother wall tubes. FAHNEN II is identical to that of FAHNEN I, but branches out

breadcrumb: Actual Photos of various components of manway cover of Steam Drum of Old Co Boiler > 5 HVGO PUMPS > FCCU-2 CO BOILER CONTROL LOOPS > 6.4.2 FCCU-I CO Boiler:
text: of tube rows i.e. 4 numbers of tubes branching out from each water wall tube of wall No.3 and again joining back to the respective mother wall tubes. FAHNEN II is identical to that of FAHNEN I, but branches out from the opposite water wall (i.e. Wall No. 4) instead. Membrane wall terminates at the boiler top headers which are connected to the steam drum through riser pipes. Economizer tube bundle is housed in a metallic casing above the top Boiler headers.

breadcrumb: Actual Photos of various components of manway cover of Steam Drum of Old Co Boiler > 5 HVGO PUMPS > FCCU-2 CO BOILER CONTROL LOOPS > 6.4.2 FCCU-I CO Boiler:
text: Power Plant-2 Operating Manual Chapter No:6 Rev. No.2 Page 35 of 251

breadcrumb: Actual Photos of various components of manway cover of Steam Drum of Old Co Boiler > 5 HVGO PUMPS > FCCU-2 CO BOILER CONTROL LOOPS > 6.4.2 FCCU-I CO Boiler:
text: Gases coming out from the economizer, passes through the gilled ring economizer and finally vented to atmosphere at 60m elevation routing through downstream ducting and grade mounted stack. The boiler system is provided with one number of steam drum of size 1500 mm I/D and 7000 mm TL to TL length at a C/L elevation of 24.9 m (i.e. Just above the economizer elevation), operating at a pressure of 43 kg/cm2 (g). Externally mounted boiler feed water preheater is provided to preheat BFW to 1650C (coming out from gilled ring economizer) before entering into economizer to avoid acid dew point corrosion in the cold end of economizer tube bundle. Saturated steam condensate returns to the steam drum by gravity. The acid dew point of flue gas in CO-mode and full fuel oil firing mode BFW preheating can be limited to 1400C, since the acid dew point of flue gas is 1340C (max). The boiler is provided with 12 numbers of retractable type soot blowers at different elevation with 2 nos. of soot blowers at each plane. Additionally, two nos retractable type soot blowers are provided in gilled ring economizer. The soot blowers are motor driven and provided with automatic relay logic sequential control system. Soot blowing medium is superheated MP steam. The boiler system is provided with one set of forced draft fan system common to both auxiliary fuel firing & CO-combustion. Normally working FD fan is motor driven, whereas

breadcrumb: Actual Photos of various components of manway cover of Steam Drum of Old Co Boiler > 5 HVGO PUMPS > FCCU-2 CO BOILER CONTROL LOOPS > 6.4.2 FCCU-I CO Boiler:
text: logic sequential control system. Soot blowing medium is superheated MP steam. The boiler system is provided with one set of forced draft fan system common to both auxiliary fuel firing & CO-combustion. Normally working FD fan is motor driven, whereas the standby one is steam turbine driven. FD fan system also provides sealing air for all soot blowers, cooling air for idle burners and cooling air for flame scanners. Seal air fans are provided to supply sealing air for guillotine dampers at inlet CO-duct and boiler exhaust duct, to achieve 100% sealing. In addition, one more set of seal air fans (1w+1s) are provided to supply seal air to FD fan discharge dampers to prevent CO gas leaking to atmosphere in the event of boiler tripping. CO-gas ducting (from TPSV to CO-furnace) material is CS and is provided with a layer of Resco cast 17 EMC (125 mm thick) with SS 304H Hexmesh. CO-gas ducting to be provided with no external insulation. Refractory lining in CO-gas ducting downstream of TPSV shall be in line with the same on upstream of the TPSV. 4 Nos. Of sample cooler are provided for sampling of BFW, Boiler water, saturated steam and superheated steam. A wet gas scrubber will be installed on flue gas circuit at downstream of CO-Boiler system (i.e. after gilled ring economizer) to remove oxides of sulphur (SO2 & SO3) from flue gas before venting it to atmosphere through a separate

breadcrumb: Actual Photos of various components of manway cover of Steam Drum of Old Co Boiler > 5 HVGO PUMPS > FCCU-2 CO BOILER CONTROL LOOPS > 6.4.2 FCCU-I CO Boiler:
text: A wet gas scrubber will be installed on flue gas circuit at downstream of CO-Boiler system (i.e. after gilled ring economizer) to remove oxides of sulphur (SO2 & SO3) from flue gas before venting it to atmosphere through a separate stack. CO-Boiler system is designed for operation with & without the wet gas scrubber on line. For online operation of wet gas scrubbers, flue gas pressure shall be 300 mm WCG at inlet of wet gas scrubber at maximum flow rate of the gas (i.e. 111666 kg/hr at Case-VI operation). At other operating cases, flue gas pressure at inlet of scrubber shall be reduced accordingly (i.e. For case I & II operation, flue gas pressure shall be 82 mm WCG at inlet of scrubber). 6.6. Detailed Process Description of WHRS: It is proposed to recover heat from the hot flue gases of FCCU-II CO boiler before routing to FGD-II by providing a new waste heat boiler. The following fig shows the water and steam flow in the WHRS

text: BFW

breadcrumb: Actual Photos of various components of manway cover of Steam Drum of Old Co Boiler > 5 HVGO PUMPS > FCCU-2 CO BOILER CONTROL LOOPS > 6.4.2 FCCU-I CO Boiler:
text: The design exit temperature of the hot flue gases from FCCU-II CO boiler is 320 degC. This high stack temperature is due to inadequacy of the existing heat transfer area for the revamped conditions of FCCU-II. Around 6 MT of steam can be generated using the waste heat from the flue gases which will lower the temperature of the flue gases to around 200 degC before routing to FGD-II. Further, reduction in flue gas temperature will reduce service water make up rate in FGD- II due to reduced evaporation losses. 6.7. Detailed Process Description of PRDS: There are Two Types of PRDS (Pressure Reducing and De-Superheating) in PP-2 they are 1) VHP-MP PRDS & 2) MP-LP PRDS. VHP-MP PRDS System: VHP steam is reduced to MP level of 10.5 Kg/cm2 pressure in a pressure reducing station 140-PIC-301 operating on the control provided downstream of the control valves and is set to relieve at 12.5 KG/cm2 pressure. MP steam is desuperheated from 354 0 C to 250 0 C in a mechanical spray type desuperheater 140-X-03 by spraying MP BFW. 140-TIC- 301 controls the temperature at 250 0 C by regulating the control valve 140-V-301 provided on the BFW line. 140-TAH-301 sounds high temperature alarm at 270 0 C and 140-TAL-302 sounds low temperature alarm at 245 0 C of the MP steam downstream of desuperheater. 140-PAL-302 gives low pressure alarm of MP steam at 10.0 Kg/cm2. MP steam generated at

breadcrumb: Actual Photos of various components of manway cover of Steam Drum of Old Co Boiler > 5 HVGO PUMPS > FCCU-2 CO BOILER CONTROL LOOPS > 6.4.2 FCCU-I CO Boiler:
text: sounds high temperature alarm at 270 0 C and 140-TAL-302 sounds low temperature alarm at 245 0 C of the MP steam downstream of desuperheater. 140-PAL-302 gives low pressure alarm of MP steam at 10.0 Kg/cm2. MP steam generated at HRSG - I & HRSG - II joins to PP-II MP header through a 14” line. The same line also joins to PP-I MP steam header. The MP steam generated from HRSG - III and HRSG - IV is routed via 20” line to PP- II. It is interconnected with 12” MP steam header with 12” connection. Under VREP-II project one more reducing station provided to reduce HP steam to MP steam to meet the requirement. PV-2301 is utilized to reduce the steam through controller PIC-2301 at DCS. A 12” tap-off is taken from new VHP header coming from FCCU-(R) and after

breadcrumb: Actual Photos of various components of manway cover of Steam Drum of Old Co Boiler > 5 HVGO PUMPS > FCCU-2 CO BOILER CONTROL LOOPS > 6.4.2 FCCU-I CO Boiler:
text: Power Plant-2 Operating Manual Chapter No:6 Rev. No.2 Page 37 of 251

breadcrumb: Actual Photos of various components of manway cover of Steam Drum of Old Co Boiler > 5 HVGO PUMPS > FCCU-2 CO BOILER CONTROL LOOPS > 6.4.2 FCCU-I CO Boiler:
text: reduction connected to new MP steam line from CPP through a 16” line. A desuperheater 140-X-09 is provided in the downstream of control valve to reduce temperature 360 to 250 0 C. Max allowable flow through 140-X-09 is 45 t/hr. Temperature is controlled by TIC 2301. Safety valves PSV - 2301 / 2302 are provided after reducing station with a set pressure 12.5 kg/cm2. MP steam is also produced in the steam generators of CDU - II, CDU - III and FCCU - II. Exhaust of HP BFW pump is also MP steam. MP steam is distributed to the following units:  To PP-I through 8” header, 140-FR/FQ-302 records and integrates the MP steam flow rate exported. The normal export rate is 10-15 t/hr. But the interconnection is designed for a flow rate of 20 t/hr.  To High Lift Pump House through 10” header  To FCCU - II unit through 12” header  To CDU - II through 12” header  To flare through 6” header  To Bitumen storage and drum filling facilities through 3” header.  To CDU - III  To DHDS MP-LP PRDS System: LP steam is mainly produced at the exhaust of steam turbines. At PP-II Boiler FD fans and MP BFW pump turbine exhaust producing LP steam. Further requirement of LP steam is produced by reducing MP steam to LP steam level in pressure reducing station. At the pressure reducing station, LP

breadcrumb: Actual Photos of various components of manway cover of Steam Drum of Old Co Boiler > 5 HVGO PUMPS > FCCU-2 CO BOILER CONTROL LOOPS > 6.4.2 FCCU-I CO Boiler:
text: turbines. At PP-II Boiler FD fans and MP BFW pump turbine exhaust producing LP steam. Further requirement of LP steam is produced by reducing MP steam to LP steam level in pressure reducing station. At the pressure reducing station, LP steam pressure is controlled by split range pressure controller 140-PIC-302 acting on pressure control valves 140-PV-302A/A’/B. When LP steam header pressure goes below the set point of 3.5 Kg/cm2, control valves 140-PV- 302 A/A’ will be regulated and 140-PV-302B will remain close. When LP steam header pressure goes above the set point, then 140-PV-302A/A’ regulated to maintain header pressure. After complete closing of control vakve140-PV-302 A/A’ if the LP header pressure still remain in the higher side, 140-PV-302 opens and LP steam vented to atmosphere to control the pressure. Pressure safety valve 140-PSV-302, set to relieve at 5.5 Kg/cm2 pressure, is provided downstream of pressure control valves 140-PV-302 A/A’. Steam is desuperheated from 250 0 C to 160 0 C in mechanical spray type Desuperheater 140-X-04 by spraying MP BFW. The temperature is maintained at 160 0 C by 140-TIC- 302 located atleast 4 meter away from the downstream of desuperheater by regulating the control valve 140-TV-302 located on the BFW line. 140-TAH-303 sounds pressure alarm of LP steam header at 3.2 Kg/cm2. LP steam produced in steam generators of units and exhaust of IFO pump turbine also joins the LP steam header. To meet VREP - II & DHDS

breadcrumb: Actual Photos of various components of manway cover of Steam Drum of Old Co Boiler > 5 HVGO PUMPS > FCCU-2 CO BOILER CONTROL LOOPS > 6.4.2 FCCU-I CO Boiler:
text: BFW line. 140-TAH-303 sounds pressure alarm of LP steam header at 3.2 Kg/cm2. LP steam produced in steam generators of units and exhaust of IFO pump turbine also joins the LP steam header. To meet VREP - II & DHDS requirement, one more reducing station is provided to reduce MP steam to LP steam. One tap-off is taken from new MP header from HRSG III & IV and reduced through PV-2303 with PIC-2303 in DCS. One attemperator 140-X-10 (with TIC 2303) is provided in the reduced 20” line to reduce temperature from 250 0 C to 155 0 C. Maximum Flow allowable through the attemperator is 30T/hr. Safety valves PSV-2303/2304/2305 are provided after reducing station with a set pressure 5.5 Kg/cm2. LP steam is distributed to the following consumers:  To Deaerators  To fuel oil tank farm  To FCCU – II  To CDU – II

text: 6.8 Detailed Process Description of BCW System:

breadcrumb: Actual Photos of various components of manway cover of Steam Drum of Old Co Boiler > 5 HVGO PUMPS > 6.8 Detailed Process Description of BCW System:
text: VREP-I BCW System: Bearing cooling water requirement is met by four pumps 170-PM-5A/B/C and 170/24- PM-2G2. 170-PM-5A/C are of capacity 350 m3/hr at 6 kg/cm2 pressure and 170/24-PM- 2G2 is of capacity 97 m3/hr at 6.5 kg/cm2 pressure take suction from the sump and discharge into a 8 inch supply header. This header supplies to all the units. The 8 inch supply header is provided with a pressure gauge, Temperature Indicator, Pressure transmitter and flow transmitter. Also a low pressure alarm provided, which sounds at MOI PP panel when low pressure activates. There is one more pump 170-PM-5B is available of capacity 275 m3/hr at 3.5 kg/cm2 pressure. This pump can not be put in service due to low discharge pressure (shut off pressure 4.5 kg/cm2). Normally two pumps one of 170-PM-5A/C with combination of 17024-PM-2G2 will be in service and the second one is available as standby. BCW after serving its purpose, return to cooling tower through a 8 inch header. One 6 inch line from Merox joins to 8 inch header near the battery limit. One more 6 inch return header from CDU-2 joined to the return header near the tower. A 2 inch tapping provided in return header for blowdown. The return header provided with a sample point and temperature indicator. The hot water from various units is cooled in the induced draft cross flow tower. The tower is designed for a maximum circulating rate 250m3/hr. It

breadcrumb: Actual Photos of various components of manway cover of Steam Drum of Old Co Boiler > 5 HVGO PUMPS > 6.8 Detailed Process Description of BCW System:
text: header for blowdown. The return header provided with a sample point and temperature indicator. The hot water from various units is cooled in the induced draft cross flow tower. The tower is designed for a maximum circulating rate 250m3/hr. It has two cells each of capacity 125 m3/hr. The hot water from return header distributed to both the shells either side. The distribution of water can be adjusted by the help of butterfly valves (4nos). The water then distributed through distributing nozzles and falls across the path of air flow. Packings are provided inside the tower for braking of water particles and increase the contact time with air for greater cooling. The cooled water is then collected in cooling tower basin. The basin has two compartments each receiving water from respective shells. Water from these compartments flows via two channels to BCW sump. A screen is installed at the inlet of suction of pump for preventing entry of foreign materials to pump. The suction sump is having over flow and draining facility. The sump is provided with high and low level alarms, which sound at MOI PP panel . The sump also provided with a level controller, which controls makeup water flow depending on level. To supplement the loss of cooling water due to leak, blowdown, evaporation and drift through cooling tower, raw water is supplied through 2 inch line by raw water pumps 170-PM-02A/B/D/E situated at DM-2. Water flow

breadcrumb: Actual Photos of various components of manway cover of Steam Drum of Old Co Boiler > 5 HVGO PUMPS > 6.8 Detailed Process Description of BCW System:
text: water flow depending on level. To supplement the loss of cooling water due to leak, blowdown, evaporation and drift through cooling tower, raw water is supplied through 2 inch line by raw water pumps 170-PM-02A/B/D/E situated at DM-2. Water flow is controlled by control valve, which

breadcrumb: Actual Photos of various components of manway cover of Steam Drum of Old Co Boiler > 5 HVGO PUMPS > 6.8 Detailed Process Description of BCW System:
text: Power Plant-2 Operating Manual Chapter No:6 Rev. No.2 Page 39 of 251

breadcrumb: Actual Photos of various components of manway cover of Steam Drum of Old Co Boiler > 5 HVGO PUMPS > 6.8 Detailed Process Description of BCW System:
text: get input from sump level controller. By-pass facility of control valve is also available. In case of failure of make-up water supply, raw water which is stored for CRP filter back wash in the Back wash raw water tank can be routed. Also DM water can be used for makeup through filter back wash lines. Usually 80-90% sump level is maintained. VREP-II BCW System: VREP-2 Bearing cooling water requirement is met by two pumps 170-PM-9A/B. 170- PM-9A/B of capacity 400 m3/hr at 5.5 kg/cm2 pressure take suction from the sump and discharge into a 12 inch supply header. From the supply header one 3 inch tap-off is taken for FCCU feed pump and Reciculating cooling water tower. Another 10 inch tap- off taken for Merox unit and air compressor. There is a 6 inch tie-in between VREP-1 and VREP-2 BCW supply at Merox unit. After the take of for Merox block the 12 inch header reduced to 10 inch and supply water to CDU-3. From this 10 inch header one 2 inch tap-off is taken to cater to VBU feed pump near DM plant. The supply header is provided with a pressure gauge, Temperature Indicator, Pressure transmitter and flow transmitter. Also a low pressure alarm provided, which sounds at MOI PP panel when low pressure activates. Auto cut-in facility of spare pump is also available. At 4.5kg/cm2 auto cut-in of spare pumps takes place. BCW returns form all consumer units

breadcrumb: Actual Photos of various components of manway cover of Steam Drum of Old Co Boiler > 5 HVGO PUMPS > 6.8 Detailed Process Description of BCW System:
text: Also a low pressure alarm provided, which sounds at MOI PP panel when low pressure activates. Auto cut-in facility of spare pump is also available. At 4.5kg/cm2 auto cut-in of spare pumps takes place. BCW returns form all consumer units as same size of supply headers are connected to a 12 inch header which feeds warm water to cooling tower top. One 3 inch tapping provided in return header for Side stream filter 170-X-09 of capacity 8 m3/hr. It filters suspended solids 60 ppm at inlet to 10 ppm at outlet. Feed line to filter is provided with a flow indicator. One DP indicator is provided across the filter bed. Max allowable DP is 0.5 kg/cm2. After filtration, this water returns to cooling water sump. Back flushing facility is provided for the Side stream filter. This is to be back washed once in a day or if DP across the filter crosses 0.5 kg/cm2 which ever comes earlier. The return header provided with a pressure gauge, Temperature gauge and temperature indicator. One 8 inch tapping provided from return header for blow down. Blow down water is routed to surface drain. The hot water from the various units is cooled in the induced draft cross flow tower. The tower is designed for a maximum circulating rate 400m3/hr. It has two cells each of capacity 200 m3/hr. The hot water from return header distributed to both shells on either side. The distribution

breadcrumb: Actual Photos of various components of manway cover of Steam Drum of Old Co Boiler > 5 HVGO PUMPS > 6.8 Detailed Process Description of BCW System:
text: the induced draft cross flow tower. The tower is designed for a maximum circulating rate 400m3/hr. It has two cells each of capacity 200 m3/hr. The hot water from return header distributed to both shells on either side. The distribution of water can be adjusted by help of butterfly valves on individual distribution line. The water then distributed through distributing nozzles and fall across the path of air flow. Packing are provided inside the tower for braking of water particle and increase the contact time with air for greater cooling. The cooled water then collected in cooling tower basin. The basin has two compartments each receiving water from respective shell. Water from these compartment flows via two channels to BCW sump. A double screen is installed at the channel near inlet of sump for preventing entry of foreign materials to the pump suction. The basins are provided with isolating gate. Each basin is provided with drain. The suction sump is having over flow and draining facility. The drain valve operated to remove mud and slim deposit. BCW pumps take suction from the sump. Suction strainers are provided inside the sump. The sump is provided with high and low level alarms, which sound at MOI PP panel . The sump also provided with a level controller, which controls make-up water flow depending on level. To supplement the loss of cooling water due to leak, blowdown, evaporation and drift through cooling tower,

breadcrumb: Actual Photos of various components of manway cover of Steam Drum of Old Co Boiler > 5 HVGO PUMPS > 6.8 Detailed Process Description of BCW System:
text: which sound at MOI PP panel . The sump also provided with a level controller, which controls make-up water flow depending on level. To supplement the loss of cooling water due to leak, blowdown, evaporation and drift through cooling tower, raw water is supplied through 4 inch line by raw water pump 170- PM-02A/B situated at DM-2. One flow meter is provided on the makeup water line to know the makeup. Water flow is controlled by control valve, which gets input from sump level controller. By-pass facility of control valve also available. In case of failure of make-up water supply, raw water which is stored for CRP filter back wash in the Back

breadcrumb: Actual Photos of various components of manway cover of Steam Drum of Old Co Boiler > 5 LT-4104 CBD TANK > 7.2 WHRS Equipment Details
text: 7.2 WHRS Equipment Details

breadcrumb: Actual Photos of various components of manway cover of Steam Drum of Old Co Boiler > 5 LT-4104 CBD TANK > 7.2 WHRS Equipment Details
text:
The WHRS is a Single-drum bottom supported design with provision for free expansion
upwards. Boiler structure & Bottom supporting transmits all loads to the boiler foundation.
Major boiler components can be defined as under:

breadcrumb: Actual Photos of various components of manway cover of Steam Drum of Old Co Boiler > 5 LT-4104 CBD TANK > STEAM DRUM
text: STEAM DRUM

breadcrumb: Actual Photos of various components of manway cover of Steam Drum of Old Co Boiler > 5 LT-4104 CBD TANK > STEAM DRUM
text:
The drum is made of CS plate, cold rolled to a circular shape and fitted with dished ends
and manholes to allow easy accessibility for maintenance. Holes are drilled in the drum for
connecting down-comer / riser tubes for maintaining natural circulation. Steam separator
& baffles made of MS plates are fitted inside the drum, to separate the steam from water
and dry the same. Connections are also provided to check water level through gauge glass
and control the level through automatic controllers. Nozzles / stubs are provided to mount
safety valve, vent valve, pressure & temperature gauges.
EVAPORATOR:
Coil type tubular Evaporator, connect with inlet & outlet headers consisting of 38.1 OD x
5 mm THK tube provided. Evaporator inlet header connected to steam drum thru down
comer and top header is also connected to steam drum thru riser. This boiler has a single
evaporator. Evaporator is enclosed 4 sides by Boiler casing with refractory application.
ECONOMISER
Coil type tubular economizer, consisting of 38.1 OD x 5 mm THK tube with inlet & outlet
headers. Economizer is enclosed 4 sides by Boiler casing with refractory application.
Power Plant-2 Operating Manual Chapter No:7 Rev. No.2 Page 55 of 251

breadcrumb: Actual Photos of various components of manway cover of Steam Drum of Old Co Boiler > 5 LT-4104 CBD TANK > 8.2 Functioning of (operating Mechanism) of the complicated control Schemes:
text: 8.2 Functioning of (operating Mechanism) of the complicated control Schemes:

breadcrumb: Actual Photos of various components of manway cover of Steam Drum of Old Co Boiler > 5 LT-4104 CBD TANK > 8.2 Functioning of (operating Mechanism) of the complicated control Schemes: > Boiler Drum level control (Three Element Drum level Control)
text: Boiler Drum level control (Three Element Drum level Control)

breadcrumb: Actual Photos of various components of manway cover of Steam Drum of Old Co Boiler > 5 LT-4104 CBD TANK > 8.2 Functioning of (operating Mechanism) of the complicated control Schemes: > Boiler Drum level control (Three Element Drum level Control)
text:
The boiler drum is located near the top of the boiler. Its main function is to provide
surface area and volume for separation of steam from water. The measure of water
quantity in a boiler is indicated by the level of the water steam interface in the drum.
A drum level control system is designed for a continuous mass balance. The liquid level
in the drum must be kept within a narrow range of operation. Too high a level causes
water and it’s dissolved solids to be carried over into the steam system. Too low
circulation functions and may even result in tube rupture (burn out) due to lack of cooling
water on the boiling surfaces.
Power Plant-2 Operating Manual Chapter No:8 Rev. No.2 Page 63 of 251

breadcrumb: Actual Photos of various components of manway cover of Steam Drum of Old Co Boiler > 5 LT-4104 CBD TANK > 8.2 Functioning of (operating Mechanism) of the complicated control Schemes: > Boiler Drum level control (Three Element Drum level Control)
text:
In a three element drum level control system, three variables, viz., steam flow, feed water
flow and drum level, influence the feed water valve position (ref Fig). Steam flow is
measured by the flow transmitter FT whose signal is transmitted to the feed water flow
computer FY1. Drum level is measured by the level transmitter LT1 and its signal is
transmitted to the drum level controller LIC 1, which produces the necessary corrective
signal to maintain drum level at its set point. The controller’s output is transmitted to the
feed water flow computer, which combines the signals from the two variables and sends
an output in the form of set point to the feed water flow control FC1. Feed water flow is
measured by the flow transmitter FT2 whose signal is transmitted to the feed water
controller FC1, which produces the necessary corrective action to main feed water flow
vis-a-vis its set point. An output signal is then transmitted from feed water flow
controller FC1 to the feed water control valve FCV2.

breadcrumb: Actual Photos of various components of manway cover of Steam Drum of Old Co Boiler > 5 LT-4104 CBD TANK > 8.4 Trips/Interlocks Bypassing Procedure:
text: 8.4 Trips/Interlocks Bypassing Procedure:

breadcrumb: Actual Photos of various components of manway cover of Steam Drum of Old Co Boiler > 5 LT-4104 CBD TANK > 8.4 Trips/Interlocks Bypassing Procedure:
text:
In Refinery operation, various systems/ critical equipment’s are equipment’s are
equipped with safety interlocks and trips as protection devices to avoid the system/
equipment landing into abnormal/ unintended operations.
All operating personnel to ensure that the safety interlocks/trips are always in service for
smooth operation and to ensure required protection for all the critical equipment systems
as intended
The following procedure to be followed for bypassing trip interlocks
1. All trips & interlocks should always be on line
2. If any particular trip / interlock is to be bypassed; forced for any maintenance work,
it should be with the concurrence of unit-in-charge (during dayshift or working day)
or Division Head . Online requisition for Trip bypass to be made through TIMS
(Trips and Interlock Management System) and the required entries in the trip
register should be made. In off day shift hours, RSM/ Unit in charge/division head
of operations are authorized to grant permission.
3. Any trip/ interlock not on line for more than 72hrs has to be intimated to GM-
Operations in the specified format (annexure-I)

breadcrumb: Actual Photos of various components of manway cover of Steam Drum of Old Co Boiler > 5 LT-4104 CBD TANK > 8.4 Trips/Interlocks Bypassing Procedure:
text: Power Plant-2 Operating Manual Chapter No:8 Rev. No.2 Page 68 of 251

breadcrumb: Actual Photos of various components of manway cover of Steam Drum of Old Co Boiler > 5 LT-4104 CBD TANK > 8.4 Trips/Interlocks Bypassing Procedure:
text:
4. Any trip/ interlock not on line for more than 7days, has to be intimated to GM-
Operations in the specified Format (Foramt-II).
5. For all trips/ logistics, Instrument maintenance (in consultation with operation)
should develop and implement suitable preventive maintenance checks at suitable
periodicity.

text: ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- -- Check-list for sending : Monitoring by Sr. Manager - Refinery Safety  Issue discussed in Unit Area Meeting on  Trip taken back on line _____________ date ______________  Reported in Management Safety  Issue discussed with Division Heads of Committee Meeting on ______________ Instrumentation, PAD 6. For all trip / logics, instrument maintenance ( in concurrence with operations) should develop and implement suitable preventive maintenance checks at suitable periodicity 7. Procedure for bypassing the trip tag for handing over to instrumentation 1. In Boiler/Equipment operation the trip tags play a vital role in safe guarding the Boiler/Equipment from excessive operating conditions like level, pressure, temperature etc. For proper functioning of the trip tags PM checks as well as calibration of the tags is to be done in a periodical time frame. Handing over the tags for PM checks or for repairs without getting disturbance to the operation of the boiler is important. 8. Procedure For Bypassing The Trip Tag For Handing Over To Instrumentation a) Prior to bypassing any trip, clearance is to be taken from Unit Manager & inform the same to RSM. b) Inform the concerned operator regarding the activity prior to bypassing the trip tag. c) Monitor the parameters and maintain the process manually without any process upset & bypass the trip tag ensuring with the field operator & handover the trip tag to instrumentation. Power Plant-2 Operating Manual Chapter No:8 Rev. No.2 Page

e) If the trip tag bypass time exceeds 72 hrs and the tag is still in bypassed condition, it
has to be informed to General Manager (Operations) for approval.

text:
Rotary/Electrical PM Checks
a) Prior to handing over the equipment to the maintenance (rotary/electrical), unit
Manager Clearance is required.
b) Ensure the Stand by equipment either rotary/electrical healthiness before releasing
the in service particular equipment.
c) Instruments related to instrumentation rectification will be taken up in forced
condition with prior permission from unit Manager. These instruments cannot be
released on PM checks basis.
If proper scheduling could not be done on the particular, day for releasing the equipment,
again rescheduling to be done to release the particular equipment ensuring the standby
equipment is healthy.

Alarm Sub Status: AOF (Alarm Output Off):
Normally when an alarm occurs:
 The tag mark changes color depending on the type of the alarm.
 Audible alarm is heard.
 The alarm status (HI/LO/HH/LL etc…) is displayed on the process alarm window.
 In AOF mode:
 The tag mark changes to dark blue color irrespective of the alarm.
 Audible alarm is put off.
 The alarm status is displayed only on the instrument faceplate.
 All the alarms status except IOP & OOP on the instrument are not displayed on
the process alarm window.

To put the instrument to AOF mode:
Power Plant-2 Operating Manual Chapter No:9 Rev. No.2 Page 77 of 251

text:
 Go to the tuning window of the instrument.
 Select AOF icon. Confirm.

To bring the instrument back to normal mode:
 Go to the tuning window of the instrument.
 Select AOF icon once again. Confirm.

breadcrumb: Actual Photos of various components of manway cover of Steam Drum of Old Co Boiler > 12.0 Start-up procedure of plant after short shut down:
text:
Short shutdowns in the plant are due to Boilers trip, BFW pumps trip or power failure.
The plant to be restarted as per procedures explained below.

text: 3. Open all the drains of all headers (VHP, HP, MP & LP) so that all the steam line should be free from condensate. 4. All the man holes and covers should be properly closed. 5. Keep HP - MP, Atomizing steam & MP-LP reducing station in close position. 6. Take export steam from Power Plant - I. 7. When dry steam will come through the drains of 400# 150# and 20# steam headers, close the drain and put steam trap in service. 8. Open the valves of steam tracing line of FO line. 9. Establish fuel oil circulation. 10. Put FD fan in service. 11. Super heater inlet and outlet drain valve should be in open position. 12. Startup vent valve should be in open position. 13. Steam drum vent valve should in open position. 14. Clean the Burner and fix it properly with gasket. 15. All the relief valves must be on line. 16. All the instrument air valves should be in open position. Keep all the transmitters in service. 17. Open the block valves before and after the oil and water control valves. 18. Fill up the Boiler and put Boiler gauge glasses in service. 19. Open atomizing main stop valve for individual Boiler. 20. Open main Igniter Gas stop valve. 21. Open main Fuel Gas stop valve. 22. Open Fuel oil recirculation valve. 23. Give Power supply to BMS panel. 24. Make sure that all the

text: atomizing main stop valve for individual Boiler. 20. Open main Igniter Gas stop valve. 21. Open main Fuel Gas stop valve. 22. Open Fuel oil recirculation valve. 23. Give Power supply to BMS panel. 24. Make sure that all the systems of BMS panel to be used are in working condition. 25. Make sure that all the trip system is working (by simulation). 26. Check the igniter. 27. Be sure that all burners are cleaned. 28. Start the Boiler through BMS. Put the pilot in service. 29. Put oil Burner in service. 30. Keep the Boiler in low firing position. To attain a pressure of 36 kg/cm2 from Cold condition min. 5 hours should be given.

text:
32. Keep on watching the water level in the gauge glass and maintain the level until the
boiler stabilizes.
33. Once Boiler is stabilized, change fuel oil & feed water control to auto mode.
34. When drum pressure reaches 2 kg/cm2, close drum vent and super heater inlet drain.
35. When the drum pressure reaches up to 36 kg/cm2, open the main stop valve.
36. When the Boilers come on line close super heater outlet drain valve and start up vent
valve
37. Adjust the flame pattern by adjusting Air resisters.
38. Put fuel gas in service if available.
39. Slowly open all reducing stations.
40. Allow all condensate to drain.
41. Close the drain valves when dry steam comes through the drain.

breadcrumb: Actual Photos of various components of manway cover of Steam Drum of Old Co Boiler > 31 During the initial firing keep FW and oil control in manual control. > 12.2 Start-up of PRDS after short shut down:
text: 12.2 Start-up of PRDS after short shut down:

breadcrumb: Actual Photos of various components of manway cover of Steam Drum of Old Co Boiler > 31 During the initial firing keep FW and oil control in manual control. > 12.2 Start-up of PRDS after short shut down:
text:
1. Keep all block valve on PRDS in closed position
2. Slowly open the control valve bypass valve and block valves’ equalizing valves to
charge the PRDS
3. Once PRDS Headers charged ensure control valve in closed position and keep the
control valve in auto and set the PRDS Down pressures.
4. Slowly close the control valve’s by pass valve and observe control valve action if
control valve is not acted accordingly, maintain steam pressure manually by
operating bypass valve and follow with instrument maintenance for repair the control
valve.
5. If PRDS downstream pressure maintained with control valve keep it in service and
close bypass valve.

breadcrumb: Actual Photos of various components of manway cover of Steam Drum of Old Co Boiler > 6 Keep Attemperator in service by keeping the manual valve in service. > 12.3 Start-up of BCW Systems after short shut down:
text: 12.3 Start-up of BCW Systems after short shut down:

breadcrumb: Actual Photos of various components of manway cover of Steam Drum of Old Co Boiler > 6 Keep Attemperator in service by keeping the manual valve in service. > 12.3 Start-up of BCW Systems after short shut down:
text:
1. Clean the BCW sump thoroughly and drain the water from sump.
2. Fill the BCW Sump with service water through make up water line, Keep chemical
dosing in service.
3. Keep BCW supply and return valves in open position at battery limit.
4. Open BCW pumps suction and discharge valves in open position and start the BCW
pump.
5. Maintain BCW water supply pressure to rated valve i.e around 6-7kg/cm2 by
throttling the butterfly valves provided at the top of BCW tower.
6. Start ID fans, run both the ID fans in service if required to maintain the BCW supply
temperature at 30-35 degC.

breadcrumb: Actual Photos of various components of manway cover of Steam Drum of Old Co Boiler > 6 Keep Attemperator in service by keeping the manual valve in service. > 12.4 Start-up of FD fan after short shut down:
text: 12.4 Start-up of FD fan after short shut down:

breadcrumb: Actual Photos of various components of manway cover of Steam Drum of Old Co Boiler > 6 Keep Attemperator in service by keeping the manual valve in service. > 12.4 Start-up of FD fan after short shut down:
text:
1. If Motor tripped due to power failure, start the turbine driven pump
2. To start Turbine driven pump, inform to MOI Engineer to manage steam demand.
3. Ensure pump suction and discharge valves & BCW valves in open position.
4. Drain condensate from turbine and slowly increase the speed of the turbine.
5. Ensure Lube oil pressure is greater than 1.0kg/cm2
6. Increase the turbine speed till Feed water pressure is increased.

text:
1. Inform Shift engineer Ensure who will inform Electrical substation Engineer for
restoration of power.
2. Close isolation valves on fuel oil & Fuel gas lines. Water lines and steam line at
battery limits
3. Isolate PRDS upstream valves.
4. Once power resumed, open battery limits

breadcrumb: Actual Photos of various components of manway cover of Steam Drum of Old Co Boiler > 6 Start Boilers as per procedure. > 12.6 WHRS Startup Boiler Startup Sequence after Short shutdown or Emergency
text:
Shutdown:
1. All air vent and Start up vent partially open and Drain valve closed.
2. Water fills in the Boiler 50 percent of Steam Drum.
3. Start CO Boiler by closing new boiler inlet damper and open damper towards Chimney’
4. Open the Boiler inlet damper partially instead of slowly heat up boiler.
5. After riches 2 Kg/cm²(g) pressure all air vent closed except Startup vent.
6. After rise 5 Kg/cm²(g) crack leak Steam stop valve.
7. Steam line drain valve open.
8. After rise pressure 9 Kg/cm²(g) Steam stop valve to be open and start up vent and all
vent to be closed.
9. Then adjust – Boiler inlet damper & damper towards chimney.
10. Start Feed Water Pump and maintain Normal Water level of steam drum.
11. Every after 4 hours 1-minute main blow down valve should be opened (Apx.3% of total
draw steam.)
12. Every after 12-hour Soot blower should be operated.
13. Water quality checking every after 2 hours – Feed water and Boiler water and as per
requirement Chemical should dose.
14. Maintain Boiler log book.

13.1. Steps / Sequence of activities for start-up after T&I:
The following activities to be done for Start - up of the Unit / Equipment T&I
i. Receipt of start-up clearance forms from Other sections (Annexure-II)
ii. Checking the all the points mentioned in Start-up check list (Annexure-I)
iii. De-blinding of piping and Equipment
iv. Leak checking and identifying the problem areas
v. Checking of Trips / Interlocks and other critical control schemes / instruments
vi. Utilities commissioning
vii. Procedure for start-up of the plant

text:
1. After T&I completion, the following checkups to be carried out and Equipment to be
restarted as per Normal operation procedure mentioned in Chapter-14
2. Boilers trip checks to be carried out and to be recorded in trip check register.
3. Boiler Live test to be carried out and pop & reset pressure value to be noted in RV
check register.
4. Dearator low & high level alarms to be checked.
5. Auto operation of all MOV (Motor operated Valves) to be checked.

1. Take clearance from all the concerned departments.
2. Inform all concerned including RSM regarding start-up activity.
Power Plant-2 Operating Manual Chapter No:13 Rev. No.2 Page 99 of 251

breadcrumb: Actual Photos of various components of manway cover of Steam Drum of Old Co Boiler > 6 Start Boilers as per procedure. > 13.2 Details of each step of activities for start-up after T&I: > 13.2.7 Detailed procedure for Boiler start up:
text:
3. Confirm furnace man ways are in close position.
4. Confirm that all blinds are removed and entered in the register.
5. All foreign material should be removed from the Boiler area.
6. Ensure that both oil burners are properly fixed and with start-up tip.
7. Drum vents to be kept in open position.
8. Start-up vent to be kept in open position.
9. Both the gauge glasses to be kept in service condition.
10. Super heater inlet and outlet drains to be kept in open position.
11. All the instruments take off valves to be kept in open position.
12. All trip switches to be kept in position.
13. Carry out stroke check of all control valves and ensure that all are in working
condition.
14. Blow down valve to be kept in close position.
15. Feed water to be routed through preheater and Boiler to be filled with Boiler feed
water.
16. The water level in the steam drum to be kept at operating level.
17. Ensure that the Economizer outlet (Stack) damper is in open position.
18. Take clearance from CPP to start the FD Fan motor.
19. FD fan Motor to turbine and turbine to motor auto cut in systems to be checked.

breadcrumb: Actual Photos of various components of manway cover of Steam Drum of Old Co Boiler > 20 Start FD fan turbine as per procedure and keep the turbine at minimum operating

breadcrumb: Actual Photos of various components of manway cover of Steam Drum of Old Co Boiler > 20 Start FD fan turbine as per procedure and keep the turbine at minimum operating
text: RPM. i.e at 3200 RPM. 21. Keep Motor driven FD fan in Auto in field as well as in MOI. 22. Switch ON the power to igniters. 23. Establish both burners’ pilot flames. 24. Establish fuel oil recirculation and bring fuel oil temperature to more than 100 deg C. 25. Start Bottom gas burner or oil burner. 26. Check the flame pattern and adjust the gas flow. 27. Confirm drum level transmitters are matching with gauge glasses. 28. Maintain drum level at operating condition by giving blow down as and when required. 29. Steam temperature raise should be limited to 55 deg C per hour. 30. Close the drum vents when the drum pressure reaches 2 kg/cm2. 31. Close the super heater inlet drain when drum pressure reaches 4 kg/cm2. 32. TSP dosing is to be introduced. 33. Keep all the drains on MSL header in open position and throttle when dry steam is coming out. 34. Increase the firing rate on bottom burner. 35. Keep top gas or oil burner also in to service. 36. Open the main stop valve before drum pressure reaching line pressure (36.5 Kg/Cm2). 37. Open the MSL MOV after Main steam line temperature reached to operating temperature (365 deg C). 38. Take de super heater control valve in to service. 39. Close the Start-up vent. 40. Close the Super heater outlet drain. 41. Take feed water control in auto. 42. According to the load

breadcrumb: Actual Photos of various components of manway cover of Steam Drum of Old Co Boiler > 20 Start FD fan turbine as per procedure and keep the turbine at minimum operating
text: to operating temperature (365 deg C). 38. Take de super heater control valve in to service. 39. Close the Start-up vent. 40. Close the Super heater outlet drain. 41. Take feed water control in auto. 42. According to the load requirement keep the standby oil/gas burners in service. 43. Maintain outlet O2 in between 2 to 3 %. 44. All the above activities are to be carried out in coordination with MOI supervisor.

text: Turn around inspection is the inspection to be carried about as per Schedule fixed in T3C (Turnaround Time Table) Schedule. It will be done annually. Inspection crew thoroughly check the WHRS and release IWL (Inspection Work list) which contains all repair to be done maintenance crew during T&I. After completion of all repairs on WHRS, it is to be started as per procedure mentioned in the following steps. a. Receipt of start-up clearance forms from Other sections b. Checking the all the points mentioned in Start-up check list (Annexure-I) c. De-blinding of piping and Equipment d. Leak checking and identifying the problem areas e. Checking of Trips / Interlocks and other critical control schemes / instruments f. Utilities commissioning g. Procedure for start-up of the plant h. WHRS Startup Sequence after T&I is as follows 1. Ensure Economizer outlet damper is in open position 2. Ensure Feed water quality is good. 3. All air vent and Start up vent partially open and Drain valve closed. 4. Fill water in the Boiler up to 50 percent of Steam Drum. 5. Start CO Boiler by closing new boiler inlet damper and open damper towards Chimney’ 6. Open the Boiler inlet damper partially instead of slowly heat up boiler. 7. After riches 2 Kg/cm²(g) pressure all air vent closed except Startup vent. 8. After rise 5 Kg/cm²(g) crack leak Steam stop valve. 9. Steam line drain valve open. 10. After rise pressure 9

breadcrumb: Actual Photos of various components of manway cover of Steam Drum of Old Co Boiler > 20 Start FD fan turbine as per procedure and keep the turbine at minimum operating > 13.2.8 WHRS Startup Procedure after T&I
text: instead of slowly heat up boiler. 7. After riches 2 Kg/cm²(g) pressure all air vent closed except Startup vent. 8. After rise 5 Kg/cm²(g) crack leak Steam stop valve. 9. Steam line drain valve open. 10. After rise pressure 9 Kg/cm²(g) Steam stop valve to be open and start up vent and all vent to be closed. 11. Then adjust – Boiler inlet damper & damper towards chimney. 12. Start Feed Water Pump and maintain Normal Water level of steam drum. 13. Every after 4 hours 1-minute main blow down valve should be opened (Apx.3% of total draw steam.) 14. Every after 12-hour Soot blower should be operated. 15. Water quality checking every after 2 hours – Feed water and Boiler water and as per requirement Chemical should dose. 16. Maintain Boiler log book.

breadcrumb: Actual Photos of various components of manway cover of Steam Drum of Old Co Boiler > 20 Start FD fan turbine as per procedure and keep the turbine at minimum operating > 13.2.9 Sequence of operation for VHP steam header charging:

breadcrumb: Actual Photos of various components of manway cover of Steam Drum of Old Co Boiler > 20 Start FD fan turbine as per procedure and keep the turbine at minimum operating > 13.2.9 Sequence of operation for VHP steam header charging:
text:
1. Take clearance from Projects after completion of DHT VHP steam header hook up.
2. Take clearance from Maintenance after completion of the steam controller LSE
clamp.
3. Take clearance from RSM & MOI for charging the VHP steam header at IBH
battery limit.
4. Operate VHP battery limit isolation valve equalizing valve slowly for charging
towards IBH boilers VHP steam header.
5. Bleed the condensate in VHP steam header completely through drain bypass valves
and pressurize the header slowly.
6. Check the header leaks if any at repaired locations.
7. Compare the header instruments from field to MOI DCS.

text:
Normally FCCU-1 (New) CO Boiler, FCCU-2 (Old) CO Boilers & FCCU-I CO
Boiler-9 or 10 will be in service when complete steam generation from Units (76 T/HR)
and CPP (180 T/HR) is available. Depending on steam demand like maintenance/IBR
inspection of one boiler/HRSG or Unit start-up/shut down standby boilers at PP-1
(WIL-8 or BHPV) will be put on line. One standby boiler will be there always for
emergency use.
Average loads of UTILITIES:FCCU-1 (New) CO Boiler: 35TPH
FCCU-2 (Old) CO Boiler:25TPH
FCCU-I CO Boiler-9 or-10:48TPH
Average loads of CPP: HRSG-I or II:24TPH
HRSG-III:52TPH
HRSG-IV:32TPH
HRSG-V :32TPH
HRSG-VI:42TPH
Scenario-I

breadcrumb: Actual Photos of various components of manway cover of Steam Drum of Old Co Boiler > 8 Start IBH Boiler-9 with normal start up procedure and bring the boiler into service. > 14.2 Normal operation of Boilers: > 14.2.1 Steam management when one Boiler trips (Steam Control Procedure)
text: In case of IBH-9 or 10 Boiler trip (Deficit 48 T/hr), steam loads can be balanced in the following way Increase load on FCCU-1 (New) CO boiler. 07 T/hr Increase load on FCCU-2 (Old) CO Boiler. 20 T/hr Reduce steam to De aerators momentarily. 10 T/hr Change turbine to motor in Units. 8T/hr Reduce steam to tank form. 3 T/hr Total 48 T/hr Tripped Boiler can be restarted after the fault finding, corrective action and temporary measures taken. In case of delay in restarting standby boilers like IBH-9, WIL-8 or BHPV Boilers can be started, if required. Scenario-II In case of FCCU-1 (New) CO Boiler CO gas diverted to stack then boiler will be sustained with a load of 35 T/hr with auxiliary firing of AB-1, 2 & 3 FO & FG fires. Diverted CO can be taken back after the fault finding, corrective action and temporary measures taken. In case of delay in taking CO like FCCU-1 failure, then standby boilers like IBH-9, WIL-8 or BHPV Boilers can be started, if required. Scenario-III In case of FCCU-1 (New) CO Boiler trip (Deficit 35 T/hr), steam loads can be balanced in the following way Increase load on IBH-10 boiler. 20 T/hr Increase load on FCCU-2 (Old) CO Boiler. 15T/hr Total 35 T/hr FCCU-1 (New) CO boiler can be taken back in service after the fault finding, corrective action and temporary measures taken. In case of delay in taking boiler online,

breadcrumb: Actual Photos of various components of manway cover of Steam Drum of Old Co Boiler > 8 Start IBH Boiler-9 with normal start up procedure and bring the boiler into service. > 14.2 Normal operation of Boilers: > 14.2.2 OCO Boiler CO gas introduction procedure
text: The following Procedure to be adopted for CO Gas introduction into Old Co Boiler 1. Clearance should be obtained from YSF & Section head for CO gas introduction. 2. Field boiler flue gas path related ducts, pipe line flanges; shut-off valves working condition shall be pre-checked and shall be completed without pending. 3. Important instruments (CO GAS concentration indicator) and CO gas inlet duct 4. Pressure transmitters shall be fixed for continuous controlling and monitoring. 5. Effective Co-ordination with the FCCU-II(R) personnel for smooth introduction. DCS Operational personnel action plan:- 1. CO Boiler combustion of CO GAS temperature shall be maintained at 750 deg C above, to meet complete combustion of CO gas. 2. Slowly bring down the drum level 40% of its normal working level (50%) by reducing set value of LR403, which is in AUTO MODE (Water in drum will expand suddenly & drum level increase). 3. Slowly minimize the Boiler steam load by reduce the fuel gas firing rate by closing the fuel gas control valve (FR426) of both the gas burners to control the steam generation (pressure control). 4. All steam header pressures shall be maintained at low profile, to accommodate sudden increase steam load & avoid popping of any PSVs. 5. Give clearance to FCCU-2 MOI Engineer for operation of FCCU-2 CO Boiler inlet duct pendulum valve towards Boiler. 6. Combustion air control (FRC403) shall be adjusted accordingly, to maintain the oxygen level & temperature
