breadcrumb: Actual Photos of various components of manway cover of Steam Drum of Old Co Boiler > 8 Start IBH Boiler-9 with normal start up procedure and bring the boiler into service. > 14.2 Normal operation of Boilers: > 14.2.3 NCO Boiler CO gas introduction procedure
text:
1) To take CO in to the boiler the following 3 conditions should met.
a. HGV (04-SDV-057) is to be in open position.
b. CO Chamber Temperature is to be maintained more than 800 deg C (TSL-4305) with
the help of support burner.
c. VGV (04-SDV-056) is to be in open position.
2) At any case CO chamber Temperature should not exceed 1050 deg C.

breadcrumb: Actual Photos of various components of manway cover of Steam Drum of Old Co Boiler > 8 Start IBH Boiler-9 with normal start up procedure and bring the boiler into service. > 14.2 Normal operation of Boilers: > 14.2.3 NCO Boiler CO gas introduction procedure
text: 3) CO furnace temperature is to be raised to 1000 deg C. 4) Oxygen analyzer (OAIC-4302) is to be taken on manual mode and combustion air flow to CO burner is to be kept maximum. 5) To compensate the Temperature drop in CO furnace support burner fuel flow to be increased and CO furnace temperature is to be maintained between 850-900 deg C. 6) When CO furnace temperature is steady between 850-900 deg C, CO gas to be introduced to the CO furnace by diverting two port slide valve (TPSV) from Stack to CO furnace. 7) As soon as CO is introduced in to the CO furnace, its temperature may go up to 1000 deg C. Immediately 8) Support burner firing rate is to be reduced to minimum to keep CO furnace temperature in between 850 to 950 deg C. 9) Blow down to be operated to maintain drum level between 55–65 %. 10) CO furnace air flow is to be adjusted to keep Oxygen analyser (OAIC-43020) reading between 1 to 1.5 %. 11) CO analyser (COAI-4302) reading is to be in the range of 0 to 200 PPM. 12) In case CO furnace temperature is high and/or CO reading is high CO furnace outlet O2 (OAIC) set point to be increased further. 13) AB-2 to be kept in service with minimum fuel flows to get the required load. 14) GRE outlet flue gas temperature is to be maintained in

breadcrumb: Actual Photos of various components of manway cover of Steam Drum of Old Co Boiler > 8 Start IBH Boiler-9 with normal start up procedure and bring the boiler into service. > 14.2 Normal operation of Boilers: > 14.2.3 NCO Boiler CO gas introduction procedure
text: is high CO furnace outlet O2 (OAIC) set point to be increased further. 13) AB-2 to be kept in service with minimum fuel flows to get the required load. 14) GRE outlet flue gas temperature is to be maintained in between 180 and 200 deg C to avoid due point corrosion. CO Burner Normal Shut down Procedure: 1) CO gas to be bypassed by diverting the TPSV opening to the stack. 2) CO duct to be purged by opening the air purge line upstream of VGV (04-SDV-056). 3) VGV (04-SDV-056) to be closed with hydraulic motor. 4) Air flow to CO burners to be minimized.

breadcrumb: Actual Photos of various components of manway cover of Steam Drum of Old Co Boiler > 8 Start IBH Boiler-9 with normal start up procedure and bring the boiler into service. > 14.2 Normal operation of Boilers: > 14.2.4 NCO Boiler FO Recirculation

breadcrumb: Actual Photos of various components of manway cover of Steam Drum of Old Co Boiler > 8 Start IBH Boiler-9 with normal start up procedure and bring the boiler into service. > 14.2 Normal operation of Boilers: > 14.2.4 NCO Boiler FO Recirculation
text:
Fuel oil circulation is to be maintained to avoid chocking of fuel oil lines and to have
free flow of fuel oil at various flow rate demands. Keeping fuel oil circulation from tanks
to user points such as burner even when the boiler is not in service is done with a
procedure to avoid the pressure fluctuations in the fuel oil network.
The following procedure to be adopted for establishing FO Recirculation in NCO Boiler
1. MOI SIC will increase the flow of FO through FIC4201 to NCO burners, so
pressure PI4207 at burners will increases.
2. After intimating to MOI slowly open the recirculation block valve at downstream
of FO control valve FIC 4201. By conforming there is no passing of control valve
as well as no pressure drop (PI4207) at burner’s platform.
3. This activity is to be carried out with care as the opening rate of recirculation block
valve will not affect the recirculation flow FI4203 & FO header pressure PI4207 at
burner’s platform.
4. Once recirculation flow is observed (FT 4203), inform to MOI as well as IFO &
wait for few minutes for stability of recirculation flow.
5. Once recirculation established, wait for the stabilization of fuel oil header pressure.
Power Plant-2 Operating Manual Chapter No:14 Rev. No.2 Page 111 of 251

breadcrumb: Actual Photos of various components of manway cover of Steam Drum of Old Co Boiler > 8 Start IBH Boiler-9 with normal start up procedure and bring the boiler into service. > 14.2 Normal operation of Boilers: > 14.2.4 NCO Boiler FO Recirculation
text:
6. Slowly establish the required quantity of FO recirculation with Coordination of
MOI as well as IFO crew.
7. This operation needs Willkie talkie because MOI PA available at north of the
boiler & activity will takes place at south of boiler.
8. Any emergency in MOI, FO recirculation activity should be stopped.
9. Finally inform status of field to MOI.

text: NCO BOILER operation is directly connected to FCCU-1(R) operation as boiler is at the downstream of FCCU-(R) and the operation of Horizontal Goggle valve straight away effect the FCCU-1(R). Specified procedure is to be followed while operating HGV. The following procedure to be adopted for Opening and closing of HGV in NCO Boiler in NCO Boiler HGV Valve Opening: 1. Initiate Push Button on the Hydraulic Control Panel for Starting the hydraulic Power Pack Motor. 2. After the start of the Hydraulic Motor, initiate Push Button “VALVE OPEN” on the Control Panel. {{De-Clamping of the Valve Seat to take place}} {{ De-clamped Limit Switch activation to the Start the Actuator Motor to travel / move the Disc Frame for Opening (i.e from “CLOSE Position” to “OPEN Position”}} {{OPEN LIMIT switch activation to give signal to STOP activator Motor.}} {{Also, the OPEN LIMIT SWITCH activation, to cause Cylinders to CLAMP Position.}} 3. Hydraulic Power Pack Motor to be stopped. Opening of the Horizontal Goggle Valve: 1. De-clamping of the Seat by 8 Power cylinders via Hydraulic Power Pack 2. Travel movement of the Disc Frame – by Electric Actuator & Gear Box & Electric Control Panel. (Manual Mode Clutch Lever Disengages after resuming of the Power Supply.) Clamping of the Seats by Hydraulic Cylinders automatically after disc travel to either OPEN or CLOSE: HGV Valve Closing: 1. Initiate Push Button on the Hydraulic Control Panel for Starting the hydraulic Power Pack

text:
FCCU-I regenerator outlet flue gas is routed to New CO Boiler for Heat transfer to
take place and Boiler O/L sent to FGD-I for further treatment. NCO BOILER
operation is directly connected to FCCU-1(R) operation as boiler is at the
downstream of FCCU-I. At the D/S of TPSV, New CO Boiler CO inlet and outlet
ducts are having google valves for positive isolation. VGV (Vertical goggle valve)
is located on flue gas inlet duct and HGV (Horizontal goggle valve) located on flue
gas outlet duct. Specified procedure is to be followed while operating VGV.

breadcrumb: Actual Photos of various components of manway cover of Steam Drum of Old Co Boiler > 1 VGV Valve Opening: > II. Vertical Goggle Valve opening operation:
text:
1. Select the local in Hydraulic oil motor Control panel
2. Initiate ON Push Button on the Hydraulic Control Panel for Starting
the hydraulic Power Pack Motor.
3. Observe the Field pressure gauge of Hydraulic oil motor to develop
90-105kg/cm2.
4. After the confirm Hydraulic Motor pressure, initiate Push Button
“VALVE OPEN” on the VGV Control Panel.
5. After initiating Push Button “VALVE OPEN” on the Control
Panel.

De-Clamping of the Valve Seat to take place. Observe Glowing bulb
will transfer from Clamp to DE clamp on the VGV Control Panel.

De-clamped Limit Switch activation to the Start the Actuator
Motor to travel / move the Disc Frame for Opening (from
“CLOSE Position” to “OPEN Position”). Observe the actuator
motor start bulb will glow and VGV open starts.
 Once VGV disc touches the OPEN POSITION LIMIT
SWITCH, it will give signal to STOP activator Motor.
 Observe on VGV control panel Actuator motor will cut off with valve
open feedback and actuator motor stop bulb will glow

Also, the OPEN LIMIT SWITCH activation, to cause Cylinders to
CLAMP Position.
6. Clamping of the Seats by Hydraulic Cylinders automatically after disc travel to
either OPEN or CLOSE.
7. Initiate OFF Push Button on the hydraulic Power Pack Motor, observe
hydraulic Oil pressure drop and Glowing bulb of DE clamp will transfer to
Clamp on the VGV Control Panel.

text:
For long retractable soot blower (LRSB) the following items are to be checked
carefully before initial operation of the blower.
 Clutch on power pack shaft should be engaged,
 Lubrication level on power pack
 Chain tension
 Blowing pressure
a)Initial setting
b) Final setting
 SBV Head air valve (Non-return valve) plug is to be removed
 Direction of carriage movement
 Installation of air vents in power pack
 Interference
a) External interference
b)Internal tube (clearance and alignment)
 Packing tightness

breadcrumb: Actual Photos of various components of manway cover of Steam Drum of Old Co Boiler > 1 VGV Valve Opening: > III. Vertical Goggle Valve closing operation: > 14.2.8 Soot blowing of Boilers:
text: Cleaning of tubes external surface is necessary when increase in boiler exit gas temperature, increase in pressure loss across the boiler (assuming constant boiler load) and visual inspection of soot is noticed. It is important to carry out soot blowing when the boiler is stabilized after the initial startup. Because during initial startup frequently the burners are to be adjusted and the fuel firing rate is to be altered. This may result in soot, fuel oil spray deposition and coke formation on the tubes and at burner throat for some time. To avoid furnace explosions or puffs these deposits are to be cleared with the help of soot blowing and burner adjustment. Similarly when a boiler is taking out of service soot blowing is to be carried out to have clean external surfaces of boiler tubes. Care must be taken to avoid extending the lance into the boiler without flow of blowing medium or in subjecting the lance to temperatures in excess of 535°C. If the lance becomes stalled when partially extended, allow blowing to continue in order to cool the lance before withdrawing from the boiler. When first placing the blower in service, it is advisable to extend the lance only a small distance at a time and manually retract it (by means of the local push button). Assure it is in good mechanical condition and the flow of blowing medium is established properly. When the soot blowers are

breadcrumb: Actual Photos of various components of manway cover of Steam Drum of Old Co Boiler > 1 VGV Valve Opening: > III. Vertical Goggle Valve closing operation: > 14.2.8 Soot blowing of Boilers:
text: lance only a small distance at a time and manually retract it (by means of the local push button). Assure it is in good mechanical condition and the flow of blowing medium is established properly. When the soot blowers are not being used, the main blowing medium valve should always be closed tightly. OCO boiler soot blowers operation 1. Prior to starting of soot blowing activity, MOI SE to be informed and clearance to be obtained. 2. Open one/two threads of steam line for complete draining of condensate. 3. Drain the condensate from soot blowers steam line. 4. Open the steam take off valve of soot blowers. 5. Close the drain valve of soot blowers steam line after ensuring total condensate is drained. 6. Operate one by one individual soot blower’s push button as per availability list of soot blowers. 7. 2 nos. of soot blowers are located at each zone. Super heater-1, Super heater-2, evaporator & economizer zone. 8. Soot blowing to be started form Super heater-1, super heater-2 & economizer respectively. NCO boiler soot blowers operation 1. Prior to starting of soot blowing activity, MOI SE to informed and clearance to be taken. 2. Drain the condensate from soot blowers steam line. 3. Open one/two threads of steam line for complete removal of condensate. 4. Close the drain valve of soot blowers steam line. 5. Open the steam valve as per requirement. 6. Soot blowers panel power to

breadcrumb: Actual Photos of various components of manway cover of Steam Drum of Old Co Boiler > 1 VGV Valve Opening: > III. Vertical Goggle Valve closing operation: > 14.2.8 Soot blowing of Boilers:
text: soot blowers steam line. 3. Open one/two threads of steam line for complete removal of condensate. 4. Close the drain valve of soot blowers steam line. 5. Open the steam valve as per requirement. 6. Soot blowers panel power to be put in ON mode. 7. All soot blowers should be in home position. 8. Start soot blowers in auto mode. 9. After completion of soot blowing soot blower power push button to be kept in OFF mode. Inform SE after the activity. Power Plant-2 Operating Manual Chapter No:14 Rev. No.2 Page 115 of 251

breadcrumb: Actual Photos of various components of manway cover of Steam Drum of Old Co Boiler > 1 VGV Valve Opening: > III. Vertical Goggle Valve closing operation: > 14.2.9 Boiler Blow down Operation
text: 14.2.9 Boiler Blow down Operation

breadcrumb: Actual Photos of various components of manway cover of Steam Drum of Old Co Boiler > 1 VGV Valve Opening: > III. Vertical Goggle Valve closing operation: > 14.2.9 Boiler Blow down Operation
text: Boiler blow down is defined as to blow the feed water from the boiler drum whether it is single or bio drum. The purpose of the blow down operation is to removing the dissolved solids deposited in the boiler feed water. This non removal of dissolved solids will develop scale formation inside the boiler tube and lead to slow down the process of heat transfer and tube failure. Existing boilers TDS design limit is < 1500 ppm and regular sample LAB RECEIPT IS 120 ppm to 225 ppm. Centum (MOI) and field co-ordination during boiler blow down 1. Before operating the blow down, boiler drum level set point is to be raised from 50 to 60%.This is to avoid drum level swing while operating blow down whether it is single or bio drum. Drum level will be compensated as feed water controller cascading with feed water controller. 2. Keep the contracts working personnel away from boiler area to avoid falling of hot water from vent line. 3. Cross check the field drum level transmitter with DCS drum level transmitter. 4. Suggest field personnel to open the blow down valve slowly to avoid any drum level fluctuations. 5. Operate the blow down for at least 5 minutes by maintaining drum level above 50%. 6. Hot blow down water should be diluted with service water while discharging in to open sewer. 7. Field operator should watch the drum level field gauge continuously

breadcrumb: Actual Photos of various components of manway cover of Steam Drum of Old Co Boiler > 1 VGV Valve Opening: > III. Vertical Goggle Valve closing operation: > 14.2.9 Boiler Blow down Operation
text: down for at least 5 minutes by maintaining drum level above 50%. 6. Hot blow down water should be diluted with service water while discharging in to open sewer. 7. Field operator should watch the drum level field gauge continuously till the blow down operation is completed. 8. Suggest field personnel to close the blow down after reaching the drum level to 50%. 9. DCS Engineer should check the drum level is being controlled in cascade mode and observe for some time till the drum level is steady. Continuous blow down: 1. Continue to maintain CBD flow to all boilers as per Technical advice. (3% of BFW flow). Adjust the chemical dosing rate according to sample analysis. 2. Depending on the TDS value CBD can be slightly increased OR can be throttled.

breadcrumb: Actual Photos of various components of manway cover of Steam Drum of Old Co Boiler > 1 VGV Valve Opening: > 14.3 Procedure for FCCU-II CO Boilers FD Fan change over

breadcrumb: Actual Photos of various components of manway cover of Steam Drum of Old Co Boiler > 1 VGV Valve Opening: > 14.3 Procedure for FCCU-II CO Boilers FD Fan change over
text:
FD fan changeover - Turbine drive to Motor drive
 Check for the adequate suction damper opening of the FD fan motor (due to common
signal from the MOI; suction damper openings of the Motor & Turbine drive fans are
normally same).
 Ensure the Instrument air supply to FD fan motor discharge damper is closed. The
storage instrument air cylinder (of the motor discharge damper) to be bled until the
pressure is zero.
 Initiate the ‘CO boiler FD fan West start’ Push Button at MOI. This gives ‘start
permissive’ for the FD fan motor. Simultaneous opening of the discharge damper is
prevented due to isolation of instrument air to FD fan motor discharge damper.
 Start the FD motor from the field.
 Resume the instrument air supply to FD fan motor discharge damper so as to open it
with a note on the furnace pressure and temporary load drop due to excess air. Field
toggle switch can be operated for closing or opening of the Motor FD fan discharge
Power Plant-2 Operating Manual Chapter No:14 Rev. No.2 Page 116 of 251

breadcrumb: Actual Photos of various components of manway cover of Steam Drum of Old Co Boiler > 1 VGV Valve Opening: > 14.3 Procedure for FCCU-II CO Boilers FD Fan change over
text: damper either. As a further precaution; the discharge damper can be locked in the field to prevent accidental closure of the discharge damper.  After ensuring satisfactory performance of the FD fan motor; operate the field toggle switch to close the Turbine FD fan discharge damper. Shut off the steam to the FD fan Turbine. And open the Turbine casing drains as applicable. FD fan changeover - Motor drive to Turbine drive  Check for the adequate suction damper opening of the FD fan Turbine( due to common signal from the MOI; suction damper openings of the Motor & Turbine drive are normally same).  CO boiler FD fan Turbine AOP to be started from the field which has to stopped and placed on Auto, only after the MOP of the turbine takes over. FD fan Turbine to be put into normal operation by opening the steam inlet bypass valve.  After ensuring satisfactory performance of the FD fan Turbine; operate the field toggle switch to open the Turbine FD fan discharge damper with a note on the Furnace pressure and temporary load drop due to excess air. As a further precaution; the discharge damper can  In case the CO boiler FD turbine to be started from MOI :  Start the AOP of the FD fan Turbine in the field which has to be stopped and placed on Auto, only after the MOP of the turbine takes over.

breadcrumb: Actual Photos of various components of manway cover of Steam Drum of Old Co Boiler > 1 VGV Valve Opening: > 14.3 Procedure for FCCU-II CO Boilers FD Fan change over
text: CO boiler FD turbine to be started from MOI :  Start the AOP of the FD fan Turbine in the field which has to be stopped and placed on Auto, only after the MOP of the turbine takes over.  Warm up the Turbine.  Close the Instrument Air to FD fan discharge damper.  Initiate the ‘CO boiler FD fan East start’ PB at MOI.  FD fan turbine steam I/L shut off valve (if functioning) opens wide. Simultaneous opening of the FD fan turbine discharge damper is prevented due to isolation of instrument air to FD fan Turbine discharge damper.  After FD fan turbine comes into satisfactory rotation; restore air supply to FD fan turbine discharge damper so as to open it. Field toggle switch in the field can be operated for closing or opening of the Turbine FD fan discharge damper either.  Close the FD fan motor discharge damper switch (with a note on the Combustion air pressure) by operating the field toggle switch.  Press the stop Push button in the field for stopping the FD fan Motor and release the Push button to make it stand by.

breadcrumb: Actual Photos of various components of manway cover of Steam Drum of Old Co Boiler > 1 VGV Valve Opening: > 14.4 Procedure for FCCU-I CO Boilers FD Fan change over
text: 14.4 Procedure for FCCU-I CO Boilers FD Fan change over

breadcrumb: Actual Photos of various components of manway cover of Steam Drum of Old Co Boiler > 1 VGV Valve Opening: > 14.4 Procedure for FCCU-I CO Boilers FD Fan change over
text:
The following instructions need to be followed for the smooth operation of New CO
Boiler FD Fan auto cut-in change over facility.
 Ensure the following before starting the FD Fan drive.
 FD Fan Turbine inlet VHP steam and outlet LP steam headers are condensate free
before starting FD Fan Turbine.
 FD Fan motor electrical power is available and load clearance is taken from CPP
before starting the FD Fan Motor.
 “HGV Open” indication is ON at MOI.
Power Plant-2 Operating Manual Chapter No:14 Rev. No.2 Page 117 of 251

breadcrumb: Actual Photos of various components of manway cover of Steam Drum of Old Co Boiler > 1 VGV Valve Opening: > 14.4 Procedure for FCCU-I CO Boilers FD Fan change over
text:  Keep the Drive Selector Switch on Neutral mode (middle), on Motor field ON/OFF board.  During normal operation of FD Fan, one drive is in service and the other is in auto mode. Keep the Turbine always in service and ensure that VHP steam passing through the steam SDV. Ensure that FD Fan discharge damper is Open. Turbine ON indication will appear on Turbine ON/OFF board.  Keeping the selector switch towards Turbine (South side) indicates that Motor is in auto mode. Keeping the selector switch towards Motor (North side) indicates that Turbines is in Auto mode. After the Shell PFI implementation, by default Turbine should be in service and Motor should be in auto. Ensure that the selector switch is on Turbine side when Turbine is in service.  In auto mode, both FD Fan turbine and Motor suction dampers will operate on auto from MOI with an equal amount of opening.  FD Fan Motor or Turbine discharge damper will open on its own after auto cut-in taken place. Discharge damper of Motor or Turbine open command should be given from field BMS panel for initial startup.  In case of auto cut-in, FD Fan turbine or Motor discharge damper will open on its own for the standby auto cut-in drive after auto cut-in takes place and discharge damper will close on its own for the tripped drive. Suction damper of tripped drive to be made zero

breadcrumb: Actual Photos of various components of manway cover of Steam Drum of Old Co Boiler > 1 VGV Valve Opening: > 14.4 Procedure for FCCU-I CO Boilers FD Fan change over
text: Motor discharge damper will open on its own for the standby auto cut-in drive after auto cut-in takes place and discharge damper will close on its own for the tripped drive. Suction damper of tripped drive to be made zero after taking it to manual mode at MOI for restarting.  Keep the FD Fan Turbine rolling rpm as 250 to 400 when Motor drive is in service. Ensure that Turbine start permissive and Turbine OFF indications are present on Turbine ON/OFF board. OFF press button should be released on Turbine ON/OFF board.  Start the FD Fan Turbine as follows:  Ensure FD Fan Turbine inlet VHP steam and outlet LP steam headers are condensate free before starting FD Fan Turbine.  Ensure HGV Open (ZSH 4310) is ON at MOI.  Keep the Drive Selector Switch on Neutral mode (middle), on Motor field ON/OFF board.  Keep the suction damper is about 10% open at MOI.  Keep the FD Fan Turbine drive on rolling around 400 rpm.  Press “Turbine on” button on Turbine ON/OFF board.  Steam SDV (SOV 4301) will open and turbine rpm will reach to 1425 rpm (Normal).  Ensure that FD Fan turbine discharge damper will open on its own after SDV is open.  Ensure the turbine lube oil pressure is normal (0.6 kg/cm2 to 0.8 kg/cm2) and BCW cooling water inlet and outlet lines are open.  Combustion air pressures

breadcrumb: Actual Photos of various components of manway cover of Steam Drum of Old Co Boiler > 1 VGV Valve Opening: > 14.4 Procedure for FCCU-I CO Boilers FD Fan change over
text: turbine discharge damper will open on its own after SDV is open.  Ensure the turbine lube oil pressure is normal (0.6 kg/cm2 to 0.8 kg/cm2) and BCW cooling water inlet and outlet lines are open.  Combustion air pressures will be adjusted by operating suction damper at MOI.  FD Fan Motor start up procedure:  Ensure that FD Fan motor electrical power is available and take load clearance from CPP before starting the FD Fan Motor.

breadcrumb: Actual Photos of various components of manway cover of Steam Drum of Old Co Boiler > 1 VGV Valve Opening: > 14.4 Procedure for FCCU-I CO Boilers FD Fan change over
text: Power Plant-2 Operating Manual Chapter No:14 Rev. No.2 Page 118 of 251

breadcrumb: Actual Photos of various components of manway cover of Steam Drum of Old Co Boiler > 1 VGV Valve Opening: > 14.4 Procedure for FCCU-I CO Boilers FD Fan change over
text:  Ensure HGV Open (ZSH 4301) is ON at MOI.  Keep the Drive Selector Switch on Neutral mode (middle), on Motor field ON/OFF board.  Keep the suction damper about 10% open at MOI.  Start the FD Fan Motor from motor ON/OFF board.  Open the Motor discharge damper from the field BMS panel.  Check the motor ammeter reading, which should be between 25 to 30 Amp.  Combustion air pressures will be adjusted by operating suction damper at MOI. FD Fan Turbine to Motor auto cut in procedure:  Always keep the FD Fan turbine in service and keep motor on auto mode as per shell PFI recommendations.  Ensure that “Turbine start permissive” & Turbine off” are healthy on turbine ON/OFF board, and off push button is released.  Keeping the selector switch towards Turbine (South side) indicates Motor in auto mode.  Ensures FD Fan motor and turbine suction dampers are in auto mode at MOI.  If FD Fan Turbine trips, Motor will start in auto mode and motor discharge damper will open on its own.  FD Fan turbine discharge damper will close on its own after SDV (SOV 4301) gets closed.  Isolate the upstream and downstream steam inlet SDV isolation valves.  Suction damper of FD Fan turbine will made zero at MOI. FD Fan Motor to Turbine auto cut in procedure:  Ensure that motor is in-service and discharge

breadcrumb: Actual Photos of various components of manway cover of Steam Drum of Old Co Boiler > 1 VGV Valve Opening: > 14.4 Procedure for FCCU-I CO Boilers FD Fan change over
text:  Isolate the upstream and downstream steam inlet SDV isolation valves.  Suction damper of FD Fan turbine will made zero at MOI. FD Fan Motor to Turbine auto cut in procedure:  Ensure that motor is in-service and discharge damper is open.  Keep the turbine under rolling through steam SDV (SOV4301).  Keep the selector switch towards Motor (North side), indicates Turbine in auto mode.  Ensure that “Turbine start permissive” & “Turbine off” are healthy on turbine ON/OFF board, and off push button is released.  Ensure FD Fan motor and turbine suction dampers are in auto mode at MOI.  If FD Fan motor trips, FD Fan turbine SDV (SOV 4301) will open in auto and discharge damper will also open at the same time.  Take the suction damper into manual mode and close the motor suction damper and ensure the turbine running is smooth.  Combustion air pressures will be adjusted by operating suction damper at MOI.

text: 14.5 Procedure to operate Boilers at Turndown Capacity in Power Plant

breadcrumb: Actual Photos of various components of manway cover of Steam Drum of Old Co Boiler > 1 VGV Valve Opening: > 14.5 Procedure to operate Boilers at Turndown Capacity in Power Plant
text:
This procedure is to Operate Power Plant Utility and CO Boilers steam loads at turndown
load to avoid low steam generation and oil dripping in the furnace due to low oil pressure
when the boilers run at low steam loads. This PSI gives the information about the turn
down load of each boiler below which boiler should not be operated.

breadcrumb: Actual Photos of various components of manway cover of Steam Drum of Old Co Boiler > 1 VGV Valve Opening: > 14.5 Procedure to operate Boilers at Turndown Capacity in Power Plant
text: Power Plant-2 Operating Manual Chapter No:14 Rev. No.2 Page 119 of 251

breadcrumb: Actual Photos of various components of manway cover of Steam Drum of Old Co Boiler > 1 VGV Valve Opening: > 14.5 Procedure to operate Boilers at Turndown Capacity in Power Plant
text:
Power Plant Boilers steam load will be maintained according the steam demand based on
the process thru put. Normally both new and Old FCCU-I & II CO Boilers will be in
service on CO mode as Waste Heat Boiler and there will not be any firing except FCCU-
1 CO Boiler support burner. FCCU-I CO Boiler-9 or 10 will be in service at minimum
steam load around 30 TPH.
Safety Considerations:
a. When boilers are operated at less than turn down capacity, uneven distribution of
flue gas and steam takes place due to low flue gas and steam velocities.
b. Non uniform flow inside the super heater tubes may even cause stagnation of fluid.
c. Mal-distribution of gas flow outside the tubes can lead to overheating of some tubes
compared to others.
Boiler wise turn down steam loads are given below:

text:
a. Maintain the Boilers steam loads above the turndown capacity.
b. Stop the Boiler if the Boiler 3zs steam load is below the turndown capacity.
c. Keep close watch in furnace when the boiler is running at turndown steam load and
maintain 2kg/cm2 difference between burner fuel oil and atomizing steam.

text: 14.6 Procedure for minimizing PRDS opening (PRDS Operation)

breadcrumb: Actual Photos of various components of manway cover of Steam Drum of Old Co Boiler > 1 VGV Valve Opening: > 14.6 Procedure for minimizing PRDS opening (PRDS Operation)
text:
Need of minimising PRDS opening
This procedure is to minimize opening of pressure reducing stations control valves
(PRDS) for avoiding direct loss of enthalpy due to pressure reduction.
Various steam pressure levels (being used in VR) are mentioned below
a. VHP Steam (36 kg/cm2 & 360 Deg C)
VHP Steam header pressure is being maintained by utility boilers (IBH-9&10,
FCCU-1 CO Boiler & FCCU-2 CO Boiler) and steam generator in CCR.
b. HP steam (28kg/cm2 & 285 Deg C)
HP steam header pressure is being maintained by FCCU-II CO Boiler. There is no
HP steam consumption except for FCCU-II CO Boiler FD Fan and BFW turbines.
Balance is being reduced to MP steam through PRDS. These boilers are used only
when main boilers / HRSGs are shutdown.
c. MP steam (10.5kg/cm2 & 250 Deg C)
MP steam header is being maintained by HRSGs, process MP steam generators in
CDU-I, CDU-II, CDU-III, VBU, FCCU-I, FCCU-II & DHDS-SRU turbines exhausts
and VHP-MP & HP-MP PRDS.
d. LP steam (3.5kg/cm2 & 155 Deg C)
Power Plant-2 Operating Manual Chapter No:14 Rev. No.2 Page 120 of 251

breadcrumb: Actual Photos of various components of manway cover of Steam Drum of Old Co Boiler > 1 VGV Valve Opening: > 14.6 Procedure for minimizing PRDS opening (PRDS Operation)
text:
LP steam header pressure is being maintained by process LP steam generators in
VBU, BBU and DHDS-SRU. LP steam consumption is much higher than LP steam
generation. So LP steam header has to be maintained only through reducing turbines;
and PRDS.
Different PRDS in the Refinery are given below

1. SPI 301 PP-2 VHP-MP (VREP-I) 47.5
2. SPI 302 PP-2 MP-LP (VREP-I) 38
3. SPI 2301 PP-2 VHP-MP (VREP-II) 45
4. SPI 2303 PP-2 MP-LP (VREP-II) 30
5. SPI 303 PP-2 VHP-HP 22.5
6. SPI 300 PP-2 HP-MP 22.5
7. SPI 1001 PP-2 DHDS VHP-MP 14
8. 24 PR 061 PP-2 HP-MP 30
9. 24 PR 062 PP-2 MP-VLP 15
10. PIC 1319 PP-2 VHP-MP 150
11. PIC 1332 PP-2 MP-LP 90.6
12. PIC 7301 NHT CCR MP-LP 41.7

PIC 7301 (VRCFP MP-LP PRDS in CCR) can be closed by running pumps (that are
having turbine and motor drive both) on turbine drive in place of motor drive.
b) Due to NIL consumption of HP steam, opening of SPI 300 and 24PR061 are
inevitable when FCCU-II CO Boiler is in service.
c) MP-LP PRDS in PRU, Merox ARU and DHDS ARU are used to meet process
requirement of respective units. So, these PRDS cannot be closed.

breadcrumb: Actual Photos of various components of manway cover of Steam Drum of Old Co Boiler > 1 VGV Valve Opening: > 14.6 Procedure for minimizing PRDS opening (PRDS Operation)
text:
Guidelines for Minimising PRDS opening is as follows:
The following actions to be taken for closing PRDS
a. For closing VHP-MP PRDS
1. VHP to MP PRDS opening can be minimised by ensuring continuous service of
VHP BFW turbine pumps in PP-II and IBH system.
b. For closing MP-LP PRDS
1. PRDS opening can be minimised by running pumps (mentioned in table: 3) on
turbine drive (instead of running the pump on motor drive) until PRDS is
completely closed. The order mentioned in table: 3 can be followed for running
turbine drive.
c. For handling excess MP steam header pressure
1. Excess MP Steam (in case all six GTG/HRSG are in service) can be controlled
by stopping VHP to LP turbines. FCCU-1 CO / IBH-10 boiler FD fan turbine
and MP BFW turbine can be stopped and motor drive can be started.
2. In case of high MP header pressure and VHP-MP PRDS are completely closed,
PP-I/IBH VHP BFW turbines can be stopped (motor drive to be started).
The following actions to be taken for closing PRDS while different units are shut
down:

breadcrumb: Actual Photos of various components of manway cover of Steam Drum of Old Co Boiler > 1 VGV Valve Opening: > 14.6 Procedure for minimizing PRDS opening (PRDS Operation)

text: When CDU-I is Shut down Reduction in MP generation = 6T/hr Reduction in MP consumption = 11T/hr Net Reduction in MP steam consumption = 5T/hr Reduction in LP steam consumption = 2T/hr Action required: a) Excess MP to LP steam is to be adjusted through option of PRDS resulting in reduced opening of both the reducing stations. VHP-MP PRDS will be closed corresponding to 5T/hr of VHP-MP conversion (10%) b) If VHP to MP PRDS is completely closed, then MP steam header pressure can be controlled by running VHP-MP turbines (VHP BFW turbines in PP-2/II) on motor drive (turbine drives to be stopped). When CDU-II is Shut down Reduction in VHP Steam flow to turbine = 12T/hr (equivalent LP Steam generation will reduce) Reduction in MP generation = 7T/hr Reduction in MP consumption = 11T/hr Net Reduction in MP steam consumption = 4T/hr Reduction in LP steam consumption = 2T/hr Action required: a) Excess MP to LP steam is to be adjusted through option of PRDS resulting in reduced opening of both the reducing stations. b) During CDU-II Shut down (due to non-availability of PFD turbine), MP-LP PRDS opening should be minimised by running tempered water turbine of MP flushing oil turbine in CDU-III. When CDU-III is Shut down Reduction in MP generation = 4T/hr Reduction in MP consumption = 24T/hr Net Reduction in MP steam consumption = 20T/hr Reduction in LP steam consumption = 5T/hr Action required: a) Excess

When CDU-III is Shut down Reduction in MP generation = 4T/hr Reduction in MP consumption = 24T/hr Net Reduction in MP steam consumption = 20T/hr Reduction in LP steam consumption = 5T/hr Action required: a) Excess MP to LP steam is to be adjusted through operation of PRDS resulting in reduced opening of both the reducing stations.MP-LP PRDS opening will reduce by ~20% while VHP-MP PRDS opening will reduce by ~45%. b) If VHP to MP PRDS is completely closed, then MP steam header pressure can be controlled by running VHP-MP turbines (VHP BFW turbines in PP-2/II) on motor drive (turbine drives to be stopped). When FCCU-I is Shut down Reduction in VHP Steam consumption = 15T/hr Reduction in LP steam consumption = 4T/hr

When PRU is Shut down Reduction in MP Steam consumption = 10T/hr No effect in demand/consumption of VHP and LP Steam. Action required: a) Reduce the PRDS opening preferably VREP-II VHP to MP reducing station, which in turn will reduce the load on utilities boilers. b) If VHP to MP PRDS is completely closed, then MP steam header pressure can be controlled by running VHP-MP turbines (VHP BFW turbines in PP-2/II) on motor drive (turbine drives to be stopped). When VBU is Shut down Reduction in MP Steam generation = 5T/hr Reduction in MP Steam consumption = 6T/hr Net increase in MP Steam = 1T/hr Reduction in LP Steam generation = 7T/hr Reduction in LP Steam consumption = 2T/hr Net shortage in LP steam = 5T/hr Action required: a) Reduce VREP-II VHP to MP reducing station thus adjusting excess MP steam. b) LP steam requirement can be met by running flushing oil pump (in CDU-III) on turbine. c) MP BFW pump in PP-2 and boilers FD fan turbines (OCO, FCCU-1 CO & IBH- 9&10) can be kept in service for meeting LP steam requirement. When HRSG-I or II trips, i.e, non-availability of 20 T/hr of MP steam Reduction in MP Steam generation = 20T/hr No effect

When HRSG-I or II trips, i.e, non-availability of 20 T/hr of MP steam Reduction in MP Steam generation = 20T/hr No effect on demand / consumption of VHP and LP steam. Action required: a) Increase load on VHP steam generating boilers (FCCU-1 CO Boiler, FCCU-2 CO Boiler, FCCU-I CO Boilers). b) Ensue availability of VHP BFW pump turbine in IBH & PP-II and run the pump on turbine. When HRSG-III,IV,V or VI trips, i.e, non-availability of 50 T/hr of MP steam Reduction in MP Steam generation = 50T/hr No effect on demand / consumption of VHP and LP steam. Action required: a) Non-availability of HRSG-III/IV/V/VI will require start-up of FCCU-II CO Boiler. HP steam produced can be reduced to MP steam to meet requirement. b) MP to LP steam consumption is to be reduced wherever possible. Power Plant-2 Operating Manual Chapter No:14 Rev. No.2 Page 125 of 251

on turbine.

breadcrumb: Actual Photos of various components of manway cover of Steam Drum of Old Co Boiler > 1 VGV Valve Opening: > 14.6 Procedure for minimizing PRDS opening (PRDS Operation) > Note: 1) Stoppage of pressure reducing turbines will not give any relief in steam load.
text:
Note: 1) Stoppage of pressure reducing turbines will not give any relief in steam load.
2) Pressure reducing turbines are to be stopped only when VHP-MP and MP-LP
PRDS are completely closed.
3) During excess MP header pressure, stoppage of VHP-LP turbine will help in
reducing MP header pressure.

breadcrumb: Actual Photos of various components of manway cover of Steam Drum of Old Co Boiler > 1 VGV Valve Opening: > 14.7 PRDS changeover from IBH VHP-MP to VREP-II VHP-MP
text: 14.7 PRDS changeover from IBH VHP-MP to VREP-II VHP-MP

breadcrumb: Actual Photos of various components of manway cover of Steam Drum of Old Co Boiler > 1 VGV Valve Opening: > 14.7 PRDS changeover from IBH VHP-MP to VREP-II VHP-MP > Note: 1) Stoppage of pressure reducing turbines will not give any relief in steam load.
text:
To charge PRDS and change over from the PIC 1319 (IBH VHP TO MP) to VREP-II
VHP to MP steam SPC -2301.
Procedure for PRDS changeover from pic 1319 (IBH TO MP) to VREP_II VHP TO
MP:
1. Check the PRDS instrument connections, air leaks if any in the field before making
it ready for charging.
2. Check the control valve upstream and downstream block valves should be in closed
position and stroke check the control valve for correct operation.
3. Initially open the reducing station control valve SPC 2301 VHP TO MP steam
reducing station for 5% which we are going to take into service and observe the rise
in MP steam header pressure. Simultaneously close the PRDS PIC1319 for 5%.To
balance MP Steam header pressure at 10.5kg/cm2 gradually close the PIC 1319
control valve and leave the reducing station control vale SPC 2301 at certain opening
position.
4. BEWARE of control valve operating position Please LOOK UP AND DOWN
whether it is 0 to 100%open OR reverse operation from 100% to 0% Open.
5. After confirming the MP steam header pressure and pressure reducing station
performance which we have taken in to service, then isolate the control valve which
is taken out of service.

breadcrumb: Actual Photos of various components of manway cover of Steam Drum of Old Co Boiler > 1 VGV Valve Opening: > 14.8 Normal Operation of WHRS
text: 14.8 Normal Operation of WHRS

breadcrumb: Actual Photos of various components of manway cover of Steam Drum of Old Co Boiler > 1 VGV Valve Opening: > 14.8 Normal Operation of WHRS > Note: 1) Stoppage of pressure reducing turbines will not give any relief in steam load.
text:
The objective of this Operating manual is to provide procedure for commissioning &
normal Operation of new WHRS (Waste Heat Recovery Steam Generator) between Old
CO Boiler and FGD-II to enable recovery of heat from hot flue gases at Old CO Boiler
outlet to FGD-II.
BACKGROUND:
a. Hot flue gases from FCCU-II are routed to Old CO Boiler through orifice chamber for
steam generation and hot flue gases, downstream of the Old CO Boiler at 320 DegC,
are routed to FGD-II for SPM & Sox removal.
b. It is observed that 6.0tph pf LP steam generated by recovering the heat from outlet
flue gases of Old CO Boiler.
c. Hence, it is proposed to provide WHRS (Waste Heat Recovery Steam Generator)
between Old CO Boiler and FGD-II to generate steam recovery heat from hot flue
gases. In this process, flue gas temperature at inlet of FGD-II will be reduced from
320 Deg C to 200 Deg C.
SCOPE:
a. This procedure is applicable for commissioning and normal operation of WHRS
installed at the outlet of OCO Boiler.
Power Plant-2 Operating Manual Chapter No:14 Rev. No.2 Page 126 of 251

breadcrumb: Actual Photos of various components of manway cover of Steam Drum of Old Co Boiler > 1 VGV Valve Opening: > 14.8 Normal Operation of WHRS
text: b. There is no bypass facility for the Waste Heat Recovery System. c. WHRS is designed for dry run conditions. d. OCO boiler flue gas outlet will be routed to new WHRS instead of FGD-II. WHRS outlet will be routed to FGD-II for treatment. e. Sulphur dew point was calculated as 175 deg C for the design composition of the flue gas. Therefore, in order to avoid acid dew point corrosion, the evaporator coil operating temperature has to be at least 10 deg C above Sulphur dew point. f. Accordingly, it was envisaged that the boiler is to be operated at 10.5 kg/cm2g and saturation temperature of 185 deg C. The same is to be let down to 3.0/3.5 kg/cm2g using a Pressure Control Valve. g. LP steam generated from the system is routed to LP steam header at the OCO boiler FD fan turbine discharge. h. HP BFW for the new system is taken from OCO boiler HP BFW provision at the upstream of BFW control valve. i. All other utilities requirement for the new system are taken from FCCU-II battery limit. WHRS Design Parameters a. Steam drum outlet pressure: 12.5 Kg/cm2 (g) b. Steam drum outlet steam temperature: 185 Deg. C c. Boiler Capacity : 6.1 TPH d. Chemical Boil out Procedure: e. Ensure that DM water/BFW connection is made to the boiler and necessary chemicals are available at site. f. Following chemicals are required for the chemical

breadcrumb: Actual Photos of various components of manway cover of Steam Drum of Old Co Boiler > 1 VGV Valve Opening: > 14.8 Normal Operation of WHRS
text: 185 Deg. C c. Boiler Capacity : 6.1 TPH d. Chemical Boil out Procedure: e. Ensure that DM water/BFW connection is made to the boiler and necessary chemicals are available at site. f. Following chemicals are required for the chemical boil out (as per boiler supplier): g. Ion Exchange make ‘Indion – 1462’ h. Ion Exchange make ‘Indion – 2564’ i. Remove one of the safety valves on the steam drum and make arrangements for injecting the chemical through the safety valve nozzle. j. Take fresh water (preferably DM water) in the boiler up to 100 mm above the normal operating water level of the steam drum. k. Take sample of this water and check pH, hardness and TDS of the same. l. Add Indion – 1462 chemical into the steam drum through the provision made. After chemical loading, nozzle to be blinded. m. Start the boiler by operating the guillotine dampers on WHRS inlet duct and OCO Boiler outlet to stack ducts such that the flue gas is partially routed to WHRS. Please ensure that the pressure of the boiler is maintained at 2.0 kg/cm2g n. Run the boiler on 2 kg/cm2g for 16 to 18 hours. o. During this period, check pH and TDS of the boiler water after every 4 hours. p. After 16 to 18 hours, close the guillotine damper towards WHRS and route the flue gas from OCO Boiler to stack. q. Drain this boiler

breadcrumb: Actual Photos of various components of manway cover of Steam Drum of Old Co Boiler > 1 VGV Valve Opening: > 14.8 Normal Operation of WHRS
text: this period, check pH and TDS of the boiler water after every 4 hours. p. After 16 to 18 hours, close the guillotine damper towards WHRS and route the flue gas from OCO Boiler to stack. q. Drain this boiler water slowly. Take fresh water two times and flush the boiler. Power Plant-2 Operating Manual Chapter No:14 Rev. No.2 Page 127 of 251

breadcrumb: Actual Photos of various components of manway cover of Steam Drum of Old Co Boiler > 1 VGV Valve Opening: > 14.8 Normal Operation of WHRS
text: r. Repeat the procedure with second chemical also. s. Please note that during this operation, as the flue is partially being routed to WHRS, FGD will be in operation. t. Approximately, 72 m 3 of water is required for this process. WHRS Commissioning Procedure a. Once the chemical boil out process of the boiler is completed, steam blow out to be carried out to remove any unwanted particles in the steam line. b. Fix the PSVs back to steam drum (that was taken out for facilitating chemical boil out). c. Install all Instrumentation control valves, pressure gauges, temperature guages and other instruments. d. Slowly take HP BFW into the system. HP BFW pressure will be reduced to 18 kg/cm2g from 55 kg/cm 2 g using PRV located at the battery limit. e. Ensure that the BFW is filled in the economizer tubes and steam drum is filled up to 50% level. f. Open steam drum vent valve and start up vent valve before introducing flue gas into the system. This is to remove any entrained air pockets from the system. g. Slowly introduce flue gas into the system by operating / controlling Old CO Boiler to stack damper as WHRS inlet damper was stuck up in open position. h. As hot flue gases are introduced into the system, BFW will heat up and steam will be generated, pressurizing the system. i. Close the steam drum vent valve as the pressure

breadcrumb: Actual Photos of various components of manway cover of Steam Drum of Old Co Boiler > 1 VGV Valve Opening: > 14.8 Normal Operation of WHRS
text: inlet damper was stuck up in open position. h. As hot flue gases are introduced into the system, BFW will heat up and steam will be generated, pressurizing the system. i. Close the steam drum vent valve as the pressure reaches 2.0 kg/cm 2 g. j. Close start up vent valve as the pressure reaches 5-6 kg/cm 2 g. k. Pressurize the system slowly till 11.0 kg/cm2g (normal operating pressure of steam drum) in 8 hours from introduction of flue gases. l. Please ensure that the steam drum level is maintained continuously at 50% level during this process. m. Steam generated is to be routed to LP steam header after reducing the pressure using PCV (from 11.0 kg/cm 2 g to 3.5 kg/cm 2 g). n. Soot blowers to be operated once in every 12 hours. o. Blow down to be maintained at 3% of total steam draw rate.

b. It must be ensured that the gauge glasses and other instruments are in good working

c. It must be ensured that all the dampers are in a good working order and their actual
position is indicated correctly.
WHRS system is equipped with the following facilities (P&IDs are attached for ready
reference):
i. 1.5” HP Boiler Feed Water line with PRV, pressure indicator, flow meter, PSV,
pressure gauge, flow control valve and temperature indicator.

Steam drum is provided with demister pad, pressure safety valves with silencers, temperature & pressure indicators, two numbers of level transmitter and gauges (each) and pressure gauge. iv. ¾” chemical injection facility exists from OCO Boiler to WHRS steam drum. v. 1” CBD facility with flow indicator. breadcrumb: Actual Photos of various components of manway cover of Steam Drum of Old Co Boiler > 1 VGV Valve Opening: > 14.9 Normal Operation of BCW Systems:
text: 14.9 Normal Operation of BCW Systems:

breadcrumb: Actual Photos of various components of manway cover of Steam Drum of Old Co Boiler > 1 VGV Valve Opening: > 14.9 Normal Operation of BCW Systems:
text:
During the normal operation of cooling water system. The following routine checks
should be made:
 Keep watch on the bearing cooling water system pressure at the discharge of
cooling water pumps. If the pressure falls below 4.0 kg/em2g set pressure alarm will
come in the DCS control room.
 Check that the cooling water pumps are running satisfactorily.
 Also check from the process control rooms that the consumer are not using cooling
water at more than their design rates.
 Keep Watch on the operation of the Cooling water pumps. Regularly check the
lubrication. Gland cooling water. Motor ampere. Vibration or noise on these
machines.
 Maintain a Watch on the water level in the cooling tower stump. If low-level alarm
sounds then find out the reasons at the earliest and take necessary action.
 Regularly Check temperature of supply & return headers. The pitch of the fans can
also be adjusted (after stopping the fan) to control water temperature.

breadcrumb: Actual Photos of various components of manway cover of Steam Drum of Old Co Boiler > 1 VGV Valve Opening: > 14.9 Normal Operation of BCW Systems:
text: Power Plant-2 Operating Manual Chapter No:14 Rev. No.2 Page 129 of 251

breadcrumb: Actual Photos of various components of manway cover of Steam Drum of Old Co Boiler > 1 VGV Valve Opening: > 14.9 Normal Operation of BCW Systems:
text:
 Keep a Watch on the operation of cooling tower fans. Check the lubrication.
Bearing temperature and any vibration in the fans.
 The cooling water parameters should be checked regularly through lab result.
Inhibitor dosage in the first start-up will have to be higher than normal to allow for
layer to be formed on pipeline.
 Cooling tower blow down should be operated basing on lab result.
 Check the suspend solids content of return water from side stream filter, Back flush
the filters as per vendor’s requirement & when suspended solid level goes above
maximum acceptable value. The flow through the filter should be kept minimum
commensurate with suspended solid contents of circulating water.

breadcrumb: Actual Photos of various components of manway cover of Steam Drum of Old Co Boiler > 1 VGV Valve Opening: > 14.9 Normal Operation of BCW Systems: > 14.9.1 VREP-I BCW System Operation Procedure
text: 14.9.1 VREP-I BCW System Operation Procedure

breadcrumb: Actual Photos of various components of manway cover of Steam Drum of Old Co Boiler > 1 VGV Valve Opening: > 14.9 Normal Operation of BCW Systems: > 14.9.1 VREP-I BCW System Operation Procedure
text: Bearing cooling water requirement is met by four pumps 170-PM-5A/B/C and 170/24- PM-2G2. 170-PM-5A/C are of capacity 350 m 3 /hr at 6 kg/cm 2 pressure and 170/24- PM-2G2 is of capacity 97 m 3 /hr at 6.5 kg/cm 2 pressure take suction from the sump and discharge into a 8 inch supply header. This header supplies to all the units. The 8 inch supply header is provided with a pressure gauge, Temperature Indicator, Pressure transmitter and flow transmitter. Also a low pressure alarm provided, which sounds at MOI PP panel when low pressure activates. There is one more pump 170-PM-5B is available of capacity 275 m3/hr at 3.5 kg/cm2 pressure. This pump cannot be put in service due to low discharge pressure (shut off pressure 4.5 kg/cm2). Normally two pumps one of 170-PM-5A/C with combination of 17024-PM-2G2 will be in service and the second one is available as standby. The following is the VREP-1 BCW requirement. Unit BCW Consumption (M 3 /Hr) Crude Distillation Unit-2 42.2 Vacuum Distillation Unit-2 23.6 Bitumen Blowing Unit 19.0 Fluidised Catalytic Cracking Unit-2 16.9 Merox 25.4 Air Compressor 50.0 Power Plant-2 20.0 Off-site pumps/turbines 14.0 PRU & SRU 4.5 Total Consumption 215.6 BCW after serving its purpose, return to cooling tower through a 8 inch header. One 6 inch line from Merox joins to 8 inch header near the battery limit. One more 6 inch return header from CDU-2 joined to the return header

breadcrumb: Actual Photos of various components of manway cover of Steam Drum of Old Co Boiler > 1 VGV Valve Opening: > 14.9 Normal Operation of BCW Systems: > 14.9.1 VREP-I BCW System Operation Procedure
text: serving its purpose, return to cooling tower through a 8 inch header. One 6 inch line from Merox joins to 8 inch header near the battery limit. One more 6 inch return header from CDU-2 joined to the return header near the tower. A 2 inch tapping provided in return header for blow down. The return header provided with a sample point and temperature indicator. The hot water from various units is cooled in the induced draft cross flow tower. The tower is designed for a maximum circulating rate 250m 3 /hr. It has two cells each of capacity 125 m 3 /hr. The hot water from return header distributed to both the shells either Power Plant-2 Operating Manual Chapter No:14 Rev. No.2 Page 130 of 251

breadcrumb: Actual Photos of various components of manway cover of Steam Drum of Old Co Boiler > 1 VGV Valve Opening: > 14.9 Normal Operation of BCW Systems: > 14.9.1 VREP-I BCW System Operation Procedure
text: side. The distribution of water can be adjusted by the help of butterfly valves (4nos). The water then distributed through distributing nozzles and falls across the path of air flow. Packings are provided inside the tower for braking of water particles and increase the contact time with air for greater cooling. The cooled water is then collected in cooling tower basin. The basin has two compartments each receiving water from respective shells. Water from these compartments flows via two channels to BCW sump. A screen is installed at the inlet of suction of pump for preventing entry of foreign materials to pump. The suction sump is having over flow and draining facility. The sump is provided with high and low level alarms, which sound at MOI PP panel. The sump also provided with a level controller, which controls makeup water flow depending on level. To supplement the loss of cooling water due to leak, blowdown, evaporation and drift through cooling tower, raw water is supplied through 2 inch line by raw water pumps 170-PM-02A/B/C/D/E situated at DM-2. Water flow is controlled by control valve, which get input from sump level controller. By-pass facility of control valve is also available. In case of failure of make-up water supply, raw water which is stored for CRP filter back wash in the Back wash raw water tank can be routed. Also DM water can be used for makeup through filter back wash lines.

text: 14.9.2 VREP-I BCW Pump Change Over Procedure

breadcrumb: Actual Photos of various components of manway cover of Steam Drum of Old Co Boiler > 1 VGV Valve Opening: > 14.9 Normal Operation of BCW Systems: > 14.9.2 VREP-I BCW Pump Change Over Procedure
text:
To charge over VREP-I BCW Pumps without interrupting the BCW Supply to Process Units.
The following Procedure to be adopted for VREP-I BCW Pump Change Over:
1. Inform to RSM and concerned units about BCW pump change over.
2. Ensure that a suction and discharge valves of the pump which is going to be kept in
service is in open condition and open the pump bleeder to release the air lock if any
and close the bleeder, inform to DCS in charge.
3. Note the BCW supply header pressure prior to the changeover.
4. Close the pressure switch take off valve then drain the pressure switch point on
BCW supply header.
5. Stand by pump which is in auto mode gets started automatically sensing the BCW
pressure drop to 4.0 kg/cm 2 .
6. Open the pressure switch take off valve and close the pressure switch drain valve on
BCW supply header.
7. Note down the BCW supply header pressure after auto cut in by stand by pump, this
pressure should be more than the pressure noted prior to the start up the stand by
pump.
8. Stop the pump which is in service earlier not and ensure the pump rotating in
reverse direction.
9. Again observe the discharge pressure of the pump that is kept in service.

VREP-2 Bearing cooling water requirement is met by two pumps 170-PM-9A/B. 170-
PM-9A/B of capacity 400 m3/hr at 5.5 kg/cm2 pressure take suction from the sump and
discharge into a 12 inch supply header. From the supply header one 3 inch tap-off is taken
for FCCU feed pump and Recirculating cooling water tower. Another 10 inch tap-off
taken for Merox unit and air compressor. There is a 6 inch tie-in between VREP-1 and
VREP-2 BCW supply at Merox unit. After the take of for Merox block the 12 inch header

text: reduced to 10 inch and supply water to CDU-3. From this 10 inch header one 2 inch tap- off is taken to cater to VBU feed pump near DM plant. The supply header is provided with a pressure gauge, Temperature Indicator, Pressure transmitter and flow transmitter. Also a low pressure alarm provided, which sounds at MOI PP panel when low pressure activates. Auto cut-in facility of spare pump is also available. At 4.5kg/cm2 auto cut-in of spare pumps takes place. Unit BCW Consumption (M3/Hr) Crude Distillation Unit-3 155.0 Vacuum Distillation Unit-3 Vis Breaker Unit 5.5 Salt water cooling tower Merox / ATU/SWS 37.0 Air Compressor-VREP-2 130.0 VBU feed pumps 7.0 HVGO (FCCU feed) pumps Total Consumption 334.5 m 3 /hr BCW returns form all consumer units as same size of supply headers are connected to a 12 inch header which feeds warm water to cooling tower top. One 3 inch tapping provided in return header for Side stream filter 170-X-09 of capacity 8 m3/hr. It filters suspended solids 60 ppm at inlet to 10 ppm at outlet. Feed line to filter is provided with a flow indicator. One DP indicator is provided across the filter bed. Max allowable DP is 0.5 kg/cm2. After filtration, this water returns to cooling water sump. Back flushing facility is provided for the Side stream filter. This is to be back washed once in a day or if DP across the filter crosses 0.5 kg/cm2

This is to be back washed once in a day or if DP across the filter crosses 0.5 kg/cm2 whichever comes earlier. The return header provided with a pressure gauge, Temperature gauge and temperature indicator. One 8 inch tapping provided from return header for blow down. Blow down water is routed to surface drain. The hot water from the various units is cooled in the induced draft cross flow tower. The tower is designed for a maximum circulating rate 400m3/hr. It has two cells each of capacity 200 m3/hr. The hot water from return header distributed to both shells on either side. The distribution of water can be adjusted by help of butterfly valves on individual distribution line. The water then distributed through distributing nozzles and fall across the path of air flow. Packing are provided inside the tower for braking of water particle and increase the contact time with air for greater cooling. The cooled water then collected in cooling tower basin. The basin has two compartments each receiving water from respective shell. Water from these compartment flows via two channels to BCW sump. A double screen is installed at the channel near inlet of sump for preventing entry of foreign materials to the pump suction. The basins are provided with isolating gate. Each basin is provided with

breadcrumb: Actual Photos of various components of manway cover of Steam Drum of Old Co Boiler > 10 Keep the stand by pump in auto mode and running pump in manual mode and > 14.9.4 VREP-II BCW Pump Change Over Procedure
text: Bearing cooling water supply to rotary equipment of the refinery is continuous in nature and has to be maintained without any disturbance. To get Reliability and continuous performance of pumps periodical change over as well as PM checks are required. The following procedure to adopted while changing the pump 1. Inform to RSM and concerned units about BCW pump changeover. 2. Ensure that a suction and discharge valves of the pump which is going to be kept in service is in open condition, inform to DCS in charge. 3. Note the BCW supply header pressure prior to the changeover. 4. Close the pressure switch take off valve then drain the pressure switch point on BCW supply header. 5. Stand by pump which is in auto mode gets started automatically sensing the BCW pressure drop to 4.5 kg/cm 2 . 6. Open the pressure switch take off valve and close the pressure switch drain valve on BCW supply header. 7. Note down the BCW supply header pressure after auto cut in of stand by pump, this pressure should be more than the pressure noted prior to the start up the stand by pump. 8. Stop the pump which is in service earlier and ensure the pump not rotating in reverse direction. 9. Again observe the discharge pressure of the pump that is kept in service. 10. Ask DCS in-charge to keep the stand by pump in auto mode and running pump

breadcrumb: Actual Photos of various components of manway cover of Steam Drum of Old Co Boiler > 10 Keep the stand by pump in auto mode and running pump in manual mode and > 14.10 Normal Operation of PA System
text:
HPCL-VR Power and utilities require maximum safety levels due to the chemical
processing industry. Reliable voice communication as well as clear full-coverage
announcements and warnings in potentially explosive environments are the critical
factors here for man, machinery and the environment.
Industronic PA System using in this area and total no. of available PAs are 27 available.
PA operation will do in following cases are
1. Any communication from field to DCS
2. Any communication from DCS to field.
To Operate PA precautions to be followed to maintain quality of Communication
between DCS and Field

breadcrumb: Actual Photos of various components of manway cover of Steam Drum of Old Co Boiler > 10 Keep the stand by pump in auto mode and running pump in manual mode and > 14.10 Normal Operation of PA System
text:
1. PA system has base unit in DCS and Outdoor inter come station in field and both
have microphone and loudspeaker
2. To communicate any information from DCS to field, DCS operator use the base
unit of particular earmark outdoor inter com station on base unit available in DCS
area
3. To communicate any information from field to DCS, Field crew use the outdoor
inter come station of PA system available at location, Power & utilities available
outdoor inter come stations are 27 for field communication to DCS. DCS Base
unit has membrane keypad to communicate field locations

breadcrumb: Actual Photos of various components of manway cover of Steam Drum of Old Co Boiler > 10 Keep the stand by pump in auto mode and running pump in manual mode and > 14.10 Normal Operation of PA System

text:
4. Rocker switch available in field out door inter come station for communicate any
information to DCS.
5. Rocker switch is generally in horizontal position by default.
6. Field crew pressing of Rocker switch of outdoor inter come station, DCS operator
will get the LED bulb of Base unit will glow at particular location and receive the
voice through speaker.
7. DCS crew pressing of base unit membrane key board particular switch of
outdoor inter come station, Field crew will get the LED bulb will glow at
particular location outdoor inter com station and receive the voice through
speaker.
8. Base unit connected to 27 locations. Therefore, any PA usage from base unit is
alert the other outdoor inter com stations with LED bulb glow in all outdoor inter
com stations.
9. LED bulb of outdoor inter com station glow means DCS operator is already busy
with other Outdoor inter com station crew. So field crew should wait up to
completion of other outdoor inter com conservation means LED bulb off. Then
start to communicate.
10. By mistake two or more outdoor inter com stations are operating in same time
due to emergency DCS operator will not get the clear voice from anyone. So
before operating rocker switch LED bulb alertness should be confirm.

breadcrumb: Actual Photos of various components of manway cover of Steam Drum of Old Co Boiler > VOLUME 119 M 3 > 16.2 Operating Procedures of Critical Equipment: > 16.2.1 Operating Procedures for Centrifugal Pumps:

breadcrumb: Actual Photos of various components of manway cover of Steam Drum of Old Co Boiler > VOLUME 119 M 3 > 16.2 Operating Procedures of Critical Equipment: > 16.2.1 Operating Procedures for Centrifugal Pumps:
text:
Sl. Activity Procedure
1. Start-up i. Inspect and see if all the mechanical jobs are completed.
ii. Establish cooling water flow where there is such provision.
Also open steam for seal quenching in pumps having such
facilities.
iii. Check oil level in the bearing housing, flushing may be
necessary if oil is dirty or contains some foreign material.
iv. Rotate the shaft by hand to ensure that it is free and coupling is
secure. Coupling guard should be in position and secured
properly.
v. Open suction valve. Ensure that the casing is full of liquid.
Bleed, if necessary, from the bleeder valve.
vi. Energize the motor. Start the pump and check the direction of
rotation. Rectify the direction of rotation if it is not right.
vii. Check the discharge pressure. Bleed if necessary to avoid vapor
locking.
viii. Open the discharge valve slowly. Keep watch on the current
drawn by the motor, if ammeter is provided. In other cases
Power Plant-2 Operating Manual Chapter No:16 Rev. No.2 Page 149 of 251

breadcrumb: Actual Photos of various components of manway cover of Steam Drum of Old Co Boiler > VOLUME 119 M 3 > 16.2 Operating Procedures of Critical Equipment: > 16.2.1 Operating Procedures for Centrifugal Pumps:
text: Sl. Activity Procedure check at motor control Centre. In some pumps a by-pass has been provided across the check valve and discharge valve to keep the idle pump hot. In such pumps, the by-pass valve should be closed before starting the pump. It should be ensured that casing of these pumps are heated up sufficiently prior to starting of the pump to guard against damage of the equipment and associated piping due to thermal shock. 2. Shutdown a. Close discharge valve fully. b. Stop the pump 3. Keeping If pump is going to remain as standby and has provision for keeping pump the pump hot, proceed as follows: standby. o Open the valve in the by-pass line across the discharge valve and check valve. o The circulation rate should not be so high to cause reverse rotation of idle pump and also overloading of the running pump. 4. Releasing to If pump is to be prepared for maintenance, proceed as follows: maintenance o Close suction and discharge valves. . o Close valve on check valve by-pass line, if provided. o Close cooling water to bearing, if provided. Also shut off steam for seal quenching, if provided. o Slowly open pump bleeder and drain liquid from pump if the liquid is very hot allow sufficient time before draining is started. Ensure that there is no pressure in the pump. Also drain pump casing. o Blind suction and discharge and check valve

breadcrumb: Actual Photos of various components of manway cover of Steam Drum of Old Co Boiler > VOLUME 119 M 3 > 16.2 Operating Procedures of Critical Equipment: > 16.2.1 Operating Procedures for Centrifugal Pumps:
text: bleeder and drain liquid from pump if the liquid is very hot allow sufficient time before draining is started. Ensure that there is no pressure in the pump. Also drain pump casing. o Blind suction and discharge and check valve by-pass line and flare connection if any. o Cut-off electrical supply to pump motor prior to handling over for maintenance.

breadcrumb: Actual Photos of various components of manway cover of Steam Drum of Old Co Boiler > VOLUME 119 M 3 > 16.2 Operating Procedures of Critical Equipment: > 16.2.2 Operating Procedures for FD fans:

breadcrumb: Actual Photos of various components of manway cover of Steam Drum of Old Co Boiler > VOLUME 119 M 3 > 16.2 Operating Procedures of Critical Equipment: > 16.2.2 Operating Procedures for FD fans:
text:
Operation of the centrifugal fan should follow the equipment manufacturers
recommended procedures. Verify that the lubrication and cooling water systems are
clean and free of contamination and that all control systems function properly. Verify
that the shutdown system of the blower/fan is according to design. Verify that the
blower/fan rotate freely.
Sl. Activity Procedure
1. Start-up /  Open and suction and discharge dampers.
Normal  Check the lubricating oil & grease system on all bearings.
operation  Log all temperatures, pressures, levels, flows.

Adjust cooling water flows to compensate for changes in inlet water
temperature or ambient temperature.

Start the Blower drive.

Listen for any unusual noise while the machine is operating. These
should be investigated immediately
2. Shutdown a. Stop the blower drive.
b. Start the oil pump to lubricate the bearing till required.
c. Close the discharge valve.
