breadcrumb: Actual Photos of various components of manway cover of Steam Drum of Old Co Boiler > VOLUME 119 M 3 > 16.3 Operating Procedures of WHRS:
text: 16.3 Operating Procedures of WHRS:

breadcrumb: Actual Photos of various components of manway cover of Steam Drum of Old Co Boiler > VOLUME 119 M 3 > 16.3 Operating Procedures of WHRS: > 16.3.1 PRELIMINARY CHECK
text: 16.3.1 PRELIMINARY CHECK

breadcrumb: Actual Photos of various components of manway cover of Steam Drum of Old Co Boiler > VOLUME 119 M 3 > 16.3 Operating Procedures of WHRS: > 16.3.1 PRELIMINARY CHECK
text:
Before commencing pressure increase make sure that every auxiliary / equipment is
in a satisfactory working order and all checks have been made. 100 mm water must
be indicated in the water gauge glasses. The water level must be checked by opening
and closing the drain valves. When the water moves slowly in the glasses, check why
this is so. All blow down and drain valves must be closed.

breadcrumb: Actual Photos of various components of manway cover of Steam Drum of Old Co Boiler > VOLUME 119 M 3 > 16.3 Operating Procedures of WHRS: > 16.3.2 EXPANSION OF PRESSURE PARTS
text: 16.3.2 EXPANSION OF PRESSURE PARTS

breadcrumb: Actual Photos of various components of manway cover of Steam Drum of Old Co Boiler > VOLUME 119 M 3 > 16.3 Operating Procedures of WHRS: > 16.3.2 EXPANSION OF PRESSURE PARTS
text:
Make sure that all suspension ties are stressed and that the weight is uniformly
distributed over all suspension points for top supported units.
All tube walls must be gas tight, and the interspaces between the boiler headers and
tubes must, where necessary, be sealed with non-asbestos rope.
Prior to each startup of suspended boilers it is necessary to check whether or not there
is any foreign matter in the part expanding downward. Serious complications may
result from obstructions to expansion.
Similarly, for bottom supported boilers, free expansion to be allowed for steam drum
& top headers. When the boiler is generating at full capacity & pressure the actual
vertical and lateral expansions should be recorded and compared with anticipated
expansion diagram enclosed. This could be done by marking cold & hot position with
marker on a near stationary structural member. For anticipated expansion refer to
fixture holder drawing.
After modifications and repairs have been made the engineer in-charge should be
responsible for checking, prior to re-commissioning, if sufficient margins are given
for expansion.
At intervals of 1 to 2 months it is necessary to check the extent of the expansions
under equal operating conditions. Modifications must be communicated to the station
superintendent.

breadcrumb: Actual Photos of various components of manway cover of Steam Drum of Old Co Boiler > VOLUME 119 M 3 > 16.3 Operating Procedures of WHRS: > 16.3.3 PRECAUTIONS PRIOR TO INLET OF CO BOILER GAS
text: 16.3.3 PRECAUTIONS PRIOR TO INLET OF CO BOILER GAS

breadcrumb: Actual Photos of various components of manway cover of Steam Drum of Old Co Boiler > VOLUME 119 M 3 > 16.3 Operating Procedures of WHRS: > 16.3.3 PRECAUTIONS PRIOR TO INLET OF CO BOILER GAS
text:
Any scaffolding timber must be removed from the Boiler and from the gas passes,
especially where the gas temperature reaches the ignition temperature of the timber.
As a matter of fact, glowing ash could cause the ignition of highly explosive gases
obtained by combustion with low firing rate during boil out.
The pipe line connections must be made so that all vent and drain valves can be
Power Plant-2 Operating Manual Chapter No:16 Rev. No.2 Page 151 of 251

breadcrumb: Actual Photos of various components of manway cover of Steam Drum of Old Co Boiler > VOLUME 119 M 3 > 16.3 Operating Procedures of WHRS: > 16.3.3 PRECAUTIONS PRIOR TO INLET OF CO BOILER GAS
text: operated efficiently without any risk for the erection or operating personnel. It must be ensured that the gauge glasses and other instruments indicating the water level are in good working order. It must be safeguarded that all dampers are in a good working order and have been mounted in such a way that their actual position is indicated correctly. Make sure that the unit is filled with water and chemical agents in accordance with the recommended practice. When the water gauge glass is dirty, close the water shut-off cock and open the steam shut-off cock. By opening the drain valve for a short moment it is possible to purge the gauge glass. As soon as the indicating instrument is clean, the drain valve must be closed and the water shut-off cock must be opened. With the rising boiler pressure observe the movement of the pressure gauge to make sure that the instrument functions correctly. Carefully check the manhole and hand- hole cover joints. In case of necessity repair them. Check the whole boiler for gas and steam leakages. On the second day slowly increase the pressure 30 per cent of what it is in normal operation; maintain this pressure during the whole day while testing the mountings, such as water level indicators, remote water level indicators etc., with steam, preparing them for operation and then closing and draining them. After the pressure had been lowered again during the night, it

breadcrumb: Actual Photos of various components of manway cover of Steam Drum of Old Co Boiler > VOLUME 119 M 3 > 16.3 Operating Procedures of WHRS: > 16.3.3 PRECAUTIONS PRIOR TO INLET OF CO BOILER GAS
text: the whole day while testing the mountings, such as water level indicators, remote water level indicators etc., with steam, preparing them for operation and then closing and draining them. After the pressure had been lowered again during the night, it shall be brought, during the third day, to the boil out pressure which is 50 per cent of the normal working pressure; this pressure shall be maintained during the whole day in order to complete boil out. After boil out it is advisable to purge the all lines in order to make sure that no foreign matter is carried along the steam line when starting up the plant. As soon as the boiler is cold, open the vents and drains in order to empty the boiler and the Economizer. Any sludge accumulated in the drum and headers must be removed. Thereupon close all drains and fill the boiler with clean water. When water is showing in the water gauges, continue the filling process slowly until drum is completely filled. Then empty the boiler. Before enter gases removed all foreign material inside the boiler. After cleaning inlet damper open partially.

breadcrumb: Actual Photos of various components of manway cover of Steam Drum of Old Co Boiler > VOLUME 119 M 3 > 16.3 Operating Procedures of WHRS: > 16.3.4 WATER LEVEL INDICATORS
text: 16.3.4 WATER LEVEL INDICATORS

breadcrumb: Actual Photos of various components of manway cover of Steam Drum of Old Co Boiler > VOLUME 119 M 3 > 16.3 Operating Procedures of WHRS: > 16.3.4 WATER LEVEL INDICATORS
text:
Check the water gauges in accordance with the following method.
1) Partially open the discharge valve for half a minute so that glass and instrument
slowly reach the maximum temperature; then open the discharge valve wide.
When the discharge valve is being opened quickly, the glass may break.
2) Check the steam connection by closing the water valve so that steam will leave
through gauge glass and drain.
3) Open the steam valve.
4) Check the water connection by closing the steam valve and opening the water
valve. Close the water valve again when water is flowing.
5) Open the steam valve.
6) Close the drain valve when the water returns immediately to its correct level in the
Power Plant-2 Operating Manual Chapter No:16 Rev. No.2 Page 152 of 251

breadcrumb: Actual Photos of various components of manway cover of Steam Drum of Old Co Boiler > VOLUME 119 M 3 > 16.3 Operating Procedures of WHRS: > 16.3.4 WATER LEVEL INDICATORS
text: gauge glass. When the water moves slowly, this indicates that the steam or water connection is partially obstructed and that the water level indicator does not function properly. The service engineer must convince himself that each boiler operator knows how to carry out the water level check. 7) Contamination of the water gauge glasses is possible during boil out. Before commissioning boiler, it is necessary to dismantle and clean these glasses; the mica on flat glasses must be replaced. Then the responsibility for feeding can be taken over by the boiler operator. This precautionary measure is particularly necessary when starting up new plants, because the operators are generally not yet familiar with the plant and other duties deserving their attention. As soon as the water level is falling, feed a continuous water flow into the boiler. Avoid feed interruption, which will cause unwanted temperature changes in the Economiser. Check the high and low water alarm by raising and lowering the boiler water level. Note the water level at which the alarm is given. After the boiler has been fed for five minutes, close the Economiser bypass valve, if existing. A new unit should be allowed to steam at low load for some time in order to check the reliability and to give the operator the opportunity of becoming acquainted with the checks. Use should be made of this trial period for checking if all instruments and the complete equipment of

breadcrumb: Actual Photos of various components of manway cover of Steam Drum of Old Co Boiler > VOLUME 119 M 3 > 16.3 Operating Procedures of WHRS: > 16.3.4 WATER LEVEL INDICATORS
text: for some time in order to check the reliability and to give the operator the opportunity of becoming acquainted with the checks. Use should be made of this trial period for checking if all instruments and the complete equipment of the boiler plant are in a satisfactory working order. When the boiler has taken up steam generation, rules should be established for the operating conditions existing; such rules can then be used as a guide for safe and economic operation. Temperature and draught shall be measured exactly during a period of some hours to check if they correspond to the values indicated so that changes are generally not necessary. Minor differences are of little importance. When the draught loss in the boiler increases considerably, inspect the heating surfaces occasionally in order to find and remove deposits, if any, in the flue gas passes. It is advisable to commence keeping records about fuel consumption, steam quantity, flue gas analyses, gas temperatures, draught, fuel analyses, operating hours, average data etc. as soon as possible, because such records give an idea of measures which are valuable for finding and eliminating faults.

breadcrumb: Actual Photos of various components of manway cover of Steam Drum of Old Co Boiler > VOLUME 119 M 3 > 17.3 Avoiding Deviations & Plant Upsets:
text: 17.3 Avoiding Deviations & Plant Upsets:

breadcrumb: Actual Photos of various components of manway cover of Steam Drum of Old Co Boiler > VOLUME 119 M 3 > 17.3 Avoiding Deviations & Plant Upsets:
text:
To avoid Deviations and Plant upsets precautionary steps to be taken to the extent
possible. These steps may include

Routine bi-hourly field rounds.

100% compliance of DRJ.

Releasing equipment to PM checks.

Releasing equipment to PET RP.

Tracking the system through DSI Reports.
17.3.1Routine bi-hourly field rounds:
Employee has to take a routine bi-hourly field round and observe in a detailed way
about the condition of each equipment. Readings are to be noted in Turnover book. If
any of the reading is abnormal then it is to be highlighted immediately for corrective
action. Besides reading also observe the physical condition related to performance and
safety. Eg lube oil dripping from soot blowers, poor housekeeping etc.
17.3.2100% compliance of DRJ:
Daily Routine Jobs (DRJs) related to operation are to be followed with 100%
compliance as applicable to shift, day, week and month. Deviations in DRJs are to be
addressed immediately for system improvement.

breadcrumb: Actual Photos of various components of manway cover of Steam Drum of Old Co Boiler > VOLUME 119 M 3 > 17.3 Avoiding Deviations & Plant Upsets: > 17.3.3 Releasing equipment to PM checks:
text: 17.3.3 Releasing equipment to PM checks:

breadcrumb: Actual Photos of various components of manway cover of Steam Drum of Old Co Boiler > VOLUME 119 M 3 > 17.3 Avoiding Deviations & Plant Upsets: > 17.3.3 Releasing equipment to PM checks:
text:
Release of Rotary and Electrical equipment is to be followed as per PM check schedule.
The hidden problems in equipment will be highlighted and corrected through this
Preventive Maintenance schedule concept. Otherwise it may lead to damage of
equipment and at times loss of production also. Deviations in PM checks are to be
addressed and tracked for system improvement.

breadcrumb: Actual Photos of various components of manway cover of Steam Drum of Old Co Boiler > SCHEDULE (Monthly, Quarterly & Yearly). Deviations recorded in DSI Reports are to be > 18.1 Automatic Emergency Shutdowns:
text:
Emergencies that occur in power plant units must be recognized and acted upon
immediately. The operators and supervisory personnel should carefully study in advance,
and become thoroughly familiar with, the proper steps to be taken in such situations.
Some of the emergency conditions described here will not only resulting in unit shut
down, but also unsafe handling can lead to serious damage to the equipment and
personnel.
In general, the objective of the emergency procedures is to avoid damage to the
equipment and personnel.
In addition, specific rules cannot be made to cover all situations that might arise.
Therefore, the following procedures cover only those emergency situations that are the
most common and, for the most part, only the immediate steps to be taken are listed. It
should be noted that the procedures outlined here concentrate on the power plant section.

breadcrumb: Actual Photos of various components of manway cover of Steam Drum of Old Co Boiler > SCHEDULE (Monthly, Quarterly & Yearly). Deviations recorded in DSI Reports are to be > 18.2 Emergency Shutdown Procedures:

breadcrumb: Actual Photos of various components of manway cover of Steam Drum of Old Co Boiler > SCHEDULE (Monthly, Quarterly & Yearly). Deviations recorded in DSI Reports are to be > 18.2 Emergency Shutdown Procedures:
text:
In case of emergency, immediate shut down is required. Certain emergencies are
identified and the procedures are explained as follows.
1. FCCU-II CO Boiler Trip
2. FCCU-I CO Boiler Trip
3. Electrical power failure
4. Boiler tube leak

breadcrumb: Actual Photos of various components of manway cover of Steam Drum of Old Co Boiler > SCHEDULE (Monthly, Quarterly & Yearly). Deviations recorded in DSI Reports are to be > 18.2 Emergency Shutdown Procedures: > 18.2.1 Emergency shutdown procedure when FCCU-II CO Boiler trip:
text:
1. Press emergency trip PB.
2. Immediately close oil valve.
3. Immediately close gas valve.
4. Immediately divert CO gas to stack.
5. Scavenge the Burner, Manually if possible.
6. If you want to put in service in a short time then purge the Boiler for few minutes and
start again.

text:
Possible Causes
1. Control failure
2. Boiler Feed Pump failure
3. Tube leak
4. Operator's error

breadcrumb: Actual Photos of various components of manway cover of Steam Drum of Old Co Boiler > SCHEDULE (Monthly, Quarterly & Yearly). Deviations recorded in DSI Reports are to be > 18.3 EMERGENCY HANDLING PROCEDURE > 18.3.1 Low Water Level
text: Action to be taken 1. Compare indication, if the water level falls out of sight in the water gauge, due to failure of the feed water supply or neglect of the operator, except in cases of momentary fluctuations that might occur with extra ordinary changes in load, appropriate action should be taken at once to trip the fuel. Any decision to continue to operate, even if only for a short time at a reduced rating, would have to be made by someone in authority who is thoroughly familiar with the circumstances that led to the emergency and positively certain that the water level can be restored immediately without damaging the boiler. In the absence of such a decision: 2. Gas inlet damper closed immediately 3. Open all inspection and access door. 4. Shut off all steam discharged from the unit (trip turbine, steam driven auxiliaries, etc.) open start up vent valve. 5. Simultaneously, if feed water has become available and the operator is assured that no pressure parts have been damaged, gradually reduce (never increase) the flow of feed water to the boiler by manual regulation. The purpose of handling the feed in this manner is to avoid quenching pressure parts with relatively cold water by raising the water level and on the other hand to avoid exposing additional metal. The feed water regulating valve should be closed completely when all steam flow from the unit has ceased. 6. Maintain

text: parts with relatively cold water by raising the water level and on the other hand to avoid exposing additional metal. The feed water regulating valve should be closed completely when all steam flow from the unit has ceased. 6. Maintain a high air flow at first to hasten the cooling process. 7. If pressure parts damage is suspected, reduce the steam pressure gradually by opening Power Plant-2 Operating Manual Chapter No:19 Rev. No.2 Page 166 of 251

boiler cools reduce the air flow. Shut down the damper as soon as the unit is cool enough
for a man to enter. Drain the boiler when the drum metal temperature is 200 0 F following

breadcrumb: Actual Photos of various components of manway cover of Steam Drum of Old Co Boiler > SCHEDULE (Monthly, Quarterly & Yearly). Deviations recorded in DSI Reports are to be > WATER IMPINGEMENT MAY CAUSE TURBINE DAMAGE
text:
Possible Causes
1. Feed water control malfunction.
2. Operator's error.
Action to be taken
Abnormally high water level should be avoided as it may lead to carryover and even
priming. If the water level rises above the recommended normal operating range proceed
as follows:
1. Reduce the water level immediately by opening the intermittent blow down valves.
2. Reduce the steam rate, if necessary, and place feed water control on Manual & reduce
water flow rate.
3. If priming should occur, as indicated by rapid fluctuations in outlet steam temperature,
proceed as follows:
4. Reduce the steaming rate.
5. If the water level is abnormally high, reduce the level by opening the intermittent blow
down valves and place feed water control on manual.
6. Investigate the water condition (alkalinity and solids) and other contaminants.
7. Investigate the condition of the drum internals as soon as an opportunity is afforded.

breadcrumb: Actual Photos of various components of manway cover of Steam Drum of Old Co Boiler > SCHEDULE (Monthly, Quarterly & Yearly). Deviations recorded in DSI Reports are to be > WATER IMPINGEMENT MAY CAUSE TURBINE DAMAGE > 18.3.3 TUBE LEAKS INDICATION OF TUBE LEAKS
text:
Indication of Tube leaks
1. Feed water flow greater than steam flow.
2. Hissing noise from inside boiler.
3. Wetted insulation of water running down casing with no apparent cause.
4. Excessive make-up.
Action to be taken
1. Water wall tube leaks
1. The action taken is dependent on size and location of tube leak. A large leak may
extinguish fire or cause loss of drum level. A medium size leak may force an immediate
shut down. A small leak may allow continued operation after considering its
consequences on continued operation.
2. If at all possible, locate the leak.
3. In the event of failure of one or more water carrying tubes, the best method of shutting
down the unit will be dictated by the size of the failure, the ability to maintain normal
water level and the demands for the service of the unit.
4. The following instructions regarding tube failures are of a general nature. It must be
understood that conditions may arise which will require exercise of judgement by the
Power Plant-2 Operating Manual Chapter No:19 Rev. No.2 Page 167 of 251

breadcrumb: Actual Photos of various components of manway cover of Steam Drum of Old Co Boiler > SCHEDULE (Monthly, Quarterly & Yearly). Deviations recorded in DSI Reports are to be > WATER IMPINGEMENT MAY CAUSE TURBINE DAMAGE > 18.3.3 TUBE LEAKS INDICATION OF TUBE LEAKS
text:
operators.
5. In case of a leak or tube failure, which does not involve a serious drain on the feed
water supply, the water level should be maintained, and the unit taken out of service in
the normal manner. Refer to normal shutdown procedures.
6. If the tube failure results in a loss of water so great that the water level cannot be
maintained with the feed water supply available, use the method outlined below :
 Close immediately inlet Flue gas
damper.
 Shut off feed water to the boiler.
 Open all inspection and accesses door.
7. After the unit has cooled enough to permit a man to enter it, make a thorough inspection
of pressure parts of any indication of damage resulting from loss of water level. After
the necessary repairs have been made, apply a hydrostatic test and obtain the approval
of the proper authorities before putting the unit back in service.

breadcrumb: Actual Photos of various components of manway cover of Steam Drum of Old Co Boiler > SCHEDULE (Monthly, Quarterly & Yearly). Deviations recorded in DSI Reports are to be > WATER IMPINGEMENT MAY CAUSE TURBINE DAMAGE > 18.3.4 Economizer Tubes and Evaporator tube
text:
a) An economizer or evaporator tube leak can be detected by sound and/or increased make
up water requirements. The leak should be investigated at the earliest possible time and
unit shutdown in a normal manner.
b) Water leaks in the economizer or evaporator can cause considerable erosion damage to
adjacent tubes. Water carried over from an economiser tube leak may cause plugging of
hoppers. Any decision to continue operation with known leaks should be made with this
in mind.

breadcrumb: Actual Photos of various components of manway cover of Steam Drum of Old Co Boiler > SCHEDULE (Monthly, Quarterly & Yearly). Deviations recorded in DSI Reports are to be > WATER IMPINGEMENT MAY CAUSE TURBINE DAMAGE > 18.3.5 Safety Precautions to be followed in any Boiler

breadcrumb: Actual Photos of various components of manway cover of Steam Drum of Old Co Boiler > SCHEDULE (Monthly, Quarterly & Yearly). Deviations recorded in DSI Reports are to be > WATER IMPINGEMENT MAY CAUSE TURBINE DAMAGE > 18.3.5 Safety Precautions to be followed in any Boiler
text: The handling and burning of any fuel is potentially hazardous. Some fuels ignite more readily than others. Safe handling and operation demands knowledge of the characteristics of the fuel and careful observance of necessary precautions. 1) The raising and lowering of steam parameters should be restricted to the value given in the starting diagram. Exceeding these values will result in reduced fatigue life of pressure part. 2) In the case of tube failure which can be identified by hearing the noise in the boiler gallery and cross checked by difference in steam and water flow, gas and steam temperature, the boiler should be shut-down at the earliest by regular procedure for maintenance work, otherwise large number of tubes may fail due to steam erosion and impingement. 3) Boiler washing when the unit is shutdown can be done effectively by using hot water. Immediately after washing, the surfaces should be dried by firing boiler at low rate. 4) Always use deaerated water for boiler feeding. Feed water temperature must also be maintained at the highest level possible either with the help of feed water heaters or heating the water in the feed tank. 5) Low feed water temperature obtained during low load operation will result in external corrosion of economizer. 6) Carryover of salts in steam occurs either due to mechanical or vapor carry over. Efficient drum internals can only reduce mechanical carry over. Silica is always carried over in vapor

breadcrumb: Actual Photos of various components of manway cover of Steam Drum of Old Co Boiler > SCHEDULE (Monthly, Quarterly & Yearly). Deviations recorded in DSI Reports are to be > WATER IMPINGEMENT MAY CAUSE TURBINE DAMAGE > 18.3.5 Safety Precautions to be followed in any Boiler
text: load operation will result in external corrosion of economizer. 6) Carryover of salts in steam occurs either due to mechanical or vapor carry over. Efficient drum internals can only reduce mechanical carry over. Silica is always carried over in vapor form. Continuous monitoring of sodium & silica in steam is desirable. Power Plant-2 Operating Manual Chapter No:19 Rev. No.2 Page 168 of 251

breadcrumb: Actual Photos of various components of manway cover of Steam Drum of Old Co Boiler > SCHEDULE (Monthly, Quarterly & Yearly). Deviations recorded in DSI Reports are to be > WATER IMPINGEMENT MAY CAUSE TURBINE DAMAGE > 18.3.5 Safety Precautions to be followed in any Boiler
text:
7) Before operating the boiler, ensure complete knowledge of water chemistry.
8) The steam drum should not normally be filled beyond the point when water is
showing in the bottom of the gauge glass. This is to allow for the swell on heating
and to reduce any blowing down from this cause to a minimum.
9) Raising pressure on boilers, a technique of continuous firing should be developed if
possible, since intermittent firing may result in re condensation in the tube loops.
10) The blow down or drain valves which allow the hot water or steam to flow under
pressure in a relatively cold line shall be opened slowly. For low pressures &
temperatures relatively less time is required. When the water level cannot be
observed by the person operating the blow down valves, a helper shall be stationed
so that he can observe the water level & can simultaneously give the instructions for
opening or closing the blow down valves in accordance with the requirements.

breadcrumb: Actual Photos of various components of manway cover of Steam Drum of Old Co Boiler > SCHEDULE (Monthly, Quarterly & Yearly). Deviations recorded in DSI Reports are to be > 18.4 Re-Startup after Emergency Shutdowns:
text:
During emergency some equipment will trip in auto mode for safety reason. Some of the
equipment have to be shutdown manually when condition is beyond control and
normalcy is not coming immediately. In such situations careful coordination from field
to MOI and to other units and YSF is essential. There may be many examples of
emergency. Few of them are

Boiler trip

BCW ID Pump or Fan trip

PRDS controller failure
After rectification of emergency problem the affected equipment can be put back in
service as per standard procedure.
Shift Event Report (SER) is to be filled for the emergency faced. Lessons learnt are to be
captured for system improvement.

breadcrumb: Actual Photos of various components of manway cover of Steam Drum of Old Co Boiler > SCHEDULE (Monthly, Quarterly & Yearly). Deviations recorded in DSI Reports are to be > 19.1 Preparations and readiness for planned shutdown:
text: 19.1 Preparations and readiness for planned shutdown:

breadcrumb: Actual Photos of various components of manway cover of Steam Drum of Old Co Boiler > SCHEDULE (Monthly, Quarterly & Yearly). Deviations recorded in DSI Reports are to be > 19.1 Preparations and readiness for planned shutdown:
text:
If shutdown is necessary due to failure of feed water supply, stop firing as quickly as
possible. Open super heater drains and shut off main valve. If shutdown is happened
due to failure of one or more steam generating tubes, follow the normal shutdown
procedure. Always check for the possibility of maintaining normal drum level. If it is
not possible to maintain water level, damp or extinguish fires as quickly as possible.
Regardless of the cause of shutdown, allow the steam generating unit sufficient time to
cool down after the fire is out and unit is shut off from the steam line before circulating
cold air or water. Cold air may be admitted to furnace after the pressure has dropped to
zero and after the refractory surfaces have ceased to show any color.

breadcrumb: Actual Photos of various components of manway cover of Steam Drum of Old Co Boiler > SCHEDULE (Monthly, Quarterly & Yearly). Deviations recorded in DSI Reports are to be > 19.2 Checklists to be followed for S/D.

breadcrumb: Actual Photos of various components of manway cover of Steam Drum of Old Co Boiler > SCHEDULE (Monthly, Quarterly & Yearly). Deviations recorded in DSI Reports are to be > 19.2 Checklists to be followed for S/D.
text:
The Check list to be followed during Shut down is mentioned in in Annexure-II which
is attached at the end of Manual.
19.3. Detailed procedure for safe shut down of Boiler
1. Soot blowing should be done at normal load, if possible.
2. Slowly change over from gas to oil firing (if on dual firing).

breadcrumb: Actual Photos of various components of manway cover of Steam Drum of Old Co Boiler > 3 Place boiler on manual control.
text: 3 Place boiler on manual control.

breadcrumb: Actual Photos of various components of manway cover of Steam Drum of Old Co Boiler > 3 Place boiler on manual control.
text:
4. Slowly reduce the load.
5. Shut-down the Burner.
6. Close the oil valve.
7. Scavenge the Burner and pull out the burner, after pilot flame is cut off.
8. Open the startup vent valve and super heater outlet drain valve few threads
immediately after removing oil fire.
9. Close the main stop valve.
10. Keep the FD fan running until the furnace cooled up to 50 to 60 Deg.C
11. This is contracted to air to be allowed after boiler pressure reaches low.
12. Maintain normal water level during cooling period.
13. When the drum pressure reaches 20# open the vent valve.
14. Close the main FO, FG, atomizing steam and FW isolation valve.
15. When the temperature drops below 100 Deg.C operate MBD.

breadcrumb: Actual Photos of various components of manway cover of Steam Drum of Old Co Boiler > 3 Place boiler on manual control. > 19.4 Detailed procedure for safe shut down of PRDS.
text: 19.4 Detailed procedure for safe shut down of PRDS.

breadcrumb: Actual Photos of various components of manway cover of Steam Drum of Old Co Boiler > 3 Place boiler on manual control. > 19.4 Detailed procedure for safe shut down of PRDS.
text:
 Close de-super heater water valve.
 Coordinate with MOI Engineer to shift steam distribution to another PRDS.
 Close PRDS upstream and downstream valves slowly in coordination with MOI.

breadcrumb: Actual Photos of various components of manway cover of Steam Drum of Old Co Boiler > 3 Place boiler on manual control. > 19.5 Detailed procedure for safe shut down of BCW Systems

breadcrumb: Actual Photos of various components of manway cover of Steam Drum of Old Co Boiler > 3 Place boiler on manual control. > 19.5 Detailed procedure for safe shut down of BCW Systems
text:
 Maintain BCW Sump level low to accommodate BCW return
 Stop BCW Pumps after informing to YSF and MOI Engineer and close pump suction
and discharge valves
 Close BCW supply and return lines at Battery limit.
 Stop chemical dosing to BCW Sump
 Stop ID fans.

text: 1 Precaution while shutting down the burner/ Boiler to prevent:

breadcrumb: Actual Photos of various components of manway cover of Steam Drum of Old Co Boiler > 1 Precaution while shutting down the burner/ Boiler to prevent:
text: 1. Plugging of the Oil gun. 2. Damage of the Ignitor due to deposition of heavy oil. 3. Accumulation of water inside furnace. 2. Plugging of the Oil gun : Some times, the Fuel Oil gun is not scavenged immediately after stopping of the burner. The fuel oil inside the burner pipe & tip solidifies. These solidified oil is very difficult to clean / clear the burner holes. The Performance of the burner reduces due to partial cleaning. 3. Inorder to avoid ‘ Plugging of the Oil gun after stopping the burner, the instruction mentioned in the S. No: 5.1 & 5.3 to be followed. 4. Damage of the Ignitor due to deposition of heavy oil: Also, on few occasions, steam scavenging of the Fuel Oil burners is attempted with out the Pilot flame where Automatic Ignition Cycle ON provision is not available during scavenging. This causes the Fuel oil traces to fall on the Ignitor tip and the surrounding, affecting the Ignitor’s performance. 5. Inorder to avoid the Fuel Oil traces deposition on the Boiler Ignitor while scavenging the Fuel Oil burners, the instructions mentioned in S. Nos : 2 & 3 are to be followed 6. Accumulation of water inside furnace: Passing of atomising steam into the furnace may cause accumulation of water due to condensation, inside of the furnace leading to start up delay. Inorder to avoid water accumulation in the furnace through the objectionable water carry

breadcrumb: Actual Photos of various components of manway cover of Steam Drum of Old Co Boiler > 1 Precaution while shutting down the burner/ Boiler to prevent:
text: water inside furnace: Passing of atomising steam into the furnace may cause accumulation of water due to condensation, inside of the furnace leading to start up delay. Inorder to avoid water accumulation in the furnace through the objectionable water carry over in the yard air meant for the peep hole glass of the Boiler/ Soot blowers when the Boiler is shutdown; instructions mentioned in the S. No: 5.4 & 5.5 to be followed.

breadcrumb: Actual Photos of various components of manway cover of Steam Drum of Old Co Boiler > 7 The following precautions to be taken while scavenging the burner
text: 7 The following precautions to be taken while scavenging the burner

breadcrumb: Actual Photos of various components of manway cover of Steam Drum of Old Co Boiler > 7 The following precautions to be taken while scavenging the burner
text:

Fuel Oil burners have to be scavenged immediately after removing the Oil fires
while shutting down the boiler or even while stopping the burner.

If the fuel oil burner is not scavenged immediately after stopping, the fuel oil may
clog the burner pipe and burner tip as well. If the gas burner kept ‘ON’ with out
scavenging oil burner the oil may form coke in the burner tip due to heat from gas
burner.

During the scavenging process, remaining fuel oil traces inside the burner is steam
flushed. The heavy oil (fuel traces) need to be burnt while scavenging or otherwise
it will be deposited on the ignitors/adjacent burners/ tubes. To burn this Fuel Oil
traces, pilot flame or gas flame need to be established during scavenging process.

breadcrumb: Actual Photos of various components of manway cover of Steam Drum of Old Co Boiler > 7 The following precautions to be taken while scavenging the burner > 19.7 WHRS Normal Shutdown Procedure:
text: 19.7 WHRS Normal Shutdown Procedure:

breadcrumb: Actual Photos of various components of manway cover of Steam Drum of Old Co Boiler > 7 The following precautions to be taken while scavenging the burner > 19.7 WHRS Normal Shutdown Procedure:
text:
1. Inform to concerned units FCCU-II & YSF about the shutdown activity and the
duration.
2. Ensure alternative arrangements are lined up, flue gas to be diverted to boiler stack.
3. Slowly isolate the damper towards WHRS and ensure no passing of flue gas.
4. Maintain the steam drum level till the temperature comes down.
5. Inform to FCCU-II / FGD-II to stop water circulation pump.

breadcrumb: Actual Photos of various components of manway cover of Steam Drum of Old Co Boiler > 7 The following precautions to be taken while scavenging the burner > 19.8 WHRS Dry Run Procedure (To be used during Emergency Operation)
text: A. The Following are the standard operating conditions of WHRS 1. Evaporation capacity: 6.1 TPH 2. Maximum working pressure: 12.5 Kg/cm2 (g) 3. Feed water Temperature: 115 Deg. C 4. Steam Drum Outlet Pressure: 10.5 Kg/cm2 (g) 5. Steam Temp. At steam drum outlet: 185 Deg. C 6. Flue gas inlet Temperature (Operating): 320 Deg.C 7. Flue Gas flow (Operating): 87779 Kg/Hr. 8. Flue Gas Outlet Temperature: 200 Deg. C B. General in all condition: The practice of operating a waste heat recovery boiler without circulation through the tubes should be avoided except in emergency situations. The following normal dry running procedures must be adhered to:  Inform respective Management Office regarding the situation.  Read instruction manual section before operating the equipment in a dry condition.  The flue gas outlet temperature must be no higher than the 250/260 Deg. C. Any temperature above 300 Deg. C can be disturbing for boiler, dry run operation in emergency condition also.  The period of dry running must be as short as possible.  Our recommendation is 30 – 40 minutes.  Prior to dry running the tube banks must be cleaned to remove all traces of soot and other accumulations.  Cleaning by soot blowing must continue throughout the dry running period with at least the same frequency as for normal operation.  All internal must be inspected before and after dry run operation for any type of deposit, Sulphur

breadcrumb: Actual Photos of various components of manway cover of Steam Drum of Old Co Boiler > 7 The following precautions to be taken while scavenging the burner > 19.8 WHRS Dry Run Procedure (To be used during Emergency Operation)
text:  Cleaning by soot blowing must continue throughout the dry running period with at least the same frequency as for normal operation.  All internal must be inspected before and after dry run operation for any type of deposit, Sulphur retention of any soot and any loosening abnormality.  The tube bank must be drained and vented.  Insure exhaust temperature should always be lower than inlet temperature.  As good engineering practice, boiler dry run operation is to be undertaken during emergency situation and temperature must be monitored closely to be below 300 Deg. C maximum. In case dry run operation is required frequently this is not good operation practice, should be avoided.

breadcrumb: Actual Photos of various components of manway cover of Steam Drum of Old Co Boiler > 7 The following precautions to be taken while scavenging the burner > C. Dry Run without water Inside of the pressure parts:
text: C. Dry Run without water Inside of the pressure parts:

breadcrumb: Actual Photos of various components of manway cover of Steam Drum of Old Co Boiler > 7 The following precautions to be taken while scavenging the burner > C. Dry Run without water Inside of the pressure parts:
text:
For dry run all water inside the pressure parts should be removed. All vent (steam drum,
Evaporator header, Economizer header and start up vent) valve should be open. By
operating IBD valve we can remove all boiler water from the steam drum. To remove water
from economizer and evaporator coils, open the drain valve of the bottom header of these
coils. Also open the drain valve of down comer so that all boiler water from the pressure
part will be removed.
During dry run flue gas inlet temperature should lesser than operating temperature (300
Deg.C). Soot blower should be operating frequently. Flue gas outlet temperature should not
more than the inlet temperature. Monitoring inlet and outlet flue gas temperature is
important to ensure no sudden rise occurs.
Temperature excursion may indicate the presence of a soot fire. Dry run period should
not more than 30 minutes. Dry run should not be frequently practices otherwise tube and
Power Plant-2 Operating Manual Chapter No:19 Rev. No.2 Page 175 of 251

dry run operation for any type of deposit, Sulphur retention of any soot and any loosening
abnormality.

breadcrumb: Actual Photos of various components of manway cover of Steam Drum of Old Co Boiler > 7 The following precautions to be taken while scavenging the burner > D. Dry Run procedure water inside (Black out) in emergency case:
text: D. Dry Run procedure water inside (Black out) in emergency case:

breadcrumb: Actual Photos of various components of manway cover of Steam Drum of Old Co Boiler > 7 The following precautions to be taken while scavenging the burner > D. Dry Run procedure water inside (Black out) in emergency case:
text:
This condition will be arrived because of suddenly feed water flow cut off, feed water pump
breakdown; feed water pump motor was not working, power supply of motor fails. At this
black out condition, all drains and vents of the boiler should be closed. Monitor temperature
and pressure of the boiler. When pressure of the boiler reaches to the maximum allowable
pressure, crack open the startup vent valve and maintain boiler pressure just below the
maximum allowable pressure. Maintain this situation still another feed water pump takes
in line or power supply resume. In this condition to maintain steam drum level in between
normal to low level.
Total volume of pressure part is around 12 Mtr.3. Design flow of boiler is 6.1 TPH and
operating flow is 5.1 TPH. In this situation boiler can be run maximum 60 / 70 minutes. If
dry run increased more than this, it will be cause of premature failure of the tubes. During
black out, soot blower should be operated frequently to avoid soot deposition on the coils.
RAV should be operated continuously.
All internal must be inspected before and after dry run operation for any type of deposit,
Sulphur retention of any soot and any loosening abnormality.

breadcrumb: Actual Photos of various components of manway cover of Steam Drum of Old Co Boiler > 7 The following precautions to be taken while scavenging the burner > 20.1 Temporary Operations:
text: 20.1 Temporary Operations:

breadcrumb: Actual Photos of various components of manway cover of Steam Drum of Old Co Boiler > 7 The following precautions to be taken while scavenging the burner > 20.1 Temporary Operations:
text:
To improve the Equipment / plant performance, certain temporary operations are to be
carried out periodically. Those are listed below.

breadcrumb: Actual Photos of various components of manway cover of Steam Drum of Old Co Boiler > 7 The following precautions to be taken while scavenging the burner > 20.1 Temporary Operations: > 20.1.1 Preservation FCCU-I & FCCU-II CO Boilers:
text: 20.1.1 Preservation FCCU-I & FCCU-II CO Boilers:

breadcrumb: Actual Photos of various components of manway cover of Steam Drum of Old Co Boiler > 7 The following precautions to be taken while scavenging the burner > 20.1 Temporary Operations: > 20.1.1 Preservation FCCU-I & FCCU-II CO Boilers:
text:
Boiler though healthy, deteriorates due to corrosion if precautions not taken for
preservation when it is idle for a long time.
The principal cause for corrosion is the oxygen ingress in to the Boiler when not in
service. The oxygen corrosion can usually be recognized from pitting, thinning out etc.
The corrosion also can take place outside of drum and tube due to presence of moisture
in air.
The oxygen corrosion results in thinning out of materials isolated pit or crater,
introduction of loose oxides in the piping system and failures resulting from these three
factors.
The boiler requires preservation inside as well outside right from the period of hydraulic
test when water is first introduced into the boiler till boiler comes on line or boiler kept
idle for long period after use.

breadcrumb: Actual Photos of various components of manway cover of Steam Drum of Old Co Boiler > 7 The following precautions to be taken while scavenging the burner > 20.1 Temporary Operations: > The following Procedure to be followed for fire side preservation
text: The following Procedure to be followed for fire side preservation

breadcrumb: Actual Photos of various components of manway cover of Steam Drum of Old Co Boiler > 7 The following precautions to be taken while scavenging the burner > 20.1 Temporary Operations: > The following Procedure to be followed for fire side preservation
text: a. Keep the Silica Gel (200Kgs) for each Boiler ready in powder / bead form. b. Operate all soot blowers several times before shutting down the Boiler, to remove as much deposit and soot as possible. Start the last operating sequence shall start with the soot blower at the fire box side and follow the flue gas path to the blower nearest the stack. c. Open the furnace manhole. d. Clean the furnace floor. e. Blank off all (top and bottom) burners openings from the fire box side before water washing to avoid wetting metal parts of the burner. f. A cover of plastic or other available material should be used to protect the refractory from wash water. g. Firing oil with ash content leads to deposition of some ash on the boiler and super heater surfaces. It is composed of insoluble residue along with a bonding material, which adheres to the boiler and super heater surfaces. This densely bonded mass may not be completely removed by normal cleaning devices as soot blowers. The boiler should be cooled to prevent spalling and stresses in the refractory from the wash water. If the unit is cool enough for workmen to stay inside the fire box then the refractory is cool enough to permit washing. It is preferable that the refractory retains some heat to aid in drying (from inside) at the completion of wash. After the Boiler (fire side) is cooled

breadcrumb: Actual Photos of various components of manway cover of Steam Drum of Old Co Boiler > 7 The following precautions to be taken while scavenging the burner > 20.1 Temporary Operations: > The following Procedure to be followed for fire side preservation
text: inside the fire box then the refractory is cool enough to permit washing. It is preferable that the refractory retains some heat to aid in drying (from inside) at the completion of wash. After the Boiler (fire side) is cooled to 45oC, water wash the Boiler, economiser on the flue gas side (as shown in attached drawing). Carry out the final washing from top to bottom completing it while the Boiler is still warm. h. When washing, efforts should be made to direct the water on the heaviest accumulations. A large quantity of water is to be used so as to flush out the insoluble material and to dilute the acid forming elements in the ash. Manual cleaning to be done to dislodge the more tenacious accumulation on the super heater elements. Care should be taken to avoid damage to the tubes when using rods or bar during this exercise. Power Plant-2 Operating Manual Chapter No:20 Rev. No.2 Page 179 of 251

breadcrumb: Actual Photos of various components of manway cover of Steam Drum of Old Co Boiler > 7 The following precautions to be taken while scavenging the burner > 20.1 Temporary Operations: > The following Procedure to be followed for fire side preservation
text:
i. It is extremely important, once washing is started, to clean the surface down to bare
metal. Otherwise corrosion is encouraged; the deposits which remain accelerate on a
round-the-clock basis until the boiler surfaces are clean.
j. As soon as the boiler is washed, it should be dried out with compressed air
immediately.
k. Before start up, dry out of heater to be carried out.
l. Place silica gel (One drum i.e., 200 kg) in the fire box. Lime trays require raking
once a month and replacement after six months. Silica is placed when it changes
colour.
m. Close all openings, Sealing as necessary.
n. Close the stack damper completely.

breadcrumb: Actual Photos of various components of manway cover of Steam Drum of Old Co Boiler > 7 The following precautions to be taken while scavenging the burner > 20.1 Temporary Operations: > The following Procedure to be followed for tube side preservation
text: The following Procedure to be followed for tube side preservation

breadcrumb: Actual Photos of various components of manway cover of Steam Drum of Old Co Boiler > 7 The following precautions to be taken while scavenging the burner > 20.1 Temporary Operations: > The following Procedure to be followed for tube side preservation
text: a. Refer to the following P&IDs for Nitrogen hose connection at Economiser inlet, super heater outlet. FCCU-I CO Boiler: VR-04-COB-00106 (Attached) FCCU-II CO Boiler: VR-14-F-03-0051(Attached) Nitrogen headers to be charged. b. Positively isolate the Boiler by installing blinds. c. Keep the following drain valves open: i. Economiser bottom Header ii. Steam drum iii. Water drum iv. Super heater inlet v. Super heater outlet d. Keep the vent closed. e. Blow the Boiler with Nitrogen in forward direction: For FCCU-I CO Boiler, connect Nitrogen hose to vent valve (V1) at economizer inlet and purge Nitrogen through drain valve (V2) of pressure gauge at super heater outlet as shown in P&ID. For FCCU-II CO Boiler, connect Nitrogen hose to vent valve (V1) at economizer inlet and purge Nitrogen through drain valve (V2) of pressure gauge at super heater outlet as shown in P&ID. Open Nitrogen inlet valve at economizer (V1&V3) as indicated in P&ID and purge it at the outlet of super heater (V2&V4) as indicated in P&ID, continue Nitrogen blowing in forward direction for 4 hours f. Blow the Boiler with Nitrogen in reverse direction. After 4 hours, close V1 &V3 and disconnect Nitrogen hoses at V1 & V3. Connect Nitrogen hoses to spools at the outlet of super heater (V2&V4 respectively as indicated in P&ID). Open valve at economizer inlet (V1&V3 respectively as indicated in P&ID). Open Nitrogen inlet valve at outlet of super heater (V2) and purge it through

breadcrumb: Actual Photos of various components of manway cover of Steam Drum of Old Co Boiler > 7 The following precautions to be taken while scavenging the burner > 20.1 Temporary Operations: > The following Procedure to be followed for tube side preservation
text: hoses to spools at the outlet of super heater (V2&V4 respectively as indicated in P&ID). Open valve at economizer inlet (V1&V3 respectively as indicated in P&ID). Open Nitrogen inlet valve at outlet of super heater (V2) and purge it through V1 at economizer inlet. Continue Nitrogen blowing from reverse direction for 4 hours. Power Plant-2 Operating Manual Chapter No:20 Rev. No.2 Page 180 of 251

breadcrumb: Actual Photos of various components of manway cover of Steam Drum of Old Co Boiler > 7 The following precautions to be taken while scavenging the burner > 20.1 Temporary Operations: > The following Procedure to be followed for tube side preservation
text: g. Continue the above procedure till moisture in the loop is removed (no visible white fog at vent outlet). h. Isolate Nitrogen valves. i. Close all the five drain valves as mentioned above. j. Crack open the vent valves (V2&V4). k. Start Nitrogen purging through V1&V3. l. Check the oxygen content in the Nitrogen in the steam circuit. If the Oxygen present is less than 0.4%, then close the vent valves. m. Monitor by keeping regular watch on pressure of BFW inlet, economizer inlet and steam drum and shiftily reading should be logged. It should be around 4 to 6kg/cm2. Pressure up with Nitrogen through V1& V3, if the pressure comes down. n. Field observations to be logged during preservation, i.e, Pressure of the steam drum and color of Silica gel. o. Chemicals are to be handled as per the safety procedure given in MSDS (Attached). p. Oxygen checking to be done before giving any entry permit. q. Responsibility of the operations is to implement the procedure and continuous monitoring of steam pressure. r. Periodic monitoring of silica gel color and replacement of thereof by operations/Technical. WHRS Preservation: WHRS Boiler though healthy, deteriorates due to corrosion if precautions not taken for preservation when it is idle for a long time. The principal cause for corrosion is the oxygen ingress in to the Boiler when not in service. The oxygen corrosion can usually be recognized from pitting, thinning out etc. The

breadcrumb: Actual Photos of various components of manway cover of Steam Drum of Old Co Boiler > 7 The following precautions to be taken while scavenging the burner > 20.1 Temporary Operations: > The following Procedure to be followed for tube side preservation
text: for preservation when it is idle for a long time. The principal cause for corrosion is the oxygen ingress in to the Boiler when not in service. The oxygen corrosion can usually be recognized from pitting, thinning out etc. The corrosion also can take place outside of drum and tube due to presence of moisture in air. The oxygen corrosion results in thinning out of materials isolated pit or crater, introduction of loose oxides in the piping system and failures resulting from these three factors. The boiler requires preservation inside as well outside right from the period of hydraulic test when water is first introduced into the boiler till boiler comes on line or boiler kept idle for long period after use.

breadcrumb: Actual Photos of various components of manway cover of Steam Drum of Old Co Boiler > 7 The following precautions to be taken while scavenging the burner > 20.1 Temporary Operations: > 20.1.2 Procedure for Boiler Burner Scanners Bypass
text: 20.1.2 Procedure for Boiler Burner Scanners Bypass

breadcrumb: Actual Photos of various components of manway cover of Steam Drum of Old Co Boiler > 7 The following precautions to be taken while scavenging the burner > 20.1 Temporary Operations: > 20.1.2 Procedure for Boiler Burner Scanners Bypass
text:
Sometimes Boiler burner scanners are to be bypassed for various reasons like poor
sensing, malfunctioning, under maintenance and non-availability of spares. During the
bypass time of scanners, the frequency of manual observation of fuel firing in boiler
furnace is to be increased than the normal observation.
Boiler burner scanner is a safety device used to protect the boiler, operating
personnel and the environment in case of flame failure. Fuel supply into the hot
furnace in absence of flame will lead to furnace explosion. Scanner working
condition is an important factor to be monitored all the time.

breadcrumb: Actual Photos of various components of manway cover of Steam Drum of Old Co Boiler > 7 The following precautions to be taken while scavenging the burner > 20.1 Temporary Operations: > 20.1.2 Procedure for Boiler Burner Scanners Bypass
text: Power Plant-2 Operating Manual Chapter No:20 Rev. No.2 Page 181 of 251

breadcrumb: Actual Photos of various components of manway cover of Steam Drum of Old Co Boiler > 7 The following precautions to be taken while scavenging the burner > 20.1 Temporary Operations: > 20.1.2 Procedure for Boiler Burner Scanners Bypass
text:
For sensing the flame in the furnace, boilers are equipped with flame scanners. Each
fuel (fuel oil & fuel gas) burner is provided with a scanner called “Main flame
scanner”. To ignite the main fuel, an ignitor is required, called pilot burner and is
provided with a scanner called “Pilot flame scanner”. These scanners are to be in
service to safeguard the boiler operation.
The scanners setup of the FCCU-I & II CO Boilers is as follows:
Boiler is equipped with two main flame scanners for top burner (one for oil& one for
gas) and two for bottom burner (one for oil & one for gas). Two pilot flame
scanners, also provided, one for top burner and one for bottom burner.
The various alternate protection plans during the bypass of scanners are listed below.
1. Viewing through peep hole very often. (max interval 30 min)
2. Viewing the stack smoke very often. (max interval 30 min)
3. Watching the fuel oil/ fuel gas burner and header pressures on the DCS screen by
the DCS Incharge. (max interval 30 min)
4. Boiler steam header steady flow and pressure on the DCS screen are the direct
indications that flame is there in the boiler. (max interval 30 min)
5. Periodical cleaning of peep hole is to be ensured to maintain the good visibility
of the flame pattern. (once in a week)

breadcrumb: Actual Photos of various components of manway cover of Steam Drum of Old Co Boiler > 7 The following precautions to be taken while scavenging the burner > 20.1 Temporary Operations: > 20.1.3 Refinery Fuel Gas System Management and Control as applicable to Power
text: Plants The following instruction needs to be followed as a Fuel Gas Consuming Units to contribute for NIL Flaring of Fuel Gas for any reason as it is a direct loss of Fuel and on the other hand; LPG vaporization, if any (for Fuel Gas make up) leads to ‘Fuel quality giveaway’ which tantamount to burning of LPG instead of LSHS . This gives way for the Fuel Gas Controlling unit (normally FCCU2) to better balance the refinery Fuel Gas generation and consumption. 1. During startup of the units, consume minimum quantity of Fuel Gas. Inform to Fuel Gas controlling unit in advance. 2. During normal run, maintain constant consumption of Fuel Gas. In case of any change in Fuel Gas consumption, prior permission from the F.G controlling unit need to be obtained in co-ordination with YSF. 3. Follow the instructions from Fuel Gas controlling unit for increase / decrease in Fuel Gas consumption as dual firing facility is available. Generally FCCU – II is the controlling unit of the Fuel Gas system, unless otherwise instructed through DOB. 4. Check the Fuel Gas Knock out Drum level frequently and drain the liquid carryover if any. In case of any upsets in SRU (if informed by SRU personnel), check it frequently every 15 minutes. Continuously monitor if the liquid carry over is continuous. 5. Make changes in the Fuel Gas consumption when Yard Shift Foreman advices upon requirement of the Fuel

breadcrumb: Actual Photos of various components of manway cover of Steam Drum of Old Co Boiler > 7 The following precautions to be taken while scavenging the burner > 20.1 Temporary Operations: > 20.1.3 Refinery Fuel Gas System Management and Control as applicable to Power
text: in SRU (if informed by SRU personnel), check it frequently every 15 minutes. Continuously monitor if the liquid carry over is continuous. 5. Make changes in the Fuel Gas consumption when Yard Shift Foreman advices upon requirement of the Fuel Gas Controlling Unit to match the total generation & consumption.

breadcrumb: Actual Photos of various components of manway cover of Steam Drum of Old Co Boiler > 7 The following precautions to be taken while scavenging the burner > 20.1 Temporary Operations: > 20.1.4 Procedure for Draining liquid from Fuel Gas knockout drum
text: The following Procedure to be followed while draining liquid from Fuel Gas knockout drum for safely routing liquid to OWS and avoid fuel gas routing to OWS. 1. There is one fuel gas knockout drum at PP-2 to separate liquid from gas incase liquid carryover takes place along with fuel gas due to process upset. 2. The original provision was to route the liquid to FCCU-I fuel gas absorber drum. 3. Consequent to FCCU-I become obsolete, the liquid was being drained to empty containers by connecting a hose to the vessel drain and subsequently the liquid was being transferred to OWS. 4. Since the above practice was not safe, as an alternate, it was advised by technical to route the liquid to nearby OWS through pipe line. The new underground line was connected to OWS near North-East corner of FCCU-II CO Boiler. 5. Fuel gas routing to OWS can cause escaping of gaseous HC to atmosphere, which is unsafe practice. Hence the procedure to be followed to prevent escaping of gas to OWS. 6. The liquid carryover in Fuel gas knock out drum to be observed once in two hours in normal time and more frequently during process units offset and startup. 7. If the liquid level is observed in gauge glass, the liquid to be drained to OWS through pipe line. While draining following procedure to be followed. 8. Ensure no hot work near the fuel gas knockout drum

breadcrumb: Actual Photos of various components of manway cover of Steam Drum of Old Co Boiler > 7 The following precautions to be taken while scavenging the burner > 20.1 Temporary Operations: > 20.1.4 Procedure for Draining liquid from Fuel Gas knockout drum
text: and startup. 7. If the liquid level is observed in gauge glass, the liquid to be drained to OWS through pipe line. While draining following procedure to be followed. 8. Ensure no hot work near the fuel gas knockout drum and near OWS pit north-east corner of FCCU-II CO Boiler. Drain till the liquid is visible in gauge glass. The moment liquid level goes below the visible level in glass close the 1st isolation drain valve. This is done to maintain a liquid level in the bottom and prevent escaping of gas to OWS. 9. After draining the liquid, flush the drain line with service water by connecting hose to the flushing point in between 1st isolation valve and 2nd Isolation valve on drain line. After flushing close the flushing point and disconnect the hose and cap the point. 10. Close the 2nd isolation drain valve after flushing. 11. The area near knockout drum and near OWS to be checked with HC detector for any presence of hydrocarbons.

breadcrumb: Actual Photos of various components of manway cover of Steam Drum of Old Co Boiler > 7 The following precautions to be taken while scavenging the burner > 20.1 Temporary Operations: > 20.1.6 Procedure For Boiler Fuel Oil Burners Gun Cleaning
text:
1. This procedure is developed for the following purposes
a) To avoid incomplete combustion inside furnace due to oil dipping/improper fuel
oil atomization because of oil burner plugging and to overcome boiler
shutdown/plant upset and hazards.
b) To eliminate the chances of potential nearmiss due to accumulation of
combustibles inside furnace causing fire explosion.
c) To ensure environmental friendly operation by reducing emissions through stack
due to incomplete combustion. To improve boiler reliability & performance by
ensuring proper combustion & heat distribution.
2. The following Boilers are having fuel oil/ fuel gas provisions like:
a) FCCU-I(R) New CO Boiler: Four Nos oil/gas firing burners with process
exhaust CO gas intake from FCCU-I(R) unit.
b) FCCU-II Old CO Boiler: Two Nos oil/gas firing burners with process exhaust
CO gas intake from FCCU-II unit, but at present Oil firing is not in use.
3. In case of fuel oil firing there is chance of furnace inside oil dripping/coke formation,
burner plugging/clogging due to some probable cause of improper atomization/ lack
of heat tracing/ presence of foreign particles in fuel oil/ improper air distribution/
insufficient air for combustion etc.
Power Plant-2 Operating Manual Chapter No:20 Rev. No.2 Page 184 of 251

breadcrumb: Actual Photos of various components of manway cover of Steam Drum of Old Co Boiler > 7 The following precautions to be taken while scavenging the burner > 20.1 Temporary Operations: > 20.1.6 Procedure For Boiler Fuel Oil Burners Gun Cleaning
text: 4. As a result which could lead to fire explosion/hazards due to oil accumulation, unplanned boiler shutdown/plant upset due to tube/refractory failure because of uneven firing pattern /flame impingement on furnace wall. 5. Periodical oil gun cleaning is planned as unit level maintenance activity from safety and reliability point of view. 6. Clean each fuel oil burner once in month on DRJ basis or clean it when there is such following observations:  Individual burner fuel oil pressure is holding on higher side even at low fuel oil flow  Fuel oil dripping from burner  Coke formation on fuel oil burners  Flame pattern is not good ie. flickering, short, uneven, impingement even after all adjustments.  Fuel oil/steam leak from burner assembly  Boiler taking for shut down etc. 7. Take DCS shift in charge concurrence before initiation of oil gun dropping activity and & placing back the oil fire in the respective burner so that he can co-ordinate & adjust the loads accordingly. 8. Ensure that gas fire and pilot are there in the particular burner if it’s in dual firing mode or ensure the healthiness of firing and pilot in the other burner if it’s only in single oil firing mode. 9. Remove the oil fire by stopping from BMS panel & close fuel oil block valve, then adjust the firing accordingly in the other burners. 10. Do scavenging the oil gun at least thrice by

breadcrumb: Actual Photos of various components of manway cover of Steam Drum of Old Co Boiler > 7 The following precautions to be taken while scavenging the burner > 20.1 Temporary Operations: > 20.1.6 Procedure For Boiler Fuel Oil Burners Gun Cleaning
text: in single oil firing mode. 9. Remove the oil fire by stopping from BMS panel & close fuel oil block valve, then adjust the firing accordingly in the other burners. 10. Do scavenging the oil gun at least thrice by atomizing steam until the flame disappeared & ensure only dry steam is getting purged into oil gun. Then close atomizing steam block valve after scavenging. 11. Don’t keep oil burner more time inside without steam purging esp. when there is gas firing which will lead to burner tip detoriation due to high heat radiation. 12. Close the individual air resister to the burner if there is no gas fire in the same burner as it will lead to incomplete combustion in the other burner and will cause smoke even after maintaining excess air to furnace & higher O2% at stack outlet. 13. Disconnect the oil hose and atomizing steam hose from oil gun after ensuring no passing from oil & atomizing steam block valves. 14. Remove the oil gun from the burner assembly by using proper PPE ie. Wearing face shield & asbestos gloves. 15. Keep the oil gun outside & purge it again with necessary steam hose arrangement until it’s cleared of all oil traces & then let it gets completely cooled to work on it. 16. Fix the oil gun properly in the bench vice, then open & clean all the gun parts thoroughly with kerosene/ atomizing steam/

breadcrumb: Actual Photos of various components of manway cover of Steam Drum of Old Co Boiler > 7 The following precautions to be taken while scavenging the burner > 20.1 Temporary Operations: > 20.1.6 Procedure For Boiler Fuel Oil Burners Gun Cleaning
text: it’s cleared of all oil traces & then let it gets completely cooled to work on it. 16. Fix the oil gun properly in the bench vice, then open & clean all the gun parts thoroughly with kerosene/ atomizing steam/ plant air. If any parts found damaged then replace with the spare ones. 17. Take utmost care while reassembling the oil gun as wrong threading or over tightening may damage the oil gun parts. 18. Check for any leaks with help of steam, then fix the oil gun in the burner assembly & connect oil/ atomizing steam hoses with copper gaskets/ ‘O’ rings.

traces & again for checking any leaks.
20. Inform DCS shift in charge for lightening up the oil burner as per BMS logic after
normalizing air resister, fuel oil & atomizing steam.

breadcrumb: Actual Photos of various components of manway cover of Steam Drum of Old Co Boiler > 21 Adjust fuel oil & atomizing steam pressure at burner as per burner design datasheet > 20.1.7 Trips/Interlocks Management Procedure
text:
1. All trips and interlocks should always be online.
2. If any particular trip/ interlock is to be bypassed/ forced for any maintenance work,
it should be with the concurrence of unit-in-charge (during day shift of working day)
or Division Head and the required entries in the trip register should be made. In off
day shift hours, YSF/Unit-in-charge/ Division Head of Operations are authorised to
grant permission.
3. Any trip/ interlock not on line for more than 72 hrs. has to be intimated to DGM-
Operations, DGM-Maintenance in the specified format (Annexure-I).
4. Any trip/ interlock not on line for more than 7days, has to be intimated to GM-
Operations in the specified Format (Annexure-II).
5. For all trips/ logistics, Instrument maintenance (in consultation with operation)
should develop and implement suitable preventive maintenance checks at suitable
periodicity.

text: 22.2 Procedure for sample collection:

breadcrumb: Actual Photos of various components of manway cover of Steam Drum of Old Co Boiler > 13 14-PSV-7505 > 22.2 Procedure for sample collection:
text:
Procedure for collecting samples at various locations and the safety involved in
collecting the samples is described below.
(1) Use only plastic bottles while collecting samples for MSL to avoid silica leaching
in glass bottle. Remaining water samples can be collected in Glass bottles.

breadcrumb: Actual Photos of various components of manway cover of Steam Drum of Old Co Boiler > 13 14-PSV-7505 > 22.2 Procedure for sample collection:
text: Power Plant-2 Operating Manual Chapter No:22 Rev. No.2 Page 193 of 251

breadcrumb: Actual Photos of various components of manway cover of Steam Drum of Old Co Boiler > 13 14-PSV-7505 > 22.2 Procedure for sample collection:
text:
(2) Sample should be collected after 5 min of flushing to avoid contamination from
stagnated water in the sample line. Keep the valve opening 1-2 threads to maintain
smooth flow through sample line in such a way that it should not form aeration in
sample bottle to avoid error in sampling and absorption / dissolution of present gases
in collected sample?
(3) Wash the sample bottles thrice and collect the sample up to full level in sample
bottle.
(4) Testing of collected samples is to be carried out at the earliest in the laboratory.
(5) In case of abnormal result, check sample should be sent at the earliest to confirm
abnormal parameters and to avoid its effect on downstream quality.
(6) Place the sample bottle at the designated location (samples work bench). Insist
sample boy to keep the empty bottles on the same bench aside.
Safety involved in sample collection:
CBD & MSL sample is very hot, they need to cool completely in cooler then only
samples to be cooled.
pH TDS M-Alk Ca hardness
8.5 - 8.8 <800 <300 <170

breadcrumb: Actual Photos of various components of manway cover of Steam Drum of Old Co Boiler > 23.0 List of Plant Chemicals: > 23.4 Procedure for Process Chemical Usage in PP-2: > Notes: PAD section of Technical department monitors regularly & advises increase/
text:
Chemical treatment in Boilers is used to maintain Steam quality and to protect steam
generation equipment from corrosion and deposits.
Chemical treatment in bearing cooling water (BCW) system is done to prevent bio-
organism growth , scale formation and corrosion.

text:
It is necessary that each plant personnel is familiar with the nature of the material in the
process area, aware of the hazard to his own and to other people's health, the fire and
explosion danger, and should know the protective measures to be taken

breadcrumb: Actual Photos of various components of manway cover of Steam Drum of Old Co Boiler > 24.0 Intoduction: > 24.1 Chemicals Hazards in the plant:
text: 24.1 Chemicals Hazards in the plant:

breadcrumb: Actual Photos of various components of manway cover of Steam Drum of Old Co Boiler > 24.0 Intoduction: > 24.1 Chemicals Hazards in the plant:
text:
The following are the Hazards associated with the chemicals are due to chemical ingestion
or contamination:
Skin : Irritation with corrosive destruction at site of contact. Pain can be delayed up to
three or more minutes after exposure, but tissue destruction begins immediately on
contact.
Eye: Severe tissue destruction, corneal pacification, and possible loss of vision.
Lung: Causes small burns in the respiratory tract with corrosive destruction to the
mucosa, loss of hair in the nasal passages, and possible delayed pulmonary oedema (fluid
in the lungs), which can be fatal.
Ingestion: results in serious, life-threatening destruction of tissues along the digestive
tract. Death can occur within days as a result of shock, asphyxia (due to blocked airways
caused by the swelling of the oesophagus and associated tissues), or pneumonia.

As the chemical to be handled frequently in the plant, There is possibility of chemical
spillage on the operating personnel. Standard procedures developed for chemical handling
to be followed for chemical handling mentioned in chapter-23.

text:
The following are the first aid measures in various cases of hazards
Sl Hazard/ Injury First Aid Procedure
1. Chemical Eye Check for and remove any contact lenses. In case of contact,
Contact immediately flush eyes with plenty of water for at least 15
minutes. Cold water may be used. Get medical attention
immediately
2. Chemical Skin In case of contact, immediately flush skin with plenty of water
Contact for at least 15 minutes while removing contaminated clothing
and shoes. Cover the irritated skin with an emollient. Cold
water may be used. Wash clothing before reuse. Thoroughly
clean shoes before reuse. Get medical attention immediately
In case of Serious Skin Contact:
Wash with a disinfectant soap and cover the contaminated skin
with an anti-bacterial cream. Seek immediate medical
attention.
3. Chemical If inhaled, remove to fresh air. If not breathing, give artificial
Inhalation respiration. If breathing is difficult, give oxygen. Get medical
attention immediately.
In case of Chemical Serious Inhalation:
Evacuate the victim to a safe area as soon as possible. Loosen
tight clothing such as a collar, tie, belt or waistband. If
breathing is difficult, administer oxygen. If the victim is not

text: Sl Hazard/ Injury First Aid Procedure breathing, perform mouth-to-mouth resuscitation. WARNING: It may be hazardous to the person providing aid to give mouth-to-mouth resuscitation when the inhaled material is toxic, infectious or corrosive. Seek immediate medical attention 4. Chemical If swallowed, do not induce vomiting unless directed to do so Ingestion by medical personnel. Never give anything by mouth to an uFCCU-1 COnscious person. Loosen tight clothing such as a collar, tie, belt or waistband. Get medical attention immediately. 5. Chemical In case of burns, the following first aid has to be provided: Burns:  Remove patient from accident site  Wash injury with tepid water liberally  Identify chemical for effective therapy  Seek medical treatment  IV fluids need to be administered  Pain medications and antibiotics needed  Wounds cleaned and bandaged  Follow -up care compulsory  Consultation with specialist is a must 6. Chemical In case of Chemical splash in the eye, the following first aid Splash in the has to be provided: Eye  Lay the person on the floor  Keep eye lids open forcibly  Use clean cold water to wash eyes gently  Keep washing steadily for at least 20 minutes  Rinse/wash hands thoroughly to remove chemical  If wearing contact lens remove them  Do not rub eyes  Do not use eye drops until told  Wear sunglasses to minimize irritation  After these basic steps seek medical

text:  Rinse/wash hands thoroughly to remove chemical  If wearing contact lens remove them  Do not rub eyes  Do not use eye drops until told  Wear sunglasses to minimize irritation  After these basic steps seek medical help  Remember the name of chemical to tell doctor 7. Dislocation: In case of dislocation, the following first aid has to be provided:  Call medical help as soon as possible  Do not move the joint or try to place it back  Place ice to control swelling  If skin is cut, clean gently and bandage with sterile gauze  Sling or splint the injury in its original position  If injury is serious, check for breathing  If not breathing, provide Cardio pulmonary resuscitation (CPR). Elevate the feet up to 12 inches  Cover the patient with a blanket. 8. No.2 Page 208 of 251

breadcrumb: Actual Photos of various components of manway cover of Steam Drum of Old Co Boiler > 24.0 Intoduction: > 24.3 First Aid Facilities: > 24.3.1 First aid procedures:
text: Sl Hazard/ Injury First Aid Procedure  First step is to switch off the current source  Otherwise, move the source using a wooden stick  Attend to the victim  Check for breathing  No breathing, do Cardio pulmonary resuscitation (CPR)  Call emergency medical aid  If breathing, do a physical examination  Treat for minor burns  Re-establish vital functions  Excessive burns may require hospitalization/ surgery  Supportive care must be provided 9. Fracture There are two types of fractures.  Open fracture: Skin breaks causing open wound  Closed fracture: Skin not broken In case of both the fractures, the first aid differs and is followed as discussed below: 10. Sprain In case of sprain, the following first aid has to be provided:  Apply a Compress to injured area for 20 min  This may be done 4-8 times a day  Use a plastic bag with crushed ice, wrapped in a towel  Use compression bandages to reduce swelling  Keep the injured leg elevated on a pillow  Take anti-inflammatory pills if necessary  Take rest for the recommended period  When pain/swelling is diminished, do recommended exercises 11. Heart Attack In case of a heart attack, the following first aid has to be provided:  Try to relax  Loosen tight clothes  Take medicines if any  Pain subsides within 3 min of medicine intake  If not, see a

breadcrumb: Actual Photos of various components of manway cover of Steam Drum of Old Co Boiler > 24.0 Intoduction: > 24.3 First Aid Facilities: > 24.3.1 First aid procedures:
text: case of a heart attack, the following first aid has to be provided:  Try to relax  Loosen tight clothes  Take medicines if any  Pain subsides within 3 min of medicine intake  If not, see a doctor.  Give artificial respiration if required  Give Cardiopulmonary Resuscitation (CPR): a) If no pulse is detected b) By placing palm on chest to pump  15 pumps are followed by 2 artificial respiration Continue till ambulance / doctor arrives 12. Heat Stroke In case of a heat stroke, the following first aid has to be provided:  Remove the person to a shady place  Cool the person by sponging with wet towel  Apply ice packs in armpits and groin  Water with electrolyte, fruit / vegetable juice should be given  Victim must be rested.

breadcrumb: Actual Photos of various components of manway cover of Steam Drum of Old Co Boiler > 24.0 Intoduction: > 24.3 First Aid Facilities: > 24.3.1 First aid procedures:
text: Sl Hazard/ Injury First Aid Procedure 13. Severe Bleeding In case of severe bleeding, the following first aid has to be provided:  Wash hands well before administering to patient  Wear synthetic gloves  Make the victim lie down  Slightly elevate the legs  If possible keep the affected area elevated  Remove any obvious debris/particle  Apply direct pressure using clean cloth/bandage  Use hand if cloth is not available  Apply pressure continuously for at least 20 minutes  Do not remove the cloth to check the bleeding  Hold the bandage in place using an adhesive tape  If bleeding seeps through bandage, do not remove it  Add extra bandage on top of the first one  Apply direct pressure on the artery if necessary  The pressure points for arm--below arm- pit/above elbow  For leg--behind knee/near groin  Squeeze the artery keeping finger flat  Continue applying pressure on the wound  Once bleeding stops immobilize the affected part  See a doctor 14. Spinal Cord In case of spinal cord injury, seek medical help or call for an Injury ambulance as soon as possible. Meanwhile the following first aid has to be provided -  Move the person, if surrounding is not safe  Immobilize the head, neck and body on both sides  Movements may dislocate vertebra and cause further injury  If there is no sign of breathing, perform

text: be provided -  Move the person, if surrounding is not safe  Immobilize the head, neck and body on both sides  Movements may dislocate vertebra and cause further injury  If there is no sign of breathing, perform CPR  Do not tilt head backward during CPR.
